{{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesuke|ja|е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ}} (е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ[[Category:dd]][[ja:е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ]]) [[Category:Https://ko.wikipedia.org/wiki/]][[ko:%EC%87%BC%EB%8B%88 %EC%93%B0%EB%84%A4%EC%8A%A4%EC%BC%80]] {{Infobox samurai | name = ShЕҚni Tsunesuke (е°‘ејҗ зөҢиіҮ) | image = | imagesize = | caption = | era = Mid-[[Kamakura period]] | birth_date = [[Kanki]] 1 (1229) вҖ» | death_date = [[ShЕҚЕҚ]] 5, [[8th month]], [[2nd day (lunar calendar)|2nd day]] (September 14, 1292) вҖ» | posthumous_name = JЕҚe (жө„жҒө; Dharma name)гҖҺе°ҠеҚ‘еҲҶи„үгҖҸз¬¬2зҜҮгғ»P.391гҖӮ | alias = Muto Tsunesuke | rank = | allegiance = [[Kamakura shogunate]] | lord = [[Prince Koreyasu]] | clan = {{ill|ShЕҚni clan|ja|е°‘ејҗж°Ҹ}} | parents = Father: {{ill|ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮиғҪ}} | siblings = Tsunesuke, {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ}}жңҚйғЁгҖҒ2014е№ҙгҖҒP.416гҖӮ, {{ill|ShЕҚni Morisue|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣиіҮ}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Munetsune|ja|е°‘ејҗе®—иіҮ}} (existence debated)гҖҺжұҺжө·е°ҸйҢІгҖҸгҒ«гҖҢеӨӘе®°и—ӨеҺҹе°‘еҚҝејҹе®—иіҮгҖҚгҒЁиЁҳгҒ•гӮҢгӮӢгҒҢгҖҒж—Ҙжң¬еҒҙеҸІж–ҷгҒ«гҒҜи©ІеҪ“иҖ…гҒҢгҒҠгӮүгҒҡгҖҒи«ёиӘ¬гҒӮгӮӢгҖӮе·қи¶Ҡжі°еҚҡгҖҢжұҺжө·е°ҸйҢІгҒ®ејҳе®үгҒ®еҪ№иЁҳдәӢгҒ«гҒӨгҒ„гҒҰгҖҚгҖҺи»ҚдәӢеҸІеӯҰгҖҸз¬¬11е·»з¬¬1еҸ·гҖҒ1975е№ҙ6жңҲгҖҒ26-34й ҒгҖӮ | spouse = | children = {{ill|ShЕҚni Moritsune|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣзөҢ}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷӮзөҢ}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Morishige|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣж°Ҹ}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Sadatsune|ja|е°‘ејҗиІһиіҮ}} | notable = вҖ» Birth and death dates are debated. }} '''ShЕҚni Tsunesuke''' (е°‘ејҗ зөҢиіҮ) was a {{ill|samurai}} and retainer of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate}} during the mid-{{ill|Kamakura period}}. He was the son of {{ill|ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮиғҪ}} and the third head of the {{ill|ShЕҚni clan|ja|е°‘ејҗж°Ҹ}}. == Overview == The character "зөҢ" in Tsunesuke's name was likely granted as a favor by the fourth {{ill|Shikken|ja|еҹ·жЁ©}}, {{ill|HЕҚjЕҚ Tokiyori|ja|еҢ—жқЎзөҢжҷӮ}}, or by {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoritsune|ja|и—ӨеҺҹй јзөҢ}}, the fourth {{ill|Seii Taishogun|ja|еҫҒеӨ·еӨ§е°Ҷи»Қ}}, who gave the same character to Tokiyori. This suggests Tsunesuke underwent his coming-of-age ceremony (genpuku) between 1245 and 1246. There are varying theories regarding Tsunesuke's birth and death years. One theory suggests he was born in 1229 and died in 1292, while another suggests 1226 to 1289.[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B0%91%E5%BC%90%E7%B5%8C%E8%B3%87-1082512 гӮігғҲгғҗгғігӮҜ_е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ] However, as genpuku typically occurred in oneвҖҷs teens, the 1229 birth year is widely considered accurate. Tsunesuke shared responsibilities with his father in governing northern Kyushu and played a crucial role during the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|е…ғеҜҮ}}. He oversaw the fortification of defenses, such as the {{ill|Ishizumi walls|ja|е…ғеҜҮйҳІеЎҒ}}, and coordinated efforts against the Yuan forces. His earliest documented activity appears in a record from 1269.жңҚйғЁгҖҒ2014е№ҙгҖҒP.416гҖӮе…ёжӢ гҒҜгҖҺжӯҰйӣ„зҘһзӨҫж–ҮжӣёгҖҸпјҲгҖҺйҺҢеҖүйҒәж–ҮгҖҸз¬¬14е·»гғ»10503еҸ·ж–ҮжӣёпјүгҖӮ In 1274, during the {{ill|Battle of Bun'ei|ja|ж–Үж°ёгҒ®еҪ№}}, Tsunesuke's brother {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ}} led the Japanese forces. Tsunesuke's role during this campaign is unclear. By 1275, he inherited the familyвҖҷs headship from his father. In 1281, during the {{ill|Battle of KЕҚan|ja|ејҳе®үгҒ®еҪ№}}, Tsunesuke fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki|ja|еЈұеІҗеі¶гҒ®жҲҰгҒ„}}, where he expelled the Yuan forces but lost his son, {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ}}.
[[File:Mooko-SamuraiShips.jpg|300px|right|thumb|'''ShЕҚni Tsunesuke''' and other samurai ships during the Mongol invasion.
{{small|гҖҺ{{ill|MЕҚko ShЕ«rai Ekotoba|ja|и’ҷеҸӨиҘІжқҘзөөи©һ}}гҖҸеҫҢе·»гғ»зөө15гғ»з¬¬19зҙҷ}} Despite this tragedy, he continued to serve as a {{ill|Chinzei BugyЕҚ|ja|йҺ®иҘҝеҘүиЎҢ}} with {{ill|ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|ja|еӨ§еҸӢй јжі°}}. In 1284, following the death of the eighth {{ill|Shikken|ja|еҹ·жЁ©}}, {{ill|HЕҚjЕҚ Tokimune|ja|еҢ—жқЎжҷӮе®—}}, Tsunesuke took Buddhist vows and adopted the Dharma name JЕҚe. During the {{ill|Shimozuki Disturbance|ja|йңңжңҲйЁ’еӢ•}} of 1285, he supported {{ill|Taira no Yoritsuna|ja|е№ій јз¶ұ}} and defeated his brother {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ}}, aligning with the ruling {{ill|Tokuso family|ja|еҫ—е®—е®¶}}. This led to a weakening of the ShЕҚni clanвҖҷs influence, as they lost control of several provinces. In 1286, Tsunesuke was appointed to the {{ill|Chinzei Dangi-sho|ja|йҺ®иҘҝи«Үиӯ°жүҖ}} alongside {{ill|ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|ja|еӨ§еҸӢй јжі°}}, {{ill|Utsunomiya Michifusa|ja|е®ҮйғҪе®®йҖҡжҲҝ}}, and {{ill|Shibuya Shigekuni|ja|жёӢи°·йҮҚйғ·}}. He passed away in 1292. His eldest son {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ}} having died in battle, Tsunesuke was succeeded by {{ill|ShЕҚni Moritsune|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣзөҢ}}. His younger sons {{ill|ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷӮзөҢ}} and {{ill|ShЕҚni Morishige|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣж°Ҹ}} later founded the {{ill|Shiga clan|ja|еҝ—иіҖж°Ҹ}} and {{ill|Hirai clan|ja|е№ідә•ж°Ҹ}}, respectively. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *[[Motohisa Yasuda]], ed. гҖҺйҺҢеҖүгғ»е®Өз”әдәәеҗҚдәӢе…ё гӮігғігғ‘гӮҜгғҲзүҲгҖҸпјҲ[[Shin Jinbutsu Oraisha]], 1990пјү *[[Hattori Hideo]] гҖҺи’ҷеҸӨиҘІжқҘгҖҸпјҲ[[Yamakawa Publishing]], 2014пјү *[[Kuroita Katsumi]], ed. гҖҺж–°иЁӮеў—иЈңеӣҪеҸІеӨ§зі»гғ»е°ҠеҚ‘еҲҶи„ү з¬¬2зҜҮгҖҸпјҲ[[Yoshikawa KЕҚbunkan]]пјү {{ShЕҚni clan heads}} [[Category:ShЕҚni clan]] [[Category:Kamakura shogunate retainers]] [[Category:People of the Mongol invasions of Japan]] [[Category:1229 births]] [[Category:1292 deaths]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:гғ•гӮЎгӮӨгғ«]] [[ja:е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ]]

revision.text:
{{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesuke|ja|е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ}} (е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ[[ja:е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ]]) [[Category:Https://ko.wikipedia.org/wiki/]][[ko:%EC%87%BC%EB%8B%88 %EC%93%B0%EB%84%A4%EC%8A%A4%EC%BC%80]] {{Infobox samurai | name = ShЕҚni Tsunesuke (е°‘ејҗ зөҢиіҮ) | image = | imagesize = | caption = | era = Mid-[[Kamakura period]] | birth_date = [[Kanki]] 1 (1229) вҖ» | death_date = [[ShЕҚЕҚ]] 5, [[8th month]], [[2nd day (lunar calendar)|2nd day]] (September 14, 1292) вҖ» | posthumous_name = JЕҚe (жө„жҒө; Dharma name)гҖҺе°ҠеҚ‘еҲҶи„үгҖҸз¬¬2зҜҮгғ»P.391гҖӮ | alias = Muto Tsunesuke | rank = | allegiance = [[Kamakura shogunate]] | lord = [[Prince Koreyasu]] | clan = {{ill|ShЕҚni clan|ja|е°‘ејҗж°Ҹ}} | parents = Father: {{ill|ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮиғҪ}} | siblings = Tsunesuke, {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ}}жңҚйғЁгҖҒ2014е№ҙгҖҒP.416гҖӮ, {{ill|ShЕҚni Morisue|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣиіҮ}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Munetsune|ja|е°‘ејҗе®—иіҮ}} (existence debated)гҖҺжұҺжө·е°ҸйҢІгҖҸгҒ«гҖҢеӨӘе®°и—ӨеҺҹе°‘еҚҝејҹе®—иіҮгҖҚгҒЁиЁҳгҒ•гӮҢгӮӢгҒҢгҖҒж—Ҙжң¬еҒҙеҸІж–ҷгҒ«гҒҜи©ІеҪ“иҖ…гҒҢгҒҠгӮүгҒҡгҖҒи«ёиӘ¬гҒӮгӮӢгҖӮе·қи¶Ҡжі°еҚҡгҖҢжұҺжө·е°ҸйҢІгҒ®ејҳе®үгҒ®еҪ№иЁҳдәӢгҒ«гҒӨгҒ„гҒҰгҖҚгҖҺи»ҚдәӢеҸІеӯҰгҖҸз¬¬11е·»з¬¬1еҸ·гҖҒ1975е№ҙ6жңҲгҖҒ26-34й ҒгҖӮ | spouse = | children = {{ill|ShЕҚni Moritsune|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣзөҢ}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷӮзөҢ}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Morishige|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣж°Ҹ}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Sadatsune|ja|е°‘ејҗиІһиіҮ}} | notable = вҖ» Birth and death dates are debated. }} '''ShЕҚni Tsunesuke''' (е°‘ејҗ зөҢиіҮ) was a {{ill|samurai}} and retainer of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate}} during the mid-{{ill|Kamakura period}}. He was the son of {{ill|ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮиғҪ}} and the third head of the {{ill|ShЕҚni clan|ja|е°‘ејҗж°Ҹ}}. == Overview == The character "зөҢ" in Tsunesuke's name was likely granted as a favor by the fourth {{ill|Shikken|ja|еҹ·жЁ©}}, {{ill|HЕҚjЕҚ Tokiyori|ja|еҢ—жқЎзөҢжҷӮ}}, or by {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoritsune|ja|и—ӨеҺҹй јзөҢ}}, the fourth {{ill|Seii Taishogun|ja|еҫҒеӨ·еӨ§е°Ҷи»Қ}}, who gave the same character to Tokiyori. This suggests Tsunesuke underwent his coming-of-age ceremony (genpuku) between 1245 and 1246. There are varying theories regarding Tsunesuke's birth and death years. One theory suggests he was born in 1229 and died in 1292, while another suggests 1226 to 1289.[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B0%91%E5%BC%90%E7%B5%8C%E8%B3%87-1082512 гӮігғҲгғҗгғігӮҜ_е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ] However, as genpuku typically occurred in oneвҖҷs teens, the 1229 birth year is widely considered accurate. Tsunesuke shared responsibilities with his father in governing northern Kyushu and played a crucial role during the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|е…ғеҜҮ}}. He oversaw the fortification of defenses, such as the {{ill|Ishizumi walls|ja|е…ғеҜҮйҳІеЎҒ}}, and coordinated efforts against the Yuan forces. His earliest documented activity appears in a record from 1269.жңҚйғЁгҖҒ2014е№ҙгҖҒP.416гҖӮе…ёжӢ гҒҜгҖҺжӯҰйӣ„зҘһзӨҫж–ҮжӣёгҖҸпјҲгҖҺйҺҢеҖүйҒәж–ҮгҖҸз¬¬14е·»гғ»10503еҸ·ж–ҮжӣёпјүгҖӮ In 1274, during the {{ill|Battle of Bun'ei|ja|ж–Үж°ёгҒ®еҪ№}}, Tsunesuke's brother {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ}} led the Japanese forces. Tsunesuke's role during this campaign is unclear. By 1275, he inherited the familyвҖҷs headship from his father. In 1281, during the {{ill|Battle of KЕҚan|ja|ејҳе®үгҒ®еҪ№}}, Tsunesuke fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki|ja|еЈұеІҗеі¶гҒ®жҲҰгҒ„}}, where he expelled the Yuan forces but lost his son, {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ}}.
[[File:Mooko-SamuraiShips.jpg|300px|right|thumb|'''ShЕҚni Tsunesuke''' and other samurai ships during the Mongol invasion.
{{small|гҖҺ{{ill|MЕҚko ShЕ«rai Ekotoba|ja|и’ҷеҸӨиҘІжқҘзөөи©һ}}гҖҸеҫҢе·»гғ»зөө15гғ»з¬¬19зҙҷ}} Despite this tragedy, he continued to serve as a {{ill|Chinzei BugyЕҚ|ja|йҺ®иҘҝеҘүиЎҢ}} with {{ill|ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|ja|еӨ§еҸӢй јжі°}}. In 1284, following the death of the eighth {{ill|Shikken|ja|еҹ·жЁ©}}, {{ill|HЕҚjЕҚ Tokimune|ja|еҢ—жқЎжҷӮе®—}}, Tsunesuke took Buddhist vows and adopted the Dharma name JЕҚe. During the {{ill|Shimozuki Disturbance|ja|йңңжңҲйЁ’еӢ•}} of 1285, he supported {{ill|Taira no Yoritsuna|ja|е№ій јз¶ұ}} and defeated his brother {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ}}, aligning with the ruling {{ill|Tokuso family|ja|еҫ—е®—е®¶}}. This led to a weakening of the ShЕҚni clanвҖҷs influence, as they lost control of several provinces. In 1286, Tsunesuke was appointed to the {{ill|Chinzei Dangi-sho|ja|йҺ®иҘҝи«Үиӯ°жүҖ}} alongside {{ill|ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|ja|еӨ§еҸӢй јжі°}}, {{ill|Utsunomiya Michifusa|ja|е®ҮйғҪе®®йҖҡжҲҝ}}, and {{ill|Shibuya Shigekuni|ja|жёӢи°·йҮҚйғ·}}. He passed away in 1292. His eldest son {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ}} having died in battle, Tsunesuke was succeeded by {{ill|ShЕҚni Moritsune|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣзөҢ}}. His younger sons {{ill|ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷӮзөҢ}} and {{ill|ShЕҚni Morishige|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣж°Ҹ}} later founded the {{ill|Shiga clan|ja|еҝ—иіҖж°Ҹ}} and {{ill|Hirai clan|ja|е№ідә•ж°Ҹ}}, respectively. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *[[Motohisa Yasuda]], ed. гҖҺйҺҢеҖүгғ»е®Өз”әдәәеҗҚдәӢе…ё гӮігғігғ‘гӮҜгғҲзүҲгҖҸпјҲ[[Shin Jinbutsu Oraisha]], 1990пјү *[[Hattori Hideo]] гҖҺи’ҷеҸӨиҘІжқҘгҖҸпјҲ[[Yamakawa Publishing]], 2014пјү *[[Kuroita Katsumi]], ed. гҖҺж–°иЁӮеў—иЈңеӣҪеҸІеӨ§зі»гғ»е°ҠеҚ‘еҲҶи„ү з¬¬2зҜҮгҖҸпјҲ[[Yoshikawa KЕҚbunkan]]пјү {{ShЕҚni clan heads}} [[Category:ShЕҚni clan]] [[Category:Kamakura shogunate retainers]] [[Category:People of the Mongol invasions of Japan]] [[Category:1229 births]] [[Category:1292 deaths]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:гғ•гӮЎгӮӨгғ«]] [[ja:е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ]]

He was the son of {{ill|ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮиғҪ}} and the third head of the {{ill|ShЕҚni clan|ja|е°‘ејҗж°Ҹ}}. == Overview == The character "зөҢ" in Tsunesuke's name was likely granted as a favor by the fourth {{ill|Shikken|ja|еҹ·жЁ©}}, {{ill|HЕҚjЕҚ Tokiyori|ja|еҢ—жқЎзөҢжҷӮ}}, or by {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoritsune|ja|и—ӨеҺҹй јзөҢ}}, the fourth {{ill|Seii Taishogun|ja|еҫҒеӨ·еӨ§е°Ҷи»Қ}}, who gave the same character to Tokiyori. This suggests Tsunesuke underwent his coming-of-age ceremony (genpuku) between 1245 and 1246. There are varying theories regarding Tsunesuke's birth and death years. One theory suggests he was born in 1229 and died in 1292, while another suggests 1226 to 1289.[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B0%91%E5%BC%90%E7%B5%8C%E8%B3%87-1082512 гӮігғҲгғҗгғігӮҜ_е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ] However, as genpuku typically occurred in oneвҖҷs teens, the 1229 birth year is widely considered accurate. Tsunesuke shared responsibilities with his father in governing northern Kyushu and played a crucial role during the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|е…ғеҜҮ}}. He oversaw the fortification of defenses, such as the {{ill|Ishizumi walls|ja|е…ғеҜҮйҳІеЎҒ}}, and coordinated efforts against the Yuan forces. His earliest documented activity appears in a record from 1269.жңҚйғЁгҖҒ2014е№ҙгҖҒP.416гҖӮе…ёжӢ гҒҜгҖҺжӯҰйӣ„зҘһзӨҫж–ҮжӣёгҖҸпјҲгҖҺйҺҢеҖүйҒәж–ҮгҖҸз¬¬14е·»гғ»10503еҸ·ж–ҮжӣёпјүгҖӮ In 1274, during the {{ill|Battle of Bun'ei|ja|ж–Үж°ёгҒ®еҪ№}}, Tsunesuke's brother {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ}} led the Japanese forces. Tsunesuke's role during this campaign is unclear. By 1275, he inherited the familyвҖҷs headship from his father. In 1281, during the {{ill|Battle of KЕҚan|ja|ејҳе®үгҒ®еҪ№}}, Tsunesuke fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki|ja|еЈұеІҗеі¶гҒ®жҲҰгҒ„}}, where he expelled the Yuan forces but lost his son, {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ}}.
[[File:Mooko-SamuraiShips.jpg|300px|right|thumb|'''ShЕҚni Tsunesuke''' and other samurai ships during the Mongol invasion.
{{small|гҖҺ{{ill|MЕҚko ShЕ«rai Ekotoba|ja|и’ҷеҸӨиҘІжқҘзөөи©һ}}гҖҸеҫҢе·»гғ»зөө15гғ»з¬¬19зҙҷ}} Despite this tragedy, he continued to serve as a {{ill|Chinzei BugyЕҚ|ja|йҺ®иҘҝеҘүиЎҢ}} with {{ill|ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|ja|еӨ§еҸӢй јжі°}}. In 1284, following the death of the eighth {{ill|Shikken|ja|еҹ·жЁ©}}, {{ill|HЕҚjЕҚ Tokimune|ja|еҢ—жқЎжҷӮе®—}}, Tsunesuke took Buddhist vows and adopted the Dharma name JЕҚe. During the {{ill|Shimozuki Disturbance|ja|йңңжңҲйЁ’еӢ•}} of 1285, he supported {{ill|Taira no Yoritsuna|ja|е№ій јз¶ұ}} and defeated his brother {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ}}, aligning with the ruling {{ill|Tokuso family|ja|еҫ—е®—е®¶}}. This led to a weakening of the ShЕҚni clanвҖҷs influence, as they lost control of several provinces. In 1286, Tsunesuke was appointed to the {{ill|Chinzei Dangi-sho|ja|йҺ®иҘҝи«Үиӯ°жүҖ}} alongside {{ill|ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|ja|еӨ§еҸӢй јжі°}}, {{ill|Utsunomiya Michifusa|ja|е®ҮйғҪе®®йҖҡжҲҝ}}, and {{ill|Shibuya Shigekuni|ja|жёӢи°·йҮҚйғ·}}. He passed away in 1292. His eldest son {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ}} having died in battle, Tsunesuke was succeeded by {{ill|ShЕҚni Moritsune|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣзөҢ}}. His younger sons {{ill|ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷӮзөҢ}} and {{ill|ShЕҚni Morishige|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣж°Ҹ}} later founded the {{ill|Shiga clan|ja|еҝ—иіҖж°Ҹ}} and {{ill|Hirai clan|ja|е№ідә•ж°Ҹ}}, respectively. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *[[Motohisa Yasuda]], ed. гҖҺйҺҢеҖүгғ»е®Өз”әдәәеҗҚдәӢе…ё гӮігғігғ‘гӮҜгғҲзүҲгҖҸпјҲ[[Shin Jinbutsu Oraisha]], 1990пјү *[[Hattori Hideo]] гҖҺи’ҷеҸӨиҘІжқҘгҖҸпјҲ[[Yamakawa Publishing]], 2014пјү *[[Kuroita Katsumi]], ed. гҖҺж–°иЁӮеў—иЈңеӣҪеҸІеӨ§зі»гғ»е°ҠеҚ‘еҲҶи„ү з¬¬2зҜҮгҖҸпјҲ[[Yoshikawa KЕҚbunkan]]пјү {{ShЕҚni clan heads}} [[Category:ShЕҚni clan]] [[Category:Kamakura shogunate retainers]] [[Category:People of the Mongol invasions of Japan]] [[Category:1229 births]] [[Category:1292 deaths]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:гғ•гӮЎгӮӨгғ«]] [[ja:е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ]]

{{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesuke|ja|е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ}} (е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ[[ja:е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ]]) [[Category:Https://ko.wikipedia.org/wiki/]][[ko:%EC%87%BC%EB%8B%88 %EC%93%B0%EB%84%A4%EC%8A%A4%EC%BC%80]] {{Infobox samurai | name = ShЕҚni Tsunesuke (е°‘ејҗ зөҢиіҮ) | image = | imagesize = | caption = | era = Mid-[[Kamakura period]] | birth_date = [[Kanki]] 1 (1229) вҖ» | death_date = [[ShЕҚЕҚ]] 5, [[8th month]], [[2nd day (lunar calendar)|2nd day]] (September 14, 1292) вҖ» | posthumous_name = JЕҚe (жө„жҒө; Dharma name)гҖҺе°ҠеҚ‘еҲҶи„үгҖҸз¬¬2зҜҮгғ»P.391гҖӮ | alias = Muto Tsunesuke | rank = | allegiance = [[Kamakura shogunate]] | lord = [[Prince Koreyasu]] | clan = {{ill|ShЕҚni clan|ja|е°‘ејҗж°Ҹ}} | parents = Father: {{ill|ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮиғҪ}} | siblings = Tsunesuke, {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ}}жңҚйғЁгҖҒ2014е№ҙгҖҒP.416гҖӮ, {{ill|ShЕҚni Morisue|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣиіҮ}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Munetsune|ja|е°‘ејҗе®—иіҮ}} (existence debated)гҖҺжұҺжө·е°ҸйҢІгҖҸгҒ«гҖҢеӨӘе®°и—ӨеҺҹе°‘еҚҝејҹе®—иіҮгҖҚгҒЁиЁҳгҒ•гӮҢгӮӢгҒҢгҖҒж—Ҙжң¬еҒҙеҸІж–ҷгҒ«гҒҜи©ІеҪ“иҖ…гҒҢгҒҠгӮүгҒҡгҖҒи«ёиӘ¬гҒӮгӮӢгҖӮе·қи¶Ҡжі°еҚҡгҖҢжұҺжө·е°ҸйҢІгҒ®ејҳе®үгҒ®еҪ№иЁҳдәӢгҒ«гҒӨгҒ„гҒҰгҖҚгҖҺи»ҚдәӢеҸІеӯҰгҖҸз¬¬11е·»з¬¬1еҸ·гҖҒ1975е№ҙ6жңҲгҖҒ26-34й ҒгҖӮ | spouse = | children = {{ill|ShЕҚni Moritsune|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣзөҢ}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷӮзөҢ}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Morishige|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣж°Ҹ}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Sadatsune|ja|е°‘ејҗиІһиіҮ}} | notable = вҖ» Birth and death dates are debated. }} '''ShЕҚni Tsunesuke''' (е°‘ејҗ зөҢиіҮ) was a {{ill|samurai}} and retainer of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate}} during the mid-{{ill|Kamakura period}}. He was the son of {{ill|ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮиғҪ}} and the third head of the {{ill|ShЕҚni clan|ja|е°‘ејҗж°Ҹ}}. == Overview == The character "зөҢ" in Tsunesuke's name was likely granted as a favor by the fourth {{ill|Shikken|ja|еҹ·жЁ©}}, {{ill|HЕҚjЕҚ Tokiyori|ja|еҢ—жқЎзөҢжҷӮ}}, or by {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoritsune|ja|и—ӨеҺҹй јзөҢ}}, the fourth {{ill|Seii Taishogun|ja|еҫҒеӨ·еӨ§е°Ҷи»Қ}}, who gave the same character to Tokiyori. This suggests Tsunesuke underwent his coming-of-age ceremony (genpuku) between 1245 and 1246. There are varying theories regarding Tsunesuke's birth and death years. One theory suggests he was born in 1229 and died in 1292, while another suggests 1226 to 1289.[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B0%91%E5%BC%90%E7%B5%8C%E8%B3%87-1082512 гӮігғҲгғҗгғігӮҜ_е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ] However, as genpuku typically occurred in oneвҖҷs teens, the 1229 birth year is widely considered accurate. Tsunesuke shared responsibilities with his father in governing northern Kyushu and played a crucial role during the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|е…ғеҜҮ}}. He oversaw the fortification of defenses, such as the {{ill|Ishizumi walls|ja|е…ғеҜҮйҳІеЎҒ}}, and coordinated efforts against the Yuan forces. His earliest documented activity appears in a record from 1269.жңҚйғЁгҖҒ2014е№ҙгҖҒP.416гҖӮе…ёжӢ гҒҜгҖҺжӯҰйӣ„зҘһзӨҫж–ҮжӣёгҖҸпјҲгҖҺйҺҢеҖүйҒәж–ҮгҖҸз¬¬14е·»гғ»10503еҸ·ж–ҮжӣёпјүгҖӮ In 1274, during the {{ill|Battle of Bun'ei|ja|ж–Үж°ёгҒ®еҪ№}}, Tsunesuke's brother {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ}} led the Japanese forces. Tsunesuke's role during this campaign is unclear. By 1275, he inherited the familyвҖҷs headship from his father. In 1281, during the {{ill|Battle of KЕҚan|ja|ејҳе®үгҒ®еҪ№}}, Tsunesuke fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki|ja|еЈұеІҗеі¶гҒ®жҲҰгҒ„}}, where he expelled the Yuan forces but lost his son, {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ}}.
[[File:Mooko-SamuraiShips.jpg|300px|right|thumb|'''ShЕҚni Tsunesuke''' and other samurai ships during the Mongol invasion.
{{small|гҖҺ{{ill|MЕҚko ShЕ«rai Ekotoba|ja|и’ҷеҸӨиҘІжқҘзөөи©һ}}гҖҸеҫҢе·»гғ»зөө15гғ»з¬¬19зҙҷ}} Despite this tragedy, he continued to serve as a {{ill|Chinzei BugyЕҚ|ja|йҺ®иҘҝеҘүиЎҢ}} with {{ill|ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|ja|еӨ§еҸӢй јжі°}}. In 1284, following the death of the eighth {{ill|Shikken|ja|еҹ·жЁ©}}, {{ill|HЕҚjЕҚ Tokimune|ja|еҢ—жқЎжҷӮе®—}}, Tsunesuke took Buddhist vows and adopted the Dharma name JЕҚe. During the {{ill|Shimozuki Disturbance|ja|йңңжңҲйЁ’еӢ•}} of 1285, he supported {{ill|Taira no Yoritsuna|ja|е№ій јз¶ұ}} and defeated his brother {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ}}, aligning with the ruling {{ill|Tokuso family|ja|еҫ—е®—е®¶}}. This led to a weakening of the ShЕҚni clanвҖҷs influence, as they lost control of several provinces. In 1286, Tsunesuke was appointed to the {{ill|Chinzei Dangi-sho|ja|йҺ®иҘҝи«Үиӯ°жүҖ}} alongside {{ill|ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|ja|еӨ§еҸӢй јжі°}}, {{ill|Utsunomiya Michifusa|ja|е®ҮйғҪе®®йҖҡжҲҝ}}, and {{ill|Shibuya Shigekuni|ja|жёӢи°·йҮҚйғ·}}. He passed away in 1292. His eldest son {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ}} having died in battle, Tsunesuke was succeeded by {{ill|ShЕҚni Moritsune|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣзөҢ}}. His younger sons {{ill|ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷӮзөҢ}} and {{ill|ShЕҚni Morishige|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣж°Ҹ}} later founded the {{ill|Shiga clan|ja|еҝ—иіҖж°Ҹ}} and {{ill|Hirai clan|ja|е№ідә•ж°Ҹ}}, respectively. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *[[Motohisa Yasuda]], ed. гҖҺйҺҢеҖүгғ»е®Өз”әдәәеҗҚдәӢе…ё гӮігғігғ‘гӮҜгғҲзүҲгҖҸпјҲ[[Shin Jinbutsu Oraisha]], 1990пјү *[[Hattori Hideo]] гҖҺи’ҷеҸӨиҘІжқҘгҖҸпјҲ[[Yamakawa Publishing]], 2014пјү *[[Kuroita Katsumi]], ed. гҖҺж–°иЁӮеў—иЈңеӣҪеҸІеӨ§зі»гғ»е°ҠеҚ‘еҲҶи„ү з¬¬2зҜҮгҖҸпјҲ[[Yoshikawa KЕҚbunkan]]пјү {{ShЕҚni clan heads}} [[Category:ShЕҚni clan]] [[Category:Kamakura shogunate retainers]] [[Category:People of the Mongol invasions of Japan]] [[Category:1229 births]] [[Category:1292 deaths]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]][[category:original pages]] [[ja:е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ]]

{{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesuke|ja|е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ}} (е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ[[ja:е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ]]) [[Category:Https://ko.wikipedia.org/wiki/]][[ko:%EC%87%BC%EB%8B%88 %EC%93%B0%EB%84%A4%EC%8A%A4%EC%BC%80]] {{Infobox samurai | name = ShЕҚni Tsunesuke (е°‘ејҗ зөҢиіҮ) | image = | imagesize = | caption = | era = Mid-[[Kamakura period]] | birth_date = [[Kanki]] 1 (1229) вҖ» | death_date = [[ShЕҚЕҚ]] 5, [[8th month]], [[2nd day (lunar calendar)|2nd day]] (September 14, 1292) вҖ» | posthumous_name = JЕҚe (жө„жҒө; Dharma name)гҖҺе°ҠеҚ‘еҲҶи„үгҖҸз¬¬2зҜҮгғ»P.391гҖӮ | alias = Muto Tsunesuke | rank = | allegiance = [[Kamakura shogunate]] | lord = [[Prince Koreyasu]] | clan = {{ill|ShЕҚni clan|ja|е°‘ејҗж°Ҹ}} | parents = Father: {{ill|ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮиғҪ}} | siblings = Tsunesuke, {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ}}жңҚйғЁгҖҒ2014е№ҙгҖҒP.416гҖӮ, {{ill|ShЕҚni Morisue|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣиіҮ}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Munetsune|ja|е°‘ејҗе®—иіҮ}} (existence debated)гҖҺжұҺжө·е°ҸйҢІгҖҸгҒ«гҖҢеӨӘе®°и—ӨеҺҹе°‘еҚҝејҹе®—иіҮгҖҚгҒЁиЁҳгҒ•гӮҢгӮӢгҒҢгҖҒж—Ҙжң¬еҒҙеҸІж–ҷгҒ«гҒҜи©ІеҪ“иҖ…гҒҢгҒҠгӮүгҒҡгҖҒи«ёиӘ¬гҒӮгӮӢгҖӮе·қи¶Ҡжі°еҚҡгҖҢжұҺжө·е°ҸйҢІгҒ®ејҳе®үгҒ®еҪ№иЁҳдәӢгҒ«гҒӨгҒ„гҒҰгҖҚгҖҺи»ҚдәӢеҸІеӯҰгҖҸз¬¬11е·»з¬¬1еҸ·гҖҒ1975е№ҙ6жңҲгҖҒ26-34й ҒгҖӮ | spouse = | children = {{ill|ShЕҚni Moritsune|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣзөҢ}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷӮзөҢ}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Morishige|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣж°Ҹ}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Sadatsune|ja|е°‘ејҗиІһиіҮ}} | notable = вҖ» Birth and death dates are debated. }} '''ShЕҚni Tsunesuke''' (е°‘ејҗ зөҢиіҮ) was a {{ill|samurai}} and retainer of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate}} during the mid-{{ill|Kamakura period}}. He was the son of {{ill|ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮиғҪ}} and the third head of the {{ill|ShЕҚni clan|ja|е°‘ејҗж°Ҹ}}. == Overview == The character "зөҢ" in Tsunesuke's name was likely granted as a favor by the fourth {{ill|Shikken|ja|еҹ·жЁ©}}, {{ill|HЕҚjЕҚ Tokiyori|ja|еҢ—жқЎзөҢжҷӮ}}, or by {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoritsune|ja|и—ӨеҺҹй јзөҢ}}, the fourth {{ill|Seii Taishogun|ja|еҫҒеӨ·еӨ§е°Ҷи»Қ}}, who gave the same character to Tokiyori. This suggests Tsunesuke underwent his coming-of-age ceremony (genpuku) between 1245 and 1246. There are varying theories regarding Tsunesuke's birth and death years. One theory suggests he was born in 1229 and died in 1292, while another suggests 1226 to 1289.[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B0%91%E5%BC%90%E7%B5%8C%E8%B3%87-1082512 гӮігғҲгғҗгғігӮҜ_е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ] However, as genpuku typically occurred in oneвҖҷs teens, the 1229 birth year is widely considered accurate. Tsunesuke shared responsibilities with his father in governing northern Kyushu and played a crucial role during the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|е…ғеҜҮ}}. He oversaw the fortification of defenses, such as the {{ill|Ishizumi walls|ja|е…ғеҜҮйҳІеЎҒ}}, and coordinated efforts against the Yuan forces. His earliest documented activity appears in a record from 1269.жңҚйғЁгҖҒ2014е№ҙгҖҒP.416гҖӮе…ёжӢ гҒҜгҖҺжӯҰйӣ„зҘһзӨҫж–ҮжӣёгҖҸпјҲгҖҺйҺҢеҖүйҒәж–ҮгҖҸз¬¬14е·»гғ»10503еҸ·ж–ҮжӣёпјүгҖӮ In 1274, during the {{ill|Battle of Bun'ei|ja|ж–Үж°ёгҒ®еҪ№}}, Tsunesuke's brother {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ}} led the Japanese forces. Tsunesuke's role during this campaign is unclear. By 1275, he inherited the familyвҖҷs headship from his father. In 1281, during the {{ill|Battle of KЕҚan|ja|ејҳе®үгҒ®еҪ№}}, Tsunesuke fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki|ja|еЈұеІҗеі¶гҒ®жҲҰгҒ„}}, where he expelled the Yuan forces but lost his son, {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ}}.
[[File:Mooko-SamuraiShips.jpg|300px|right|thumb|'''ShЕҚni Tsunesuke''' and other samurai ships during the Mongol invasion.
{{small|гҖҺ{{ill|MЕҚko ShЕ«rai Ekotoba|ja|и’ҷеҸӨиҘІжқҘзөөи©һ}}гҖҸеҫҢе·»гғ»зөө15гғ»з¬¬19зҙҷ}} Despite this tragedy, he continued to serve as a {{ill|Chinzei BugyЕҚ|ja|йҺ®иҘҝеҘүиЎҢ}} with {{ill|ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|ja|еӨ§еҸӢй јжі°}}. In 1284, following the death of the eighth {{ill|Shikken|ja|еҹ·жЁ©}}, {{ill|HЕҚjЕҚ Tokimune|ja|еҢ—жқЎжҷӮе®—}}, Tsunesuke took Buddhist vows and adopted the Dharma name JЕҚe. During the {{ill|Shimozuki Disturbance|ja|йңңжңҲйЁ’еӢ•}} of 1285, he supported {{ill|Taira no Yoritsuna|ja|е№ій јз¶ұ}} and defeated his brother {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ}}, aligning with the ruling {{ill|Tokuso family|ja|еҫ—е®—е®¶}}. This led to a weakening of the ShЕҚni clanвҖҷs influence, as they lost control of several provinces. In 1286, Tsunesuke was appointed to the {{ill|Chinzei Dangi-sho|ja|йҺ®иҘҝи«Үиӯ°жүҖ}} alongside {{ill|ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|ja|еӨ§еҸӢй јжі°}}, {{ill|Utsunomiya Michifusa|ja|е®ҮйғҪе®®йҖҡжҲҝ}}, and {{ill|Shibuya Shigekuni|ja|жёӢи°·йҮҚйғ·}}. He passed away in 1292. His eldest son {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ}} having died in battle, Tsunesuke was succeeded by {{ill|ShЕҚni Moritsune|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣзөҢ}}. His younger sons {{ill|ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷӮзөҢ}} and {{ill|ShЕҚni Morishige|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣж°Ҹ}} later founded the {{ill|Shiga clan|ja|еҝ—иіҖж°Ҹ}} and {{ill|Hirai clan|ja|е№ідә•ж°Ҹ}}, respectively. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *[[Motohisa Yasuda]], ed. гҖҺйҺҢеҖүгғ»е®Өз”әдәәеҗҚдәӢе…ё гӮігғігғ‘гӮҜгғҲзүҲгҖҸпјҲ[[Shin Jinbutsu Oraisha]], 1990пјү *[[Hattori Hideo]] гҖҺи’ҷеҸӨиҘІжқҘгҖҸпјҲ[[Yamakawa Publishing]], 2014пјү *[[Kuroita Katsumi]], ed. гҖҺж–°иЁӮеў—иЈңеӣҪеҸІеӨ§зі»гғ»е°ҠеҚ‘еҲҶи„ү з¬¬2зҜҮгҖҸпјҲ[[Yoshikawa KЕҚbunkan]]пјү {{ShЕҚni clan heads}} [[Category:ShЕҚni clan]] [[Category:Kamakura shogunate retainers]] [[Category:People of the Mongol invasions of Japan]] [[Category:1229 births]] [[Category:1292 deaths]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]][[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ]]

{{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesuke|ja|е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ}} (е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ[[ja:е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ]]) [[Category:Https://ko.wikipedia.org/wiki/]][[ko:%EC%87%BC%EB%8B%88 %EC%93%B0%EB%84%A4%EC%8A%A4%EC%BC%80]] {{Infobox samurai | name = ShЕҚni Tsunesuke (е°‘ејҗ зөҢиіҮ) | image = | imagesize = | caption = | era = Mid-[[Kamakura period]] | birth_date = [[Kanki]] 1 (1229) вҖ» | death_date = [[ShЕҚЕҚ]] 5, [[8th month]], [[2nd day (lunar calendar)|2nd day]] (September 14, 1292) вҖ» | posthumous_name = JЕҚe (жө„жҒө; Dharma name)гҖҺе°ҠеҚ‘еҲҶи„үгҖҸз¬¬2зҜҮгғ»P.391гҖӮ | alias = Muto Tsunesuke | rank = | allegiance = [[Kamakura shogunate]] | lord = [[Prince Koreyasu]] | clan = {{ill|ShЕҚni clan|ja|е°‘ејҗж°Ҹ}} | parents = Father: {{ill|ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮиғҪ}} | siblings = Tsunesuke, {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ}}жңҚйғЁгҖҒ2014е№ҙгҖҒP.416гҖӮ, {{ill|ShЕҚni Morisue|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣиіҮ}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Munetsune|ja|е°‘ејҗе®—иіҮ}} (existence debated)гҖҺжұҺжө·е°ҸйҢІгҖҸгҒ«гҖҢеӨӘе®°и—ӨеҺҹе°‘еҚҝејҹе®—иіҮгҖҚгҒЁиЁҳгҒ•гӮҢгӮӢгҒҢгҖҒж—Ҙжң¬еҒҙеҸІж–ҷгҒ«гҒҜи©ІеҪ“иҖ…гҒҢгҒҠгӮүгҒҡгҖҒи«ёиӘ¬гҒӮгӮӢгҖӮе·қи¶Ҡжі°еҚҡгҖҢжұҺжө·е°ҸйҢІгҒ®ејҳе®үгҒ®еҪ№иЁҳдәӢгҒ«гҒӨгҒ„гҒҰгҖҚгҖҺи»ҚдәӢеҸІеӯҰгҖҸз¬¬11е·»з¬¬1еҸ·гҖҒ1975е№ҙ6жңҲгҖҒ26-34й ҒгҖӮ | spouse = | children = {{ill|ShЕҚni Moritsune|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣзөҢ}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷӮзөҢ}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Morishige|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣж°Ҹ}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Sadatsune|ja|е°‘ејҗиІһиіҮ}} | notable = вҖ» Birth and death dates are debated. }} '''ShЕҚni Tsunesuke''' (е°‘ејҗ зөҢиіҮ) was a {{ill|samurai}} and retainer of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate}} during the mid-{{ill|Kamakura period}}. He was the son of {{ill|ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮиғҪ}} and the third head of the {{ill|ShЕҚni clan|ja|е°‘ејҗж°Ҹ}}. == Overview == The character "зөҢ" in Tsunesuke's name was likely granted as a favor by the fourth {{ill|Shikken|ja|еҹ·жЁ©}}, {{ill|HЕҚjЕҚ Tokiyori|ja|еҢ—жқЎзөҢжҷӮ}}, or by {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoritsune|ja|и—ӨеҺҹй јзөҢ}}, the fourth {{ill|Seii Taishogun|ja|еҫҒеӨ·еӨ§е°Ҷи»Қ}}, who gave the same character to Tokiyori. This suggests Tsunesuke underwent his coming-of-age ceremony (genpuku) between 1245 and 1246. There are varying theories regarding Tsunesuke's birth and death years. One theory suggests he was born in 1229 and died in 1292, while another suggests 1226 to 1289.[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B0%91%E5%BC%90%E7%B5%8C%E8%B3%87-1082512 гӮігғҲгғҗгғігӮҜ_е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ] However, as genpuku typically occurred in oneвҖҷs teens, the 1229 birth year is widely considered accurate. Tsunesuke shared responsibilities with his father in governing northern Kyushu and played a crucial role during the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|е…ғеҜҮ}}. He oversaw the fortification of defenses, such as the {{ill|Ishizumi walls|ja|е…ғеҜҮйҳІеЎҒ}}, and coordinated efforts against the Yuan forces. His earliest documented activity appears in a record from 1269.жңҚйғЁгҖҒ2014е№ҙгҖҒP.416гҖӮе…ёжӢ гҒҜгҖҺжӯҰйӣ„зҘһзӨҫж–ҮжӣёгҖҸпјҲгҖҺйҺҢеҖүйҒәж–ҮгҖҸз¬¬14е·»гғ»10503еҸ·ж–ҮжӣёпјүгҖӮ In 1274, during the {{ill|Battle of Bun'ei|ja|ж–Үж°ёгҒ®еҪ№}}, Tsunesuke's brother {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ}} led the Japanese forces. Tsunesuke's role during this campaign is unclear. By 1275, he inherited the familyвҖҷs headship from his father. In 1281, during the {{ill|Battle of KЕҚan|ja|ејҳе®үгҒ®еҪ№}}, Tsunesuke fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki|ja|еЈұеІҗеі¶гҒ®жҲҰгҒ„}}, where he expelled the Yuan forces but lost his son, {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ}}.
[[File:Mooko-SamuraiShips.jpg|300px|right|thumb|'''ShЕҚni Tsunesuke''' and other samurai ships during the Mongol invasion.
{{small|гҖҺ{{ill|MЕҚko ShЕ«rai Ekotoba|ja|и’ҷеҸӨиҘІжқҘзөөи©һ}}гҖҸеҫҢе·»гғ»зөө15гғ»з¬¬19зҙҷ}} Despite this tragedy, he continued to serve as a {{ill|Chinzei BugyЕҚ|ja|йҺ®иҘҝеҘүиЎҢ}} with {{ill|ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|ja|еӨ§еҸӢй јжі°}}. In 1284, following the death of the eighth {{ill|Shikken|ja|еҹ·жЁ©}}, {{ill|HЕҚjЕҚ Tokimune|ja|еҢ—жқЎжҷӮе®—}}, Tsunesuke took Buddhist vows and adopted the Dharma name JЕҚe. During the {{ill|Shimozuki Disturbance|ja|йңңжңҲйЁ’еӢ•}} of 1285, he supported {{ill|Taira no Yoritsuna|ja|е№ій јз¶ұ}} and defeated his brother {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ}}, aligning with the ruling {{ill|Tokuso family|ja|еҫ—е®—е®¶}}. This led to a weakening of the ShЕҚni clanвҖҷs influence, as they lost control of several provinces. In 1286, Tsunesuke was appointed to the {{ill|Chinzei Dangi-sho|ja|йҺ®иҘҝи«Үиӯ°жүҖ}} alongside {{ill|ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|ja|еӨ§еҸӢй јжі°}}, {{ill|Utsunomiya Michifusa|ja|е®ҮйғҪе®®йҖҡжҲҝ}}, and {{ill|Shibuya Shigekuni|ja|жёӢи°·йҮҚйғ·}}. He passed away in 1292. His eldest son {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ}} having died in battle, Tsunesuke was succeeded by {{ill|ShЕҚni Moritsune|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣзөҢ}}. His younger sons {{ill|ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷӮзөҢ}} and {{ill|ShЕҚni Morishige|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣж°Ҹ}} later founded the {{ill|Shiga clan|ja|еҝ—иіҖж°Ҹ}} and {{ill|Hirai clan|ja|е№ідә•ж°Ҹ}}, respectively. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *[[Motohisa Yasuda]], ed. гҖҺйҺҢеҖүгғ»е®Өз”әдәәеҗҚдәӢе…ё гӮігғігғ‘гӮҜгғҲзүҲгҖҸпјҲ[[Shin Jinbutsu Oraisha]], 1990пјү *[[Hattori Hideo]] гҖҺи’ҷеҸӨиҘІжқҘгҖҸпјҲ[[Yamakawa Publishing]], 2014пјү *[[Kuroita Katsumi]], ed. гҖҺж–°иЁӮеў—иЈңеӣҪеҸІеӨ§зі»гғ»е°ҠеҚ‘еҲҶи„ү з¬¬2зҜҮгҖҸпјҲ[[Yoshikawa KЕҚbunkan]]пјү {{ShЕҚni clan heads}} [[Category:ShЕҚni clan]] [[Category:Kamakura shogunate retainers]] [[Category:People of the Mongol invasions of Japan]] [[Category:1229 births]] [[Category:1292 deaths]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ]]

{{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesuke|ja|е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ}} (е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ[[ja:е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ]]) [[Category:Https://ko.wikipedia.org/wiki/]][[ko:%EC%87%BC%EB%8B%88 %EC%93%B0%EB%84%A4%EC%8A%A4%EC%BC%80]] {{Infobox samurai | name = ShЕҚni Tsunesuke (е°‘ејҗ зөҢиіҮ) | image = | imagesize = | caption = | era = Mid-[[Kamakura period]] | birth_date = [[Kanki]] 1 (1229) вҖ» | death_date = [[ShЕҚЕҚ]] 5, [[8th month]], [[2nd day (lunar calendar)|2nd day]] (September 14, 1292) вҖ» | posthumous_name = JЕҚe (жө„жҒө; Dharma name)гҖҺе°ҠеҚ‘еҲҶи„үгҖҸз¬¬2зҜҮгғ»P.391гҖӮ | alias = Muto Tsunesuke | rank = | allegiance = [[Kamakura shogunate]] | lord = [[Prince Koreyasu]] | clan = {{ill|ShЕҚni clan|ja|е°‘ејҗж°Ҹ}} | parents = Father: {{ill|ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮиғҪ}} | siblings = Tsunesuke, {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ}}жңҚйғЁгҖҒ2014е№ҙгҖҒP.416гҖӮ, {{ill|ShЕҚni Morisue|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣиіҮ}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Munetsune|ja|е°‘ејҗе®—иіҮ}} (existence debated)гҖҺжұҺжө·е°ҸйҢІгҖҸгҒ«гҖҢеӨӘе®°и—ӨеҺҹе°‘еҚҝејҹе®—иіҮгҖҚгҒЁиЁҳгҒ•гӮҢгӮӢгҒҢгҖҒж—Ҙжң¬еҒҙеҸІж–ҷгҒ«гҒҜи©ІеҪ“иҖ…гҒҢгҒҠгӮүгҒҡгҖҒи«ёиӘ¬гҒӮгӮӢгҖӮе·қи¶Ҡжі°еҚҡгҖҢжұҺжө·е°ҸйҢІгҒ®ејҳе®үгҒ®еҪ№иЁҳдәӢгҒ«гҒӨгҒ„гҒҰгҖҚгҖҺи»ҚдәӢеҸІеӯҰгҖҸз¬¬11е·»з¬¬1еҸ·гҖҒ1975е№ҙ6жңҲгҖҒ26-34й ҒгҖӮ | spouse = | children = {{ill|ShЕҚni Moritsune|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣзөҢ}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷӮзөҢ}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Morishige|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣж°Ҹ}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Sadatsune|ja|е°‘ејҗиІһиіҮ}} | notable = вҖ» Birth and death dates are debated. }} '''ShЕҚni Tsunesuke''' (е°‘ејҗ зөҢиіҮ) was a {{ill|samurai}} and retainer of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate}} during the mid-{{ill|Kamakura period}}. He was the son of {{ill|ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮиғҪ}} and the third head of the {{ill|ShЕҚni clan|ja|е°‘ејҗж°Ҹ}}. == Overview == The character "зөҢ" in Tsunesuke's name was likely granted as a favor by the fourth {{ill|Shikken|ja|еҹ·жЁ©}}, {{ill|HЕҚjЕҚ Tokiyori|ja|еҢ—жқЎзөҢжҷӮ}}, or by {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoritsune|ja|и—ӨеҺҹй јзөҢ}}, the fourth {{ill|Seii Taishogun|ja|еҫҒеӨ·еӨ§е°Ҷи»Қ}}, who gave the same character to Tokiyori. This suggests Tsunesuke underwent his coming-of-age ceremony (genpuku) between 1245 and 1246. There are varying theories regarding Tsunesuke's birth and death years. One theory suggests he was born in 1229 and died in 1292, while another suggests 1226 to 1289.[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B0%91%E5%BC%90%E7%B5%8C%E8%B3%87-1082512 гӮігғҲгғҗгғігӮҜ_е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ] However, as genpuku typically occurred in oneвҖҷs teens, the 1229 birth year is widely considered accurate. Tsunesuke shared responsibilities with his father in governing northern Kyushu and played a crucial role during the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|е…ғеҜҮ}}. He oversaw the fortification of defenses, such as the {{ill|Ishizumi walls|ja|е…ғеҜҮйҳІеЎҒ}}, and coordinated efforts against the Yuan forces. His earliest documented activity appears in a record from 1269.жңҚйғЁгҖҒ2014е№ҙгҖҒP.416гҖӮе…ёжӢ гҒҜгҖҺжӯҰйӣ„зҘһзӨҫж–ҮжӣёгҖҸпјҲгҖҺйҺҢеҖүйҒәж–ҮгҖҸз¬¬14е·»гғ»10503еҸ·ж–ҮжӣёпјүгҖӮ In 1274, during the {{ill|Battle of Bun'ei|ja|ж–Үж°ёгҒ®еҪ№}}, Tsunesuke's brother {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ}} led the Japanese forces. Tsunesuke's role during this campaign is unclear. By 1275, he inherited the familyвҖҷs headship from his father. In 1281, during the {{ill|Battle of KЕҚan|ja|ејҳе®үгҒ®еҪ№}}, Tsunesuke fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki|ja|еЈұеІҗеі¶гҒ®жҲҰгҒ„}}, where he expelled the Yuan forces but lost his son, {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ}}.
[[File:Mooko-SamuraiShips.jpg|300px|right|thumb|'''ShЕҚni Tsunesuke''' and other samurai ships during the Mongol invasion.
{{small|гҖҺ{{ill|MЕҚko ShЕ«rai Ekotoba|ja|и’ҷеҸӨиҘІжқҘзөөи©һ}}гҖҸеҫҢе·»гғ»зөө15гғ»з¬¬19зҙҷ}} Despite this tragedy, he continued to serve as a {{ill|Chinzei BugyЕҚ|ja|йҺ®иҘҝеҘүиЎҢ}} with {{ill|ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|ja|еӨ§еҸӢй јжі°}}. In 1284, following the death of the eighth {{ill|Shikken|ja|еҹ·жЁ©}}, {{ill|HЕҚjЕҚ Tokimune|ja|еҢ—жқЎжҷӮе®—}}, Tsunesuke took Buddhist vows and adopted the Dharma name JЕҚe. During the {{ill|Shimozuki Disturbance|ja|йңңжңҲйЁ’еӢ•}} of 1285, he supported {{ill|Taira no Yoritsuna|ja|е№ій јз¶ұ}} and defeated his brother {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ}}, aligning with the ruling {{ill|Tokuso family|ja|еҫ—е®—е®¶}}. This led to a weakening of the ShЕҚni clanвҖҷs influence, as they lost control of several provinces. In 1286, Tsunesuke was appointed to the {{ill|Chinzei Dangi-sho|ja|йҺ®иҘҝи«Үиӯ°жүҖ}} alongside {{ill|ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|ja|еӨ§еҸӢй јжі°}}, {{ill|Utsunomiya Michifusa|ja|е®ҮйғҪе®®йҖҡжҲҝ}}, and {{ill|Shibuya Shigekuni|ja|жёӢи°·йҮҚйғ·}}. He passed away in 1292. His eldest son {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ}} having died in battle, Tsunesuke was succeeded by {{ill|ShЕҚni Moritsune|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣзөҢ}}. His younger sons {{ill|ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷӮзөҢ}} and {{ill|ShЕҚni Morishige|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣж°Ҹ}} later founded the {{ill|Shiga clan|ja|еҝ—иіҖж°Ҹ}} and {{ill|Hirai clan|ja|е№ідә•ж°Ҹ}}, respectively. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *[[Motohisa Yasuda]], ed. гҖҺйҺҢеҖүгғ»е®Өз”әдәәеҗҚдәӢе…ё гӮігғігғ‘гӮҜгғҲзүҲгҖҸпјҲ[[Shin Jinbutsu Oraisha]], 1990пјү *[[Hattori Hideo]] гҖҺи’ҷеҸӨиҘІжқҘгҖҸпјҲ[[Yamakawa Publishing]], 2014пјү *[[Kuroita Katsumi]], ed. гҖҺж–°иЁӮеў—иЈңеӣҪеҸІеӨ§зі»гғ»е°ҠеҚ‘еҲҶи„ү з¬¬2зҜҮгҖҸпјҲ[[Yoshikawa KЕҚbunkan]]пјү {{ShЕҚni clan heads}} [[Category:ShЕҚni clan]] [[Category:Kamakura shogunate retainers]] [[Category:People of the Mongol invasions of Japan]] [[Category:1229 births]] [[Category:1292 deaths]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump 1-20]] [[ja:е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ]]

{{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesuke|ja|е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ}} (е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ[[ja:е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ]]) [[Category:Https://ko.wikipedia.org/wiki/]][[ko:%EC%87%BC%EB%8B%88 %EC%93%B0%EB%84%A4%EC%8A%A4%EC%BC%80]] {{Infobox samurai | name = ShЕҚni Tsunesuke (е°‘ејҗ зөҢиіҮ) | image = | imagesize = | caption = | era = Mid-[[Kamakura period]] | birth_date = [[Kanki]] 1 (1229) вҖ» | death_date = [[ShЕҚЕҚ]] 5, [[8th month]], [[2nd day (lunar calendar)|2nd day]] (September 14, 1292) вҖ» | posthumous_name = JЕҚe (жө„жҒө; Dharma name)гҖҺе°ҠеҚ‘еҲҶи„үгҖҸз¬¬2зҜҮгғ»P.391гҖӮ | alias = Muto Tsunesuke | rank = | allegiance = [[Kamakura shogunate]] | lord = [[Prince Koreyasu]] | clan = {{ill|ShЕҚni clan|ja|е°‘ејҗж°Ҹ}} | parents = Father: {{ill|ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮиғҪ}} | siblings = Tsunesuke, {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ}}жңҚйғЁгҖҒ2014е№ҙгҖҒP.416гҖӮ, {{ill|ShЕҚni Morisue|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣиіҮ}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Munetsune|ja|е°‘ејҗе®—иіҮ}} (existence debated)гҖҺжұҺжө·е°ҸйҢІгҖҸгҒ«гҖҢеӨӘе®°и—ӨеҺҹе°‘еҚҝејҹе®—иіҮгҖҚгҒЁиЁҳгҒ•гӮҢгӮӢгҒҢгҖҒж—Ҙжң¬еҒҙеҸІж–ҷгҒ«гҒҜи©ІеҪ“иҖ…гҒҢгҒҠгӮүгҒҡгҖҒи«ёиӘ¬гҒӮгӮӢгҖӮе·қи¶Ҡжі°еҚҡгҖҢжұҺжө·е°ҸйҢІгҒ®ејҳе®үгҒ®еҪ№иЁҳдәӢгҒ«гҒӨгҒ„гҒҰгҖҚгҖҺи»ҚдәӢеҸІеӯҰгҖҸз¬¬11е·»з¬¬1еҸ·гҖҒ1975е№ҙ6жңҲгҖҒ26-34й ҒгҖӮ | spouse = | children = {{ill|ShЕҚni Moritsune|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣзөҢ}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷӮзөҢ}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Morishige|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣж°Ҹ}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Sadatsune|ja|е°‘ејҗиІһиіҮ}} | notable = вҖ» Birth and death dates are debated. }} '''ShЕҚni Tsunesuke''' (е°‘ејҗ зөҢиіҮ) was a {{ill|samurai}} and retainer of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate}} during the mid-{{ill|Kamakura period}}. He was the son of {{ill|ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮиғҪ}} and the third head of the {{ill|ShЕҚni clan|ja|е°‘ејҗж°Ҹ}}. == Overview == The character "зөҢ" in Tsunesuke's name was likely granted as a favor by the fourth {{ill|Shikken|ja|еҹ·жЁ©}}, {{ill|HЕҚjЕҚ Tokiyori|ja|еҢ—жқЎзөҢжҷӮ}}, or by {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoritsune|ja|и—ӨеҺҹй јзөҢ}}, the fourth {{ill|Seii Taishogun|ja|еҫҒеӨ·еӨ§е°Ҷи»Қ}}, who gave the same character to Tokiyori. This suggests Tsunesuke underwent his coming-of-age ceremony (genpuku) between 1245 and 1246. There are varying theories regarding Tsunesuke's birth and death years. One theory suggests he was born in 1229 and died in 1292, while another suggests 1226 to 1289.[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B0%91%E5%BC%90%E7%B5%8C%E8%B3%87-1082512 гӮігғҲгғҗгғігӮҜ_е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ] However, as genpuku typically occurred in oneвҖҷs teens, the 1229 birth year is widely considered accurate. Tsunesuke shared responsibilities with his father in governing northern Kyushu and played a crucial role during the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|е…ғеҜҮ}}. He oversaw the fortification of defenses, such as the {{ill|Ishizumi walls|ja|е…ғеҜҮйҳІеЎҒ}}, and coordinated efforts against the Yuan forces. His earliest documented activity appears in a record from 1269.жңҚйғЁгҖҒ2014е№ҙгҖҒP.416гҖӮе…ёжӢ гҒҜгҖҺжӯҰйӣ„зҘһзӨҫж–ҮжӣёгҖҸпјҲгҖҺйҺҢеҖүйҒәж–ҮгҖҸз¬¬14е·»гғ»10503еҸ·ж–ҮжӣёпјүгҖӮ In 1274, during the {{ill|Battle of Bun'ei|ja|ж–Үж°ёгҒ®еҪ№}}, Tsunesuke's brother {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ}} led the Japanese forces. Tsunesuke's role during this campaign is unclear. By 1275, he inherited the familyвҖҷs headship from his father. In 1281, during the {{ill|Battle of KЕҚan|ja|ејҳе®үгҒ®еҪ№}}, Tsunesuke fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki|ja|еЈұеІҗеі¶гҒ®жҲҰгҒ„}}, where he expelled the Yuan forces but lost his son, {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ}}.
[[File:Mooko-SamuraiShips.jpg|300px|right|thumb|'''ShЕҚni Tsunesuke''' and other samurai ships during the Mongol invasion.
{{small|гҖҺ{{ill|MЕҚko ShЕ«rai Ekotoba|ja|и’ҷеҸӨиҘІжқҘзөөи©һ}}гҖҸеҫҢе·»гғ»зөө15гғ»з¬¬19зҙҷ}} Despite this tragedy, he continued to serve as a {{ill|Chinzei BugyЕҚ|ja|йҺ®иҘҝеҘүиЎҢ}} with {{ill|ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|ja|еӨ§еҸӢй јжі°}}. In 1284, following the death of the eighth {{ill|Shikken|ja|еҹ·жЁ©}}, {{ill|HЕҚjЕҚ Tokimune|ja|еҢ—жқЎжҷӮе®—}}, Tsunesuke took Buddhist vows and adopted the Dharma name JЕҚe. During the {{ill|Shimozuki Disturbance|ja|йңңжңҲйЁ’еӢ•}} of 1285, he supported {{ill|Taira no Yoritsuna|ja|е№ій јз¶ұ}} and defeated his brother {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ}}, aligning with the ruling {{ill|Tokuso family|ja|еҫ—е®—е®¶}}. This led to a weakening of the ShЕҚni clanвҖҷs influence, as they lost control of several provinces. In 1286, Tsunesuke was appointed to the {{ill|Chinzei Dangi-sho|ja|йҺ®иҘҝи«Үиӯ°жүҖ}} alongside {{ill|ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|ja|еӨ§еҸӢй јжі°}}, {{ill|Utsunomiya Michifusa|ja|е®ҮйғҪе®®йҖҡжҲҝ}}, and {{ill|Shibuya Shigekuni|ja|жёӢи°·йҮҚйғ·}}. He passed away in 1292. His eldest son {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ}} having died in battle, Tsunesuke was succeeded by {{ill|ShЕҚni Moritsune|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣзөҢ}}. His younger sons {{ill|ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷӮзөҢ}} and {{ill|ShЕҚni Morishige|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣж°Ҹ}} later founded the {{ill|Shiga clan|ja|еҝ—иіҖж°Ҹ}} and {{ill|Hirai clan|ja|е№ідә•ж°Ҹ}}, respectively. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *[[Motohisa Yasuda]], ed. гҖҺйҺҢеҖүгғ»е®Өз”әдәәеҗҚдәӢе…ё гӮігғігғ‘гӮҜгғҲзүҲгҖҸпјҲ[[Shin Jinbutsu Oraisha]], 1990пјү *[[Hattori Hideo]] гҖҺи’ҷеҸӨиҘІжқҘгҖҸпјҲ[[Yamakawa Publishing]], 2014пјү *[[Kuroita Katsumi]], ed. гҖҺж–°иЁӮеў—иЈңеӣҪеҸІеӨ§зі»гғ»е°ҠеҚ‘еҲҶи„ү з¬¬2зҜҮгҖҸпјҲ[[Yoshikawa KЕҚbunkan]]пјү {{ShЕҚni clan heads}} [[Category:ShЕҚni clan]] [[Category:Kamakura shogunate retainers]] [[Category:People of the Mongol invasions of Japan]] [[Category:1229 births]] [[Category:1292 deaths]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump 1-20]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[ja:е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ]]

He passed away in 1292. His eldest son {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ}} having died in battle, Tsunesuke was succeeded by {{ill|ShЕҚni Moritsune|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣзөҢ}}. His younger sons {{ill|ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷӮзөҢ}} and {{ill|ShЕҚni Morishige|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣж°Ҹ}} later founded the {{ill|Shiga clan|ja|еҝ—иіҖж°Ҹ}} and {{ill|Hirai clan|ja|е№ідә•ж°Ҹ}}, respectively. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *[[Motohisa Yasuda]], ed. гҖҺйҺҢеҖүгғ»е®Өз”әдәәеҗҚдәӢе…ё гӮігғігғ‘гӮҜгғҲзүҲгҖҸпјҲ[[Shin Jinbutsu Oraisha]], 1990пјү *[[Hattori Hideo]] гҖҺи’ҷеҸӨиҘІжқҘгҖҸпјҲ[[Yamakawa Publishing]], 2014пјү *[[Kuroita Katsumi]], ed. гҖҺж–°иЁӮеў—иЈңеӣҪеҸІеӨ§зі»гғ»е°ҠеҚ‘еҲҶи„ү з¬¬2зҜҮгҖҸпјҲ[[Yoshikawa KЕҚbunkan]]пјү {{ShЕҚni clan heads}} [[Category:ShЕҚni clan]] [[Category:Kamakura shogunate retainers]] [[Category:People of the Mongol invasions of Japan]] [[Category:1229 births]] [[Category:1292 deaths]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump 1-20]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:dd]] [[ja:е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ]]

{{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesuke|ja|е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ}} (е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ[[ja:е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ]]) [[Category:Https://ko.wikipedia.org/wiki/]][[ko:%EC%87%BC%EB%8B%88 %EC%93%B0%EB%84%A4%EC%8A%A4%EC%BC%80]] {{Infobox samurai | name = ShЕҚni Tsunesuke (е°‘ејҗ зөҢиіҮ) | image = | imagesize = | caption = | era = Mid-[[Kamakura period]] | birth_date = [[Kanki]] 1 (1229) вҖ» | death_date = [[ShЕҚЕҚ]] 5, [[8th month]], [[2nd day (lunar calendar)|2nd day]] (September 14, 1292) вҖ» | posthumous_name = JЕҚe (жө„жҒө; Dharma name)гҖҺе°ҠеҚ‘еҲҶи„үгҖҸз¬¬2зҜҮгғ»P.391гҖӮ | alias = Muto Tsunesuke | rank = | allegiance = [[Kamakura shogunate]] | lord = [[Prince Koreyasu]] | clan = {{ill|ShЕҚni clan|ja|е°‘ејҗж°Ҹ}} | parents = Father: {{ill|ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮиғҪ}} | siblings = Tsunesuke, {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ}}жңҚйғЁгҖҒ2014е№ҙгҖҒP.416гҖӮ, {{ill|ShЕҚni Morisue|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣиіҮ}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Munetsune|ja|е°‘ејҗе®—иіҮ}} (existence debated)гҖҺжұҺжө·е°ҸйҢІгҖҸгҒ«гҖҢеӨӘе®°и—ӨеҺҹе°‘еҚҝејҹе®—иіҮгҖҚгҒЁиЁҳгҒ•гӮҢгӮӢгҒҢгҖҒж—Ҙжң¬еҒҙеҸІж–ҷгҒ«гҒҜи©ІеҪ“иҖ…гҒҢгҒҠгӮүгҒҡгҖҒи«ёиӘ¬гҒӮгӮӢгҖӮе·қи¶Ҡжі°еҚҡгҖҢжұҺжө·е°ҸйҢІгҒ®ејҳе®үгҒ®еҪ№иЁҳдәӢгҒ«гҒӨгҒ„гҒҰгҖҚгҖҺи»ҚдәӢеҸІеӯҰгҖҸз¬¬11е·»з¬¬1еҸ·гҖҒ1975е№ҙ6жңҲгҖҒ26-34й ҒгҖӮ | spouse = | children = {{ill|ShЕҚni Moritsune|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣзөҢ}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷӮзөҢ}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Morishige|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣж°Ҹ}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Sadatsune|ja|е°‘ејҗиІһиіҮ}} | notable = вҖ» Birth and death dates are debated. }} '''ShЕҚni Tsunesuke''' (е°‘ејҗ зөҢиіҮ) was a {{ill|samurai}} and retainer of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate}} during the mid-{{ill|Kamakura period}}. He was the son of {{ill|ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮиғҪ}} and the third head of the {{ill|ShЕҚni clan|ja|е°‘ејҗж°Ҹ}}. == Overview == The character "зөҢ" in Tsunesuke's name was likely granted as a favor by the fourth {{ill|Shikken|ja|еҹ·жЁ©}}, {{ill|HЕҚjЕҚ Tokiyori|ja|еҢ—жқЎзөҢжҷӮ}}, or by {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoritsune|ja|и—ӨеҺҹй јзөҢ}}, the fourth {{ill|Seii Taishogun|ja|еҫҒеӨ·еӨ§е°Ҷи»Қ}}, who gave the same character to Tokiyori. This suggests Tsunesuke underwent his coming-of-age ceremony (genpuku) between 1245 and 1246. There are varying theories regarding Tsunesuke's birth and death years. One theory suggests he was born in 1229 and died in 1292, while another suggests 1226 to 1289.[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B0%91%E5%BC%90%E7%B5%8C%E8%B3%87-1082512 гӮігғҲгғҗгғігӮҜ_е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ] However, as genpuku typically occurred in oneвҖҷs teens, the 1229 birth year is widely considered accurate. Tsunesuke shared responsibilities with his father in governing northern Kyushu and played a crucial role during the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|е…ғеҜҮ}}. He oversaw the fortification of defenses, such as the {{ill|Ishizumi walls|ja|е…ғеҜҮйҳІеЎҒ}}, and coordinated efforts against the Yuan forces. His earliest documented activity appears in a record from 1269.жңҚйғЁгҖҒ2014е№ҙгҖҒP.416гҖӮе…ёжӢ гҒҜгҖҺжӯҰйӣ„зҘһзӨҫж–ҮжӣёгҖҸпјҲгҖҺйҺҢеҖүйҒәж–ҮгҖҸз¬¬14е·»гғ»10503еҸ·ж–ҮжӣёпјүгҖӮ In 1274, during the {{ill|Battle of Bun'ei|ja|ж–Үж°ёгҒ®еҪ№}}, Tsunesuke's brother {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ}} led the Japanese forces. Tsunesuke's role during this campaign is unclear. By 1275, he inherited the familyвҖҷs headship from his father. In 1281, during the {{ill|Battle of KЕҚan|ja|ејҳе®үгҒ®еҪ№}}, Tsunesuke fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki|ja|еЈұеІҗеі¶гҒ®жҲҰгҒ„}}, where he expelled the Yuan forces but lost his son, {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ}}.
[[File:Mooko-SamuraiShips.jpg|300px|right|thumb|'''ShЕҚni Tsunesuke''' and other samurai ships during the Mongol invasion.
{{small|гҖҺ{{ill|MЕҚko ShЕ«rai Ekotoba|ja|и’ҷеҸӨиҘІжқҘзөөи©һ}}гҖҸеҫҢе·»гғ»зөө15гғ»з¬¬19зҙҷ}} Despite this tragedy, he continued to serve as a {{ill|Chinzei BugyЕҚ|ja|йҺ®иҘҝеҘүиЎҢ}} with {{ill|ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|ja|еӨ§еҸӢй јжі°}}. In 1284, following the death of the eighth {{ill|Shikken|ja|еҹ·жЁ©}}, {{ill|HЕҚjЕҚ Tokimune|ja|еҢ—жқЎжҷӮе®—}}, Tsunesuke took Buddhist vows and adopted the Dharma name JЕҚe. During the {{ill|Shimozuki Disturbance|ja|йңңжңҲйЁ’еӢ•}} of 1285, he supported {{ill|Taira no Yoritsuna|ja|е№ій јз¶ұ}} and defeated his brother {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ}}, aligning with the ruling {{ill|Tokuso family|ja|еҫ—е®—е®¶}}. This led to a weakening of the ShЕҚni clanвҖҷs influence, as they lost control of several provinces. In 1286, Tsunesuke was appointed to the {{ill|Chinzei Dangi-sho|ja|йҺ®иҘҝи«Үиӯ°жүҖ}} alongside {{ill|ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|ja|еӨ§еҸӢй јжі°}}, {{ill|Utsunomiya Michifusa|ja|е®ҮйғҪе®®йҖҡжҲҝ}}, and {{ill|Shibuya Shigekuni|ja|жёӢи°·йҮҚйғ·}}. He passed away in 1292. His eldest son {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ}} having died in battle, Tsunesuke was succeeded by {{ill|ShЕҚni Moritsune|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣзөҢ}}. His younger sons {{ill|ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷӮзөҢ}} and {{ill|ShЕҚni Morishige|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣж°Ҹ}} later founded the {{ill|Shiga clan|ja|еҝ—иіҖж°Ҹ}} and {{ill|Hirai clan|ja|е№ідә•ж°Ҹ}}, respectively. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *[[Motohisa Yasuda]], ed. гҖҺйҺҢеҖүгғ»е®Өз”әдәәеҗҚдәӢе…ё гӮігғігғ‘гӮҜгғҲзүҲгҖҸпјҲ[[Shin Jinbutsu Oraisha]], 1990пјү *[[Hattori Hideo]] гҖҺи’ҷеҸӨиҘІжқҘгҖҸпјҲ[[Yamakawa Publishing]], 2014пјү *[[Kuroita Katsumi]], ed. гҖҺж–°иЁӮеў—иЈңеӣҪеҸІеӨ§зі»гғ»е°ҠеҚ‘еҲҶи„ү з¬¬2зҜҮгҖҸпјҲ[[Yoshikawa KЕҚbunkan]]пјү {{ShЕҚni clan heads}} [[Category:ShЕҚni clan]] [[Category:Kamakura shogunate retainers]] [[Category:People of the Mongol invasions of Japan]] [[Category:1229 births]] [[Category:1292 deaths]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump 1-20]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/]] [[ja:е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ]]

He passed away in 1292. His eldest son {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ}} having died in battle, Tsunesuke was succeeded by {{ill|ShЕҚni Moritsune|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣзөҢ}}. His younger sons {{ill|ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷӮзөҢ}} and {{ill|ShЕҚni Morishige|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣж°Ҹ}} later founded the {{ill|Shiga clan|ja|еҝ—иіҖж°Ҹ}} and {{ill|Hirai clan|ja|е№ідә•ж°Ҹ}}, respectively. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *[[Motohisa Yasuda]], ed. гҖҺйҺҢеҖүгғ»е®Өз”әдәәеҗҚдәӢе…ё гӮігғігғ‘гӮҜгғҲзүҲгҖҸпјҲ[[Shin Jinbutsu Oraisha]], 1990пјү *[[Hattori Hideo]] гҖҺи’ҷеҸӨиҘІжқҘгҖҸпјҲ[[Yamakawa Publishing]], 2014пјү *[[Kuroita Katsumi]], ed. гҖҺж–°иЁӮеў—иЈңеӣҪеҸІеӨ§зі»гғ»е°ҠеҚ‘еҲҶи„ү з¬¬2зҜҮгҖҸпјҲ[[Yoshikawa KЕҚbunkan]]пјү {{ShЕҚni clan heads}} [[Category:ShЕҚni clan]] [[Category:Kamakura shogunate retainers]] [[Category:People of the Mongol invasions of Japan]] [[Category:1229 births]] [[Category:1292 deaths]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump 1-20]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:dd]] [[ja:е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ]]

{{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesuke|ja|е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ}} (е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ[[ja:е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ]]) [[Category:Https://ko.wikipedia.org/wiki/]][[ko:%EC%87%BC%EB%8B%88 %EC%93%B0%EB%84%A4%EC%8A%A4%EC%BC%80]] {{Infobox samurai | name = ShЕҚni Tsunesuke (е°‘ејҗ зөҢиіҮ) | image = | imagesize = | caption = | era = Mid-[[Kamakura period]] | birth_date = [[Kanki]] 1 (1229) вҖ» | death_date = [[ShЕҚЕҚ]] 5, [[8th month]], [[2nd day (lunar calendar)|2nd day]] (September 14, 1292) вҖ» | posthumous_name = JЕҚe (жө„жҒө; Dharma name)гҖҺе°ҠеҚ‘еҲҶи„үгҖҸз¬¬2зҜҮгғ»P.391гҖӮ | alias = Muto Tsunesuke | rank = | allegiance = [[Kamakura shogunate]] | lord = [[Prince Koreyasu]] | clan = {{ill|ShЕҚni clan|ja|е°‘ејҗж°Ҹ}} | parents = Father: {{ill|ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮиғҪ}} | siblings = Tsunesuke, {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ}}жңҚйғЁгҖҒ2014е№ҙгҖҒP.416гҖӮ, {{ill|ShЕҚni Morisue|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣиіҮ}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Munetsune|ja|е°‘ејҗе®—иіҮ}} (existence debated)гҖҺжұҺжө·е°ҸйҢІгҖҸгҒ«гҖҢеӨӘе®°и—ӨеҺҹе°‘еҚҝејҹе®—иіҮгҖҚгҒЁиЁҳгҒ•гӮҢгӮӢгҒҢгҖҒж—Ҙжң¬еҒҙеҸІж–ҷгҒ«гҒҜи©ІеҪ“иҖ…гҒҢгҒҠгӮүгҒҡгҖҒи«ёиӘ¬гҒӮгӮӢгҖӮе·қи¶Ҡжі°еҚҡгҖҢжұҺжө·е°ҸйҢІгҒ®ејҳе®үгҒ®еҪ№иЁҳдәӢгҒ«гҒӨгҒ„гҒҰгҖҚгҖҺи»ҚдәӢеҸІеӯҰгҖҸз¬¬11е·»з¬¬1еҸ·гҖҒ1975е№ҙ6жңҲгҖҒ26-34й ҒгҖӮ | spouse = | children = {{ill|ShЕҚni Moritsune|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣзөҢ}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷӮзөҢ}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Morishige|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣж°Ҹ}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Sadatsune|ja|е°‘ејҗиІһиіҮ}} | notable = вҖ» Birth and death dates are debated. }} '''ShЕҚni Tsunesuke''' (е°‘ејҗ зөҢиіҮ) was a {{ill|samurai}} and retainer of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate}} during the mid-{{ill|Kamakura period}}. He was the son of {{ill|ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮиғҪ}} and the third head of the {{ill|ShЕҚni clan|ja|е°‘ејҗж°Ҹ}}. == Overview == The character "зөҢ" in Tsunesuke's name was likely granted as a favor by the fourth {{ill|Shikken|ja|еҹ·жЁ©}}, {{ill|HЕҚjЕҚ Tokiyori|ja|еҢ—жқЎзөҢжҷӮ}}, or by {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoritsune|ja|и—ӨеҺҹй јзөҢ}}, the fourth {{ill|Seii Taishogun|ja|еҫҒеӨ·еӨ§е°Ҷи»Қ}}, who gave the same character to Tokiyori. This suggests Tsunesuke underwent his coming-of-age ceremony (genpuku) between 1245 and 1246. There are varying theories regarding Tsunesuke's birth and death years. One theory suggests he was born in 1229 and died in 1292, while another suggests 1226 to 1289.[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B0%91%E5%BC%90%E7%B5%8C%E8%B3%87-1082512 гӮігғҲгғҗгғігӮҜ_е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ] However, as genpuku typically occurred in oneвҖҷs teens, the 1229 birth year is widely considered accurate. Tsunesuke shared responsibilities with his father in governing northern Kyushu and played a crucial role during the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|е…ғеҜҮ}}. He oversaw the fortification of defenses, such as the {{ill|Ishizumi walls|ja|е…ғеҜҮйҳІеЎҒ}}, and coordinated efforts against the Yuan forces. His earliest documented activity appears in a record from 1269.жңҚйғЁгҖҒ2014е№ҙгҖҒP.416гҖӮе…ёжӢ гҒҜгҖҺжӯҰйӣ„зҘһзӨҫж–ҮжӣёгҖҸпјҲгҖҺйҺҢеҖүйҒәж–ҮгҖҸз¬¬14е·»гғ»10503еҸ·ж–ҮжӣёпјүгҖӮ In 1274, during the {{ill|Battle of Bun'ei|ja|ж–Үж°ёгҒ®еҪ№}}, Tsunesuke's brother {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ}} led the Japanese forces. Tsunesuke's role during this campaign is unclear. By 1275, he inherited the familyвҖҷs headship from his father. In 1281, during the {{ill|Battle of KЕҚan|ja|ејҳе®үгҒ®еҪ№}}, Tsunesuke fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki|ja|еЈұеІҗеі¶гҒ®жҲҰгҒ„}}, where he expelled the Yuan forces but lost his son, {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ}}.
[[File:Mooko-SamuraiShips.jpg|300px|right|thumb|'''ShЕҚni Tsunesuke''' and other samurai ships during the Mongol invasion.
{{small|гҖҺ{{ill|MЕҚko ShЕ«rai Ekotoba|ja|и’ҷеҸӨиҘІжқҘзөөи©һ}}гҖҸеҫҢе·»гғ»зөө15гғ»з¬¬19зҙҷ}} Despite this tragedy, he continued to serve as a {{ill|Chinzei BugyЕҚ|ja|йҺ®иҘҝеҘүиЎҢ}} with {{ill|ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|ja|еӨ§еҸӢй јжі°}}. In 1284, following the death of the eighth {{ill|Shikken|ja|еҹ·жЁ©}}, {{ill|HЕҚjЕҚ Tokimune|ja|еҢ—жқЎжҷӮе®—}}, Tsunesuke took Buddhist vows and adopted the Dharma name JЕҚe. During the {{ill|Shimozuki Disturbance|ja|йңңжңҲйЁ’еӢ•}} of 1285, he supported {{ill|Taira no Yoritsuna|ja|е№ій јз¶ұ}} and defeated his brother {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ}}, aligning with the ruling {{ill|Tokuso family|ja|еҫ—е®—е®¶}}. This led to a weakening of the ShЕҚni clanвҖҷs influence, as they lost control of several provinces. In 1286, Tsunesuke was appointed to the {{ill|Chinzei Dangi-sho|ja|йҺ®иҘҝи«Үиӯ°жүҖ}} alongside {{ill|ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|ja|еӨ§еҸӢй јжі°}}, {{ill|Utsunomiya Michifusa|ja|е®ҮйғҪе®®йҖҡжҲҝ}}, and {{ill|Shibuya Shigekuni|ja|жёӢи°·йҮҚйғ·}}. He passed away in 1292. His eldest son {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ}} having died in battle, Tsunesuke was succeeded by {{ill|ShЕҚni Moritsune|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣзөҢ}}. His younger sons {{ill|ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷӮзөҢ}} and {{ill|ShЕҚni Morishige|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣж°Ҹ}} later founded the {{ill|Shiga clan|ja|еҝ—иіҖж°Ҹ}} and {{ill|Hirai clan|ja|е№ідә•ж°Ҹ}}, respectively. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *[[Motohisa Yasuda]], ed. гҖҺйҺҢеҖүгғ»е®Өз”әдәәеҗҚдәӢе…ё гӮігғігғ‘гӮҜгғҲзүҲгҖҸпјҲ[[Shin Jinbutsu Oraisha]], 1990пјү *[[Hattori Hideo]] гҖҺи’ҷеҸӨиҘІжқҘгҖҸпјҲ[[Yamakawa Publishing]], 2014пјү *[[Kuroita Katsumi]], ed. гҖҺж–°иЁӮеў—иЈңеӣҪеҸІеӨ§зі»гғ»е°ҠеҚ‘еҲҶи„ү з¬¬2зҜҮгҖҸпјҲ[[Yoshikawa KЕҚbunkan]]пјү {{ShЕҚni clan heads}} [[Category:ShЕҚni clan]] [[Category:Kamakura shogunate retainers]] [[Category:People of the Mongol invasions of Japan]] [[Category:1229 births]] [[Category:1292 deaths]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:dd]] [[ja:е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ]]

{{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesuke|ja|е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ}} (е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ[[ja:е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ]]) [[Category:Https://ko.wikipedia.org/wiki/]][[ko:%EC%87%BC%EB%8B%88 %EC%93%B0%EB%84%A4%EC%8A%A4%EC%BC%80]] {{Infobox samurai | name = ShЕҚni Tsunesuke (е°‘ејҗ зөҢиіҮ) | image = | imagesize = | caption = | era = Mid-[[Kamakura period]] | birth_date = [[Kanki]] 1 (1229) вҖ» | death_date = [[ShЕҚЕҚ]] 5, [[8th month]], [[2nd day (lunar calendar)|2nd day]] (September 14, 1292) вҖ» | posthumous_name = JЕҚe (жө„жҒө; Dharma name)гҖҺе°ҠеҚ‘еҲҶи„үгҖҸз¬¬2зҜҮгғ»P.391гҖӮ | alias = Muto Tsunesuke | rank = | allegiance = [[Kamakura shogunate]] | lord = [[Prince Koreyasu]] | clan = {{ill|ShЕҚni clan|ja|е°‘ејҗж°Ҹ}} | parents = Father: {{ill|ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮиғҪ}} | siblings = Tsunesuke, {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ}}жңҚйғЁгҖҒ2014е№ҙгҖҒP.416гҖӮ, {{ill|ShЕҚni Morisue|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣиіҮ}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Munetsune|ja|е°‘ејҗе®—иіҮ}} (existence debated)гҖҺжұҺжө·е°ҸйҢІгҖҸгҒ«гҖҢеӨӘе®°и—ӨеҺҹе°‘еҚҝејҹе®—иіҮгҖҚгҒЁиЁҳгҒ•гӮҢгӮӢгҒҢгҖҒж—Ҙжң¬еҒҙеҸІж–ҷгҒ«гҒҜи©ІеҪ“иҖ…гҒҢгҒҠгӮүгҒҡгҖҒи«ёиӘ¬гҒӮгӮӢгҖӮе·қи¶Ҡжі°еҚҡгҖҢжұҺжө·е°ҸйҢІгҒ®ејҳе®үгҒ®еҪ№иЁҳдәӢгҒ«гҒӨгҒ„гҒҰгҖҚгҖҺи»ҚдәӢеҸІеӯҰгҖҸз¬¬11е·»з¬¬1еҸ·гҖҒ1975е№ҙ6жңҲгҖҒ26-34й ҒгҖӮ | spouse = | children = {{ill|ShЕҚni Moritsune|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣзөҢ}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷӮзөҢ}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Morishige|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣж°Ҹ}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Sadatsune|ja|е°‘ејҗиІһиіҮ}} | notable = вҖ» Birth and death dates are debated. }} '''ShЕҚni Tsunesuke''' (е°‘ејҗ зөҢиіҮ) was a {{ill|samurai}} and retainer of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate}} during the mid-{{ill|Kamakura period}}. He was the son of {{ill|ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮиғҪ}} and the third head of the {{ill|ShЕҚni clan|ja|е°‘ејҗж°Ҹ}}. == Overview == The character "зөҢ" in Tsunesuke's name was likely granted as a favor by the fourth {{ill|Shikken|ja|еҹ·жЁ©}}, {{ill|HЕҚjЕҚ Tokiyori|ja|еҢ—жқЎзөҢжҷӮ}}, or by {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoritsune|ja|и—ӨеҺҹй јзөҢ}}, the fourth {{ill|Seii Taishogun|ja|еҫҒеӨ·еӨ§е°Ҷи»Қ}}, who gave the same character to Tokiyori. This suggests Tsunesuke underwent his coming-of-age ceremony (genpuku) between 1245 and 1246. There are varying theories regarding Tsunesuke's birth and death years. One theory suggests he was born in 1229 and died in 1292, while another suggests 1226 to 1289.[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B0%91%E5%BC%90%E7%B5%8C%E8%B3%87-1082512 гӮігғҲгғҗгғігӮҜ_е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ] However, as genpuku typically occurred in oneвҖҷs teens, the 1229 birth year is widely considered accurate. Tsunesuke shared responsibilities with his father in governing northern Kyushu and played a crucial role during the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|е…ғеҜҮ}}. He oversaw the fortification of defenses, such as the {{ill|Ishizumi walls|ja|е…ғеҜҮйҳІеЎҒ}}, and coordinated efforts against the Yuan forces. His earliest documented activity appears in a record from 1269.жңҚйғЁгҖҒ2014е№ҙгҖҒP.416гҖӮе…ёжӢ гҒҜгҖҺжӯҰйӣ„зҘһзӨҫж–ҮжӣёгҖҸпјҲгҖҺйҺҢеҖүйҒәж–ҮгҖҸз¬¬14е·»гғ»10503еҸ·ж–ҮжӣёпјүгҖӮ In 1274, during the {{ill|Battle of Bun'ei|ja|ж–Үж°ёгҒ®еҪ№}}, Tsunesuke's brother {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ}} led the Japanese forces. Tsunesuke's role during this campaign is unclear. By 1275, he inherited the familyвҖҷs headship from his father. In 1281, during the {{ill|Battle of KЕҚan|ja|ејҳе®үгҒ®еҪ№}}, Tsunesuke fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki|ja|еЈұеІҗеі¶гҒ®жҲҰгҒ„}}, where he expelled the Yuan forces but lost his son, {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ}}.
[[File:Mooko-SamuraiShips.jpg|300px|right|thumb|'''ShЕҚni Tsunesuke''' and other samurai ships during the Mongol invasion.
{{small|гҖҺ{{ill|MЕҚko ShЕ«rai Ekotoba|ja|и’ҷеҸӨиҘІжқҘзөөи©һ}}гҖҸеҫҢе·»гғ»зөө15гғ»з¬¬19зҙҷ}} Despite this tragedy, he continued to serve as a {{ill|Chinzei BugyЕҚ|ja|йҺ®иҘҝеҘүиЎҢ}} with {{ill|ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|ja|еӨ§еҸӢй јжі°}}. In 1284, following the death of the eighth {{ill|Shikken|ja|еҹ·жЁ©}}, {{ill|HЕҚjЕҚ Tokimune|ja|еҢ—жқЎжҷӮе®—}}, Tsunesuke took Buddhist vows and adopted the Dharma name JЕҚe. During the {{ill|Shimozuki Disturbance|ja|йңңжңҲйЁ’еӢ•}} of 1285, he supported {{ill|Taira no Yoritsuna|ja|е№ій јз¶ұ}} and defeated his brother {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ}}, aligning with the ruling {{ill|Tokuso family|ja|еҫ—е®—е®¶}}. This led to a weakening of the ShЕҚni clanвҖҷs influence, as they lost control of several provinces. In 1286, Tsunesuke was appointed to the {{ill|Chinzei Dangi-sho|ja|йҺ®иҘҝи«Үиӯ°жүҖ}} alongside {{ill|ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|ja|еӨ§еҸӢй јжі°}}, {{ill|Utsunomiya Michifusa|ja|е®ҮйғҪе®®йҖҡжҲҝ}}, and {{ill|Shibuya Shigekuni|ja|жёӢи°·йҮҚйғ·}}. He passed away in 1292. His eldest son {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ}} having died in battle, Tsunesuke was succeeded by {{ill|ShЕҚni Moritsune|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣзөҢ}}. His younger sons {{ill|ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷӮзөҢ}} and {{ill|ShЕҚni Morishige|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣж°Ҹ}} later founded the {{ill|Shiga clan|ja|еҝ—иіҖж°Ҹ}} and {{ill|Hirai clan|ja|е№ідә•ж°Ҹ}}, respectively. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *[[Motohisa Yasuda]], ed. гҖҺйҺҢеҖүгғ»е®Өз”әдәәеҗҚдәӢе…ё гӮігғігғ‘гӮҜгғҲзүҲгҖҸпјҲ[[Shin Jinbutsu Oraisha]], 1990пјү *[[Hattori Hideo]] гҖҺи’ҷеҸӨиҘІжқҘгҖҸпјҲ[[Yamakawa Publishing]], 2014пјү *[[Kuroita Katsumi]], ed. гҖҺж–°иЁӮеў—иЈңеӣҪеҸІеӨ§зі»гғ»е°ҠеҚ‘еҲҶи„ү з¬¬2зҜҮгҖҸпјҲ[[Yoshikawa KЕҚbunkan]]пјү {{ShЕҚni clan heads}} [[Category:ShЕҚni clan]] [[Category:Kamakura shogunate retainers]] [[Category:People of the Mongol invasions of Japan]] [[Category:1229 births]] [[Category:1292 deaths]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:dd]] [[ja:е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ]]

revision.text:
{{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesuke|ja|е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ}} (е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ[[ja:е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ]]) [[Category:Https://ko.wikipedia.org/wiki/]][[ko:%EC%87%BC%EB%8B%88 %EC%93%B0%EB%84%A4%EC%8A%A4%EC%BC%80]] {{Infobox samurai | name = ShЕҚni Tsunesuke (е°‘ејҗ зөҢиіҮ) | image = | imagesize = | caption = | era = Mid-[[Kamakura period]] | birth_date = [[Kanki]] 1 (1229) вҖ» | death_date = [[ShЕҚЕҚ]] 5, [[8th month]], [[2nd day (lunar calendar)|2nd day]] (September 14, 1292) вҖ» | posthumous_name = JЕҚe (жө„жҒө; Dharma name)гҖҺе°ҠеҚ‘еҲҶи„үгҖҸз¬¬2зҜҮгғ»P.391гҖӮ | alias = Muto Tsunesuke | rank = | allegiance = [[Kamakura shogunate]] | lord = [[Prince Koreyasu]] | clan = {{ill|ShЕҚni clan|ja|е°‘ејҗж°Ҹ}} | parents = Father: {{ill|ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮиғҪ}} | siblings = Tsunesuke, {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ}}жңҚйғЁгҖҒ2014е№ҙгҖҒP.416гҖӮ, {{ill|ShЕҚni Morisue|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣиіҮ}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Munetsune|ja|е°‘ејҗе®—иіҮ}} (existence debated)гҖҺжұҺжө·е°ҸйҢІгҖҸгҒ«гҖҢеӨӘе®°и—ӨеҺҹе°‘еҚҝејҹе®—иіҮгҖҚгҒЁиЁҳгҒ•гӮҢгӮӢгҒҢгҖҒж—Ҙжң¬еҒҙеҸІж–ҷгҒ«гҒҜи©ІеҪ“иҖ…гҒҢгҒҠгӮүгҒҡгҖҒи«ёиӘ¬гҒӮгӮӢгҖӮе·қи¶Ҡжі°еҚҡгҖҢжұҺжө·е°ҸйҢІгҒ®ејҳе®үгҒ®еҪ№иЁҳдәӢгҒ«гҒӨгҒ„гҒҰгҖҚгҖҺи»ҚдәӢеҸІеӯҰгҖҸз¬¬11е·»з¬¬1еҸ·гҖҒ1975е№ҙ6жңҲгҖҒ26-34й ҒгҖӮ | spouse = | children = {{ill|ShЕҚni Moritsune|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣзөҢ}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷӮзөҢ}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Morishige|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣж°Ҹ}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Sadatsune|ja|е°‘ејҗиІһиіҮ}} | notable = вҖ» Birth and death dates are debated. }} '''ShЕҚni Tsunesuke''' (е°‘ејҗ зөҢиіҮ) was a {{ill|samurai}} and retainer of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate}} during the mid-{{ill|Kamakura period}}. He was the son of {{ill|ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮиғҪ}} and the third head of the {{ill|ShЕҚni clan|ja|е°‘ејҗж°Ҹ}}. == Overview == The character "зөҢ" in Tsunesuke's name was likely granted as a favor by the fourth {{ill|Shikken|ja|еҹ·жЁ©}}, {{ill|HЕҚjЕҚ Tokiyori|ja|еҢ—жқЎзөҢжҷӮ}}, or by {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoritsune|ja|и—ӨеҺҹй јзөҢ}}, the fourth {{ill|Seii Taishogun|ja|еҫҒеӨ·еӨ§е°Ҷи»Қ}}, who gave the same character to Tokiyori. This suggests Tsunesuke underwent his coming-of-age ceremony (genpuku) between 1245 and 1246. There are varying theories regarding Tsunesuke's birth and death years. One theory suggests he was born in 1229 and died in 1292, while another suggests 1226 to 1289.[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B0%91%E5%BC%90%E7%B5%8C%E8%B3%87-1082512 гӮігғҲгғҗгғігӮҜ_е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ] However, as genpuku typically occurred in oneвҖҷs teens, the 1229 birth year is widely considered accurate. Tsunesuke shared responsibilities with his father in governing northern Kyushu and played a crucial role during the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|е…ғеҜҮ}}. He oversaw the fortification of defenses, such as the {{ill|Ishizumi walls|ja|е…ғеҜҮйҳІеЎҒ}}, and coordinated efforts against the Yuan forces. His earliest documented activity appears in a record from 1269.жңҚйғЁгҖҒ2014е№ҙгҖҒP.416гҖӮе…ёжӢ гҒҜгҖҺжӯҰйӣ„зҘһзӨҫж–ҮжӣёгҖҸпјҲгҖҺйҺҢеҖүйҒәж–ҮгҖҸз¬¬14е·»гғ»10503еҸ·ж–ҮжӣёпјүгҖӮ In 1274, during the {{ill|Battle of Bun'ei|ja|ж–Үж°ёгҒ®еҪ№}}, Tsunesuke's brother {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ}} led the Japanese forces. Tsunesuke's role during this campaign is unclear. By 1275, he inherited the familyвҖҷs headship from his father. In 1281, during the {{ill|Battle of KЕҚan|ja|ејҳе®үгҒ®еҪ№}}, Tsunesuke fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki|ja|еЈұеІҗеі¶гҒ®жҲҰгҒ„}}, where he expelled the Yuan forces but lost his son, {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ}}.
[[File:Mooko-SamuraiShips.jpg|300px|right|thumb|'''ShЕҚni Tsunesuke''' and other samurai ships during the Mongol invasion.
{{small|гҖҺ{{ill|MЕҚko ShЕ«rai Ekotoba|ja|и’ҷеҸӨиҘІжқҘзөөи©һ}}гҖҸеҫҢе·»гғ»зөө15гғ»з¬¬19зҙҷ}} Despite this tragedy, he continued to serve as a {{ill|Chinzei BugyЕҚ|ja|йҺ®иҘҝеҘүиЎҢ}} with {{ill|ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|ja|еӨ§еҸӢй јжі°}}. In 1284, following the death of the eighth {{ill|Shikken|ja|еҹ·жЁ©}}, {{ill|HЕҚjЕҚ Tokimune|ja|еҢ—жқЎжҷӮе®—}}, Tsunesuke took Buddhist vows and adopted the Dharma name JЕҚe. During the {{ill|Shimozuki Disturbance|ja|йңңжңҲйЁ’еӢ•}} of 1285, he supported {{ill|Taira no Yoritsuna|ja|е№ій јз¶ұ}} and defeated his brother {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ}}, aligning with the ruling {{ill|Tokuso family|ja|еҫ—е®—е®¶}}. This led to a weakening of the ShЕҚni clanвҖҷs influence, as they lost control of several provinces. In 1286, Tsunesuke was appointed to the {{ill|Chinzei Dangi-sho|ja|йҺ®иҘҝи«Үиӯ°жүҖ}} alongside {{ill|ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|ja|еӨ§еҸӢй јжі°}}, {{ill|Utsunomiya Michifusa|ja|е®ҮйғҪе®®йҖҡжҲҝ}}, and {{ill|Shibuya Shigekuni|ja|жёӢи°·йҮҚйғ·}}. He passed away in 1292. His eldest son {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ}} having died in battle, Tsunesuke was succeeded by {{ill|ShЕҚni Moritsune|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣзөҢ}}. His younger sons {{ill|ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷӮзөҢ}} and {{ill|ShЕҚni Morishige|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣж°Ҹ}} later founded the {{ill|Shiga clan|ja|еҝ—иіҖж°Ҹ}} and {{ill|Hirai clan|ja|е№ідә•ж°Ҹ}}, respectively. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *[[Motohisa Yasuda]], ed. гҖҺйҺҢеҖүгғ»е®Өз”әдәәеҗҚдәӢе…ё гӮігғігғ‘гӮҜгғҲзүҲгҖҸпјҲ[[Shin Jinbutsu Oraisha]], 1990пјү *[[Hattori Hideo]] гҖҺи’ҷеҸӨиҘІжқҘгҖҸпјҲ[[Yamakawa Publishing]], 2014пјү *[[Kuroita Katsumi]], ed. гҖҺж–°иЁӮеў—иЈңеӣҪеҸІеӨ§зі»гғ»е°ҠеҚ‘еҲҶи„ү з¬¬2зҜҮгҖҸпјҲ[[Yoshikawa KЕҚbunkan]]пјү {{ShЕҚni clan heads}} [[Category:ShЕҚni clan]] [[Category:Kamakura shogunate retainers]] [[Category:People of the Mongol invasions of Japan]] [[Category:1229 births]] [[Category:1292 deaths]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:dd]] [[ja:е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ]]
