{{small|гҖҺ[[и’ҷеҸӨиҘІжқҘзөөи©һ]]гҖҸеҫҢе·»гғ»зөө15гғ»з¬¬19зҙҷ}}]] гҒқгҒ®еҫҢгӮӮ[[еӨ§еҸӢй јжі°]]гҒЁе…ұгҒ«[[йҺ®иҘҝеҘүиЎҢ]]гҒ®дёҖдәәгҒЁгҒ—гҒҰгҖҒд№қе·һгҒ®и»Қж”ҝгҒ«гҒӮгҒҹгҒЈгҒҹгҖӮ ејҳе®ү7е№ҙпјҲ[[1284е№ҙ]]пјүгҖҒ8д»Ј[[еҹ·жЁ©]]гғ»[[еҢ—жқЎжҷӮе®—]]гҒҢжӯ»еҺ»гҒ—гҒҹгҒ®гӮ’еҘ‘ж©ҹгҒ«еҮәе®¶гҒ—гҖҒ'''жө„жҒө'''гҒЁеҸ·гҒ—гҒҹгҖӮзҝҢе№ҙгҒ®[[йңңжңҲйЁ’еӢ•]]гҒ§гҒҜ[[е№ій јз¶ұ]]еҒҙгҒ«дёҺгҒ—гҒҰгҖҒ[[е®үйҒ”жі°зӣӣ]]еҒҙгҒ«дёҺгҒ—гҒҹејҹгҒ®жҷҜиіҮгҒЁжі°зӣӣгҒ®еӯҗгғ»[[е®үйҒ”зӣӣе®—]]гӮ’иЁҺгҒЎеҸ–гҒЈгҒҹпјҲ[[еІ©й–ҖеҗҲжҲҰ]]пјүгҖӮгҒ“гҒ®жҲҰгҒ„гҒ®зөҗжһңгҖҒ[[еҢ—жқЎж°Ҹ|еҢ—жқЎ]][[еҫ—е®—]]е®¶гҒ®йҺ®иҘҝж”Ҝй…ҚгҒҢеј·еҢ–гҒ•гӮҢгҒҰгҖҒе°‘ејҗж°ҸгҒ®еӢўеҠӣгҒҜеүҠгӮүгӮҢгҖҒзӯ‘еҫҢгғ»иұҠеүҚгғ»иӮҘеүҚгғ»иӮҘеҫҢгҒ®е®Ҳиӯ·иҒ·гӮ’еӨұгҒЈгҒҹгҖӮ ејҳе®ү9е№ҙпјҲ[[1286е№ҙ]]пјүгҖҒ[[йҺ®иҘҝи«Үиӯ°жүҖ]]гҒ®еҘүиЎҢгҒ«[[еӨ§еҸӢй јжі°]]гҖҒ[[е®ҮйғҪе®®йҖҡжҲҝ]]гҖҒ[[жёӢи°·йҮҚйғ·]]гҒЁе…ұгҒ«д»»гҒҳгӮүгӮҢгӮӢгҖӮжӯЈеҝң5е№ҙпјҲ1292е№ҙпјүгҒ«жӯ»еҺ»гҖӮ й•·з”·гғ»иіҮжҷӮгҒҜејҳе®үгҒ®еҪ№гҒ§жҲҰжӯ»гҒ—гҒҹгҒҹгӮҒгҖҒе®¶зқЈгҒҜ[[е°‘ејҗзӣӣзөҢ|зӣӣзөҢ]]гҒҢз¶ҷгҒ„гҒ гҖӮгҒҫгҒҹгҖҒзӣӣзөҢгҒ®ејҹгғ»[[е°‘ејҗжҷӮзөҢ|жҷӮзөҢ]]гӮ„[[е°‘ејҗзӣӣж°Ҹ|зӣӣж°Ҹ]]гҒҜгҖҒгҒқгҒ®еӯҗеӯ«гҒҢгҒқгӮҢгҒһгӮҢ[[еҝ—иіҖж°Ҹ]]гҖҒ[[е№ідә•ж°Ҹ]]гҒЁгҒӘгӮӢгҖӮ == и„ҡжіЁ == {{и„ҡжіЁгғҳгғ«гғ—}} {{Reflist}} == еҸӮиҖғж–ҮзҢ® == *[[е®үз”°е…ғд№…]]з·Ё гҖҺйҺҢеҖүгғ»е®Өз”әдәәеҗҚдәӢе…ё гӮігғігғ‘гӮҜгғҲзүҲгҖҸпјҲ[[ж–°дәәзү©еҫҖжқҘзӨҫ]]гҖҒ1990е№ҙпјү
гҖҖгҖҖP.607пҪһ608гҖҢжӯҰи—ӨзөҢиіҮгҖҚгҒ®й …пјҲеҹ·зӯҶпјҡз”°жқ‘жҶІзҫҺпјү *[[жңҚйғЁиӢұйӣ„]]гҖҺи’ҷеҸӨиҘІжқҘгҖҸпјҲ[[еұұе·қеҮәзүҲзӨҫ]]гҖҒ2014е№ҙпјү *[[й»’жқҝеӢқзҫҺ]]гғ»[[еӣҪеҸІеӨ§зі»]]з·Ёдҝ®дјҡ з·ЁгҖҺ[[еӣҪеҸІеӨ§зі»|ж–°иЁӮеў—иЈңеӣҪеҸІеӨ§зі»]]гғ»[[е°ҠеҚ‘еҲҶи„ү]] з¬¬2зҜҮгҖҸпјҲ[[еҗүе·қејҳж–ҮйӨЁ]]пјүгҖҖ {{е°‘ејҗж°ҸеҪ“дё»}} {{DEFAULTSORT:гҒ—гӮҲгҒҶгҒ« гҒӨгҒӯгҒҷгҒ‘}} [[Category:е°‘ејҗж°Ҹ|гҒӨгҒӯгҒҷгҒ‘]] [[Category:йҺҢеҖүе№•еәңеҫЎе®¶дәә]] [[Category:е…ғеҜҮгҒ®дәәзү©]] [[Category:1229е№ҙз”ҹ]] [[Category:1292е№ҙжІЎ]]

{{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesuke|ja|е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ) {{Infobox samurai | name = ShЕҚni Tsunesuke (е°‘ејҗ зөҢиіҮ) | image = | imagesize = | caption = | era = Mid-[[Kamakura period]] | birth_date = [[Kanki]] 1 (1229) вҖ» | death_date = [[ShЕҚЕҚ]] 5, [[8th month]], [[2nd day (lunar calendar)|2nd day]] (September 14, 1292) вҖ» | posthumous_name = JЕҚe (жө„жҒө; Dharma name)гҖҺе°ҠеҚ‘еҲҶи„үгҖҸз¬¬2зҜҮгғ»P.391гҖӮ | alias = Muto Tsunesuke | Rank = | allegiance = [[Kamakura shogunate]] | lord = [[Prince Koreyasu]] | clan = {{ill|ShЕҚni clan|ja|е°‘ејҗж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | parents = Father: {{ill|ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮиғҪ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | siblings = Tsunesuke, {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}жңҚйғЁгҖҒ2014th year гҖҒP.416гҖӮ, {{ill|ShЕҚni Morisue|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Munetsune|ja|е°‘ејҗе®—иіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (existence debated)гҖҺжұҺжө·е°ҸйҢІгҖҸгҒ«гҖҢеӨӘе®°и—ӨеҺҹе°‘еҚҝејҹе®—иіҮгҖҚгҒЁиЁҳгҒ•гӮҢгӮӢгҒҢгҖҒж—Ҙжң¬еҒҙеҸІж–ҷгҒ«гҒҜи©ІеҪ“иҖ…гҒҢгҒҠгӮүгҒҡгҖҒи«ёиӘ¬гҒӮгӮӢгҖӮе·қи¶Ҡжі°еҚҡгҖҢжұҺжө·е°ҸйҢІгҒ®ејҳе®үгҒ®еҪ№иЁҳдәӢгҒ«гҒӨгҒ„гҒҰгҖҚгҖҺи»ҚдәӢеҸІеӯҰгҖҸз¬¬ppе·»з¬¬1еҸ·гҖҒ1975th year 6жңҲгҖҒ26-34й ҒгҖӮ | spouse = | children = {{ill|ShЕҚni Moritsune|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣзөҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷӮзөҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Morishige|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Sadatsune|ja|е°‘ејҗиІһиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | notable = вҖ» Birth and death dates are debated. }} '''ShЕҚni Tsunesuke''' (е°‘ејҗ зөҢиіҮ) was a {{ill|samurai}} and retainer of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate}} during the mid-{{ill|Kamakura period|qq=draft}}. He was the son of {{ill|ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮиғҪ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the third head of the {{ill|ShЕҚni clan|ja|е°‘ејҗж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Overview == The character "зөҢ" in Tsunesuke's name was likely granted as a favor by the fourth {{ill|Shikken|ja|Shikken|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|HЕҚjЕҚ Tokiyori|ja|еҢ—жқЎзөҢжҷӮ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, or by {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoritsune|ja|и—ӨеҺҹй јзөҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the fourth {{ill|Seii Taishogun|ja|еҫҒеӨ·еӨ§е°Ҷи»Қ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who gave the same character to Tokiyori. This suggests Tsunesuke underwent his coming-of-age ceremony (genpuku) between 1245 and 1246. There are varying theories regarding Tsunesuke's birth and death years. One theory suggests he was born in 1229 and died in 1292, while another suggests 1226 to 1289.[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B0%91%E5%BC%90%E7%B5%8C%E8%B3%87-1082512 гӮігғҲгғҗгғігӮҜ_е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ] However, as genpuku typically occurred in oneвҖҷs teens, the 1229 birth year is widely considered accurate. Tsunesuke shared responsibilities with his father in governing northern Kyushu and played a crucial role during the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|е…ғеҜҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. He oversaw the fortification of defenses, such as the {{ill|Ishizumi walls|ja|е…ғеҜҮйҳІеЎҒ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and coordinated efforts against the Yuan forces. His earliest documented activity appears in a record from 1269.жңҚйғЁгҖҒ2014th year гҖҒP.416гҖӮе…ёжӢ гҒҜгҖҺжӯҰйӣ„зҘһзӨҫж–ҮжӣёгҖҸпјҲгҖҺйҺҢеҖүйҒәж–ҮгҖҸз¬¬14е·»гғ»10503еҸ·ж–ҮжӣёпјүгҖӮ In 1274, during the {{ill|Battle of Bun'ei|ja|ж–Үж°ёгҒ®еҪ№|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tsunesuke's brother {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} led the Japanese forces. Tsunesuke's role during this campaign is unclear. By 1275, he inherited the familyвҖҷs headship from his father. In 1281, during the {{ill|Battle of KЕҚan|ja|ејҳе®үгҒ®еҪ№|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tsunesuke fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki|ja|еЈұеІҗеі¶гҒ®жҲҰгҒ„|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, where he expelled the Yuan forces but lost his son, {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
[[File:Mooko-SamuraiShips.jpg|300px|right|thumb|'''ShЕҚni Tsunesuke''' and other samurai ships during the Mongol invasion.
{{small|гҖҺ{{ill|MЕҚko ShЕ«rai Ekotoba|ja|и’ҷеҸӨиҘІжқҘзөөи©һ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}гҖҸеҫҢе·»гғ»зөө15гғ»з¬¬19зҙҷ}} Despite this tragedy, he continued to serve as a {{ill|Chinzei BugyЕҚ|ja|йҺ®иҘҝеҘүиЎҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} with {{ill|ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|ja|еӨ§еҸӢй јжі°|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In 1284, following the death of the eighth {{ill|Shikken|ja|Shikken|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|HЕҚjЕҚ Tokimune|ja|еҢ—жқЎжҷӮе®—|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tsunesuke took Buddhist vows and adopted the Dharma name JЕҚe. During the {{ill|Shimozuki Disturbance|ja|йңңжңҲйЁ’еӢ•|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of 1285, he supported {{ill|Taira no Yoritsuna|ja|е№ій јз¶ұ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and defeated his brother {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, aligning with the ruling {{ill|Tokuso family|ja|еҫ—е®—е®¶|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. This led to a weakening of the ShЕҚni clanвҖҷs influence, as they lost control of several provinces. In 1286, Tsunesuke was appointed to the {{ill|Chinzei Dangi-sho|ja|йҺ®иҘҝи«Үиӯ°жүҖ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} alongside {{ill|ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|ja|еӨ§еҸӢй јжі°|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Utsunomiya Michifusa|ja|е®ҮйғҪе®®йҖҡжҲҝ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Shibuya Shigekuni|ja|жёӢи°·йҮҚйғ·|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. He passed away in 1292. His eldest son {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} having died in battle, Tsunesuke was succeeded by {{ill|ShЕҚni Moritsune|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣзөҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. His younger sons {{ill|ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷӮзөҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|ShЕҚni Morishige|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} later founded the {{ill|Shiga clan|ja|еҝ—иіҖж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Hirai clan|ja|е№ідә•ж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, respectively. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *[[Motohisa Yasuda]], ed. гҖҺйҺҢеҖүгғ»е®Өз”әдәәеҗҚдәӢе…ё гӮігғігғ‘гӮҜгғҲзүҲгҖҸпјҲ[[Shin Jinbutsu Oraisha]], 1990пјү *[[Hattori Hideo]] гҖҺи’ҷеҸӨиҘІжқҘгҖҸпјҲ[[Yamakawa Publishing]], 2014пјү *[[Kuroita Katsumi]], ed. гҖҺж–°иЁӮеў—иЈңеӣҪеҸІеӨ§зі»гғ»е°ҠеҚ‘еҲҶи„ү з¬¬2зҜҮгҖҸпјҲ[[Yoshikawa KЕҚbunkan]]пјү {{ShЕҚni clan heads}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:pp to pp]] [[Category:ShЕҚni clan]] [[Category:Kamakura shogunate retainers]] [[Category:People of the Mongol invasions of Japan]] [[Category:1229 births]] [[Category:1292 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pp]] [[Category:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ]] [[ko:%EC%87%BC%EB%8B%88 %EC%93%B0%EB%84%A4%EC%8A%A4%EC%BC%80]] [[sn:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]] [[en:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]] {{DEFAULTSORT:ShЕҚni Kagesuke}} {{translated page|ja|е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ|version=101501413|comment=Imported full ja history}}

One theory suggests he was born in 1229 and died in 1292, while another suggests 1226 to 1289.[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B0%91%E5%BC%90%E7%B5%8C%E8%B3%87-1082512 гӮігғҲгғҗгғігӮҜ_е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ] However, as genpuku typically occurred in oneвҖҷs teens, the 1229 birth year is widely considered accurate. He oversaw the fortification of defenses, such as the {{ill|Ishizumi walls|ja|е…ғеҜҮйҳІеЎҒ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and coordinated efforts against the Yuan forces. His earliest documented activity appears in a record from 1269.жңҚйғЁгҖҒ2014th year гҖҒP.416гҖӮе…ёжӢ гҒҜгҖҺжӯҰйӣ„зҘһзӨҫж–ҮжӣёгҖҸпјҲгҖҺйҺҢеҖүйҒәж–ҮгҖҸз¬¬14е·»гғ»10503еҸ·ж–ҮжӣёпјүгҖӮ In 1274, during the {{ill|Battle of Bun'ei|ja|ж–Үж°ёгҒ®еҪ№|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tsunesuke's brother {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} led the Japanese forces. Tsunesuke's role during this campaign is unclear. By 1275, he inherited the familyвҖҷs headship from his father. In 1281, during the {{ill|Battle of KЕҚan|ja|ејҳе®үгҒ®еҪ№|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tsunesuke fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki|ja|еЈұеІҗеі¶гҒ®жҲҰгҒ„|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, where he expelled the Yuan forces but lost his son, {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
[[File:Mooko-SamuraiShips.jpg|300px|right|thumb|'''ShЕҚni Tsunesuke''' and other samurai ships during the Mongol invasion.
{{small|гҖҺ{{ill|MЕҚko ShЕ«rai Ekotoba|ja|и’ҷеҸӨиҘІжқҘзөөи©һ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}гҖҸеҫҢе·»гғ»зөө15гғ»з¬¬19зҙҷ}} Despite this tragedy, he continued to serve as a {{ill|Chinzei BugyЕҚ|ja|йҺ®иҘҝеҘүиЎҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} with {{ill|ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|ja|еӨ§еҸӢй јжі°|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In 1284, following the death of the eighth {{ill|Shikken|ja|Shikken|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|HЕҚjЕҚ Tokimune|ja|еҢ—жқЎжҷӮе®—|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tsunesuke took Buddhist vows and adopted the Dharma name JЕҚe. During the {{ill|Shimozuki Disturbance|ja|йңңжңҲйЁ’еӢ•|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of 1285, he supported {{ill|Taira no Yoritsuna|ja|е№ій јз¶ұ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and defeated his brother {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, aligning with the ruling {{ill|Tokuso family|ja|еҫ—е®—е®¶|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. This led to a weakening of the ShЕҚni clanвҖҷs influence, as they lost control of several provinces. In 1286, Tsunesuke was appointed to the {{ill|Chinzei Dangi-sho|ja|йҺ®иҘҝи«Үиӯ°жүҖ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} alongside {{ill|ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|ja|еӨ§еҸӢй јжі°|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Utsunomiya Michifusa|ja|е®ҮйғҪе®®йҖҡжҲҝ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Shibuya Shigekuni|ja|жёӢи°·йҮҚйғ·|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. He passed away in 1292. His eldest son {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} having died in battle, Tsunesuke was succeeded by {{ill|ShЕҚni Moritsune|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣзөҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. His younger sons {{ill|ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷӮзөҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|ShЕҚni Morishige|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} later founded the {{ill|Shiga clan|ja|еҝ—иіҖж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Hirai clan|ja|е№ідә•ж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, respectively. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *[[Motohisa Yasuda]], ed. гҖҺйҺҢеҖүгғ»е®Өз”әдәәеҗҚдәӢе…ё гӮігғігғ‘гӮҜгғҲзүҲгҖҸпјҲ[[Shin Jinbutsu Oraisha]], 1990пјү *[[Hattori Hideo]] гҖҺи’ҷеҸӨиҘІжқҘгҖҸпјҲ[[Yamakawa Publishing]], 2014пјү *[[Kuroita Katsumi]], ed. гҖҺж–°иЁӮеў—иЈңеӣҪеҸІеӨ§зі»гғ»е°ҠеҚ‘еҲҶи„ү з¬¬2зҜҮгҖҸпјҲ[[Yoshikawa KЕҚbunkan]]пјү {{ShЕҚni clan heads}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:ShЕҚni clan]] [[Category:Kamakura shogunate retainers]] [[Category:People of the Mongol invasions of Japan]] [[Category:1229 births]] [[Category:1292 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pp]] [[Category:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ]] [[ko:%EC%87%BC%EB%8B%88 %EC%93%B0%EB%84%A4%EC%8A%A4%EC%BC%80]] [[sn:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]] [[en:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]] {{DEFAULTSORT:ShЕҚni Kagesuke}} {{translated page|ja|е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ|version=101501413|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesuke|ja|е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ) {{Infobox samurai | name = ShЕҚni Tsunesuke (е°‘ејҗ зөҢиіҮ) | image = | imagesize = | caption = | era = Mid-[[Kamakura period]] | birth_date = [[Kanki]] 1 (1229) вҖ» | death_date = [[ShЕҚЕҚ]] 5, [[8th month]], [[2nd day (lunar calendar)|2nd day]] (September 14, 1292) вҖ» | posthumous_name = JЕҚe (жө„жҒө; Dharma name)гҖҺе°ҠеҚ‘еҲҶи„үгҖҸз¬¬2зҜҮгғ»P.391гҖӮ | alias = Muto Tsunesuke | Rank = | allegiance = [[Kamakura shogunate]] | lord = [[Prince Koreyasu]] | clan = {{ill|ShЕҚni clan|ja|е°‘ејҗж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | parents = Father: {{ill|ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮиғҪ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | siblings = Tsunesuke, {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}жңҚйғЁгҖҒ2014th year гҖҒP.416гҖӮ, {{ill|ShЕҚni Morisue|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Munetsune|ja|е°‘ејҗе®—иіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (existence debated)гҖҺжұҺжө·е°ҸйҢІгҖҸгҒ«гҖҢеӨӘе®°и—ӨеҺҹе°‘еҚҝејҹе®—иіҮгҖҚгҒЁиЁҳгҒ•гӮҢгӮӢгҒҢгҖҒж—Ҙжң¬еҒҙеҸІж–ҷгҒ«гҒҜи©ІеҪ“иҖ…гҒҢгҒҠгӮүгҒҡгҖҒи«ёиӘ¬гҒӮгӮӢгҖӮе·қи¶Ҡжі°еҚҡгҖҢжұҺжө·е°ҸйҢІгҒ®ејҳе®үгҒ®еҪ№иЁҳдәӢгҒ«гҒӨгҒ„гҒҰгҖҚгҖҺи»ҚдәӢеҸІеӯҰгҖҸз¬¬ppе·»з¬¬1еҸ·гҖҒ1975th year 6жңҲгҖҒ26-34й ҒгҖӮ | spouse = | children = {{ill|ShЕҚni Moritsune|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣзөҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷӮзөҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Morishige|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Sadatsune|ja|е°‘ејҗиІһиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | notable = вҖ» Birth and death dates are debated. }} '''ShЕҚni Tsunesuke''' (е°‘ејҗ зөҢиіҮ) was a {{ill|samurai}} and retainer of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate}} during the mid-{{ill|Kamakura period|qq=draft}}. He was the son of {{ill|ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮиғҪ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the third head of the {{ill|ShЕҚni clan|ja|е°‘ејҗж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Overview == The character "зөҢ" in Tsunesuke's name was likely granted as a favor by the fourth {{ill|Shikken|ja|Shikken|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|HЕҚjЕҚ Tokiyori|ja|еҢ—жқЎзөҢжҷӮ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, or by {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoritsune|ja|и—ӨеҺҹй јзөҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the fourth {{ill|Seii Taishogun|ja|еҫҒеӨ·еӨ§е°Ҷи»Қ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who gave the same character to Tokiyori. This suggests Tsunesuke underwent his coming-of-age ceremony (genpuku) between 1245 and 1246. There are varying theories regarding Tsunesuke's birth and death years. One theory suggests he was born in 1229 and died in 1292, while another suggests 1226 to 1289.[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B0%91%E5%BC%90%E7%B5%8C%E8%B3%87-1082512 гӮігғҲгғҗгғігӮҜ_е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ] However, as genpuku typically occurred in oneвҖҷs teens, the 1229 birth year is widely considered accurate. Tsunesuke shared responsibilities with his father in governing northern Kyushu and played a crucial role during the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|е…ғеҜҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. He oversaw the fortification of defenses, such as the {{ill|Ishizumi walls|ja|е…ғеҜҮйҳІеЎҒ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and coordinated efforts against the Yuan forces. His earliest documented activity appears in a record from 1269.жңҚйғЁгҖҒ2014th year гҖҒP.416гҖӮе…ёжӢ гҒҜгҖҺжӯҰйӣ„зҘһзӨҫж–ҮжӣёгҖҸпјҲгҖҺйҺҢеҖүйҒәж–ҮгҖҸз¬¬14е·»гғ»10503еҸ·ж–ҮжӣёпјүгҖӮ In 1274, during the {{ill|Battle of Bun'ei|ja|ж–Үж°ёгҒ®еҪ№|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tsunesuke's brother {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} led the Japanese forces. Tsunesuke's role during this campaign is unclear. By 1275, he inherited the familyвҖҷs headship from his father. In 1281, during the {{ill|Battle of KЕҚan|ja|ејҳе®үгҒ®еҪ№|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tsunesuke fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki|ja|еЈұеІҗеі¶гҒ®жҲҰгҒ„|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, where he expelled the Yuan forces but lost his son, {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.

In 1284, following the death of the eighth {{ill|Shikken|ja|Shikken|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|HЕҚjЕҚ Tokimune|ja|еҢ—жқЎжҷӮе®—|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tsunesuke took Buddhist vows and adopted the Dharma name JЕҚe. During the {{ill|Shimozuki Disturbance|ja|йңңжңҲйЁ’еӢ•|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of 1285, he supported {{ill|Taira no Yoritsuna|ja|е№ій јз¶ұ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and defeated his brother {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, aligning with the ruling {{ill|Tokuso family|ja|еҫ—е®—е®¶|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. This led to a weakening of the ShЕҚni clanвҖҷs influence, as they lost control of several provinces. In 1286, Tsunesuke was appointed to the {{ill|Chinzei Dangi-sho|ja|йҺ®иҘҝи«Үиӯ°жүҖ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} alongside {{ill|ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|ja|еӨ§еҸӢй јжі°|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Utsunomiya Michifusa|ja|е®ҮйғҪе®®йҖҡжҲҝ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Shibuya Shigekuni|ja|жёӢи°·йҮҚйғ·|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. He passed away in 1292. His eldest son {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} having died in battle, Tsunesuke was succeeded by {{ill|ShЕҚni Moritsune|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣзөҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. His younger sons {{ill|ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷӮзөҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|ShЕҚni Morishige|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} later founded the {{ill|Shiga clan|ja|еҝ—иіҖж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Hirai clan|ja|е№ідә•ж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, respectively. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *[[Motohisa Yasuda]], ed. гҖҺйҺҢеҖүгғ»е®Өз”әдәәеҗҚдәӢе…ё гӮігғігғ‘гӮҜгғҲзүҲгҖҸпјҲ[[Shin Jinbutsu Oraisha]], 1990пјү *[[Hattori Hideo]] гҖҺи’ҷеҸӨиҘІжқҘгҖҸпјҲ[[Yamakawa Publishing]], 2014пјү *[[Kuroita Katsumi]], ed. гҖҺж–°иЁӮеў—иЈңеӣҪеҸІеӨ§зі»гғ»е°ҠеҚ‘еҲҶи„ү з¬¬2зҜҮгҖҸпјҲ[[Yoshikawa KЕҚbunkan]]пјү {{ShЕҚni clan heads}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ShЕҚni Kagesuke}} {{translated page|ja|е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ|version=101501413|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:pp to pp]] [[Category:ShЕҚni clan]] [[Category:Kamakura shogunate retainers]] [[Category:People of the Mongol invasions of Japan]] [[Category:1229 births]] [[Category:1292 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ]] [[ko:%EC%87%BC%EB%8B%88 %EC%93%B0%EB%84%A4%EC%8A%A4%EC%BC%80]] [[sn:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]] [[en:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]]

{{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesuke|ja|е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ) {{Infobox samurai | name = ShЕҚni Tsunesuke (е°‘ејҗ зөҢиіҮ) | image = | imagesize = | caption = | era = Mid-[[Kamakura period]] | birth_date = [[Kanki]] 1 (1229) вҖ» | death_date = [[ShЕҚЕҚ]] 5, [[8th month]], [[2nd day (lunar calendar)|2nd day]] (September 14, 1292) вҖ» | posthumous_name = JЕҚe (жө„жҒө; Dharma name)гҖҺе°ҠеҚ‘еҲҶи„үгҖҸз¬¬2зҜҮгғ»P.391гҖӮ | alias = Muto Tsunesuke | Rank = | allegiance = [[Kamakura shogunate]] | lord = [[Prince Koreyasu]] | clan = {{ill|ShЕҚni clan|ja|е°‘ејҗж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | parents = Father: {{ill|ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮиғҪ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | siblings = Tsunesuke, {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}жңҚйғЁгҖҒ2014th year гҖҒP.416гҖӮ, {{ill|ShЕҚni Morisue|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Munetsune|ja|е°‘ејҗе®—иіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (existence debated)гҖҺжұҺжө·е°ҸйҢІгҖҸгҒ«гҖҢеӨӘе®°и—ӨеҺҹе°‘еҚҝејҹе®—иіҮгҖҚгҒЁиЁҳгҒ•гӮҢгӮӢгҒҢгҖҒж—Ҙжң¬еҒҙеҸІж–ҷгҒ«гҒҜи©ІеҪ“иҖ…гҒҢгҒҠгӮүгҒҡгҖҒи«ёиӘ¬гҒӮгӮӢгҖӮе·қи¶Ҡжі°еҚҡгҖҢжұҺжө·е°ҸйҢІгҒ®ејҳе®үгҒ®еҪ№иЁҳдәӢгҒ«гҒӨгҒ„гҒҰгҖҚгҖҺи»ҚдәӢеҸІеӯҰгҖҸз¬¬ppе·»з¬¬1еҸ·гҖҒ1975th year 6жңҲгҖҒ26-34й ҒгҖӮ | spouse = | children = {{ill|ShЕҚni Moritsune|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣзөҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷӮзөҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Morishige|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Sadatsune|ja|е°‘ејҗиІһиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | notable = вҖ» Birth and death dates are debated. }} '''ShЕҚni Tsunesuke''' (е°‘ејҗ зөҢиіҮ) was a {{ill|samurai}} and retainer of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate}} during the mid-{{ill|Kamakura period|qq=draft}}. He was the son of {{ill|ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮиғҪ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the third head of the {{ill|ShЕҚni clan|ja|е°‘ејҗж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Overview == The character "зөҢ" in Tsunesuke's name was likely granted as a favor by the fourth {{ill|Shikken|ja|Shikken|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|HЕҚjЕҚ Tokiyori|ja|еҢ—жқЎзөҢжҷӮ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, or by {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoritsune|ja|и—ӨеҺҹй јзөҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the fourth {{ill|Seii Taishogun|ja|еҫҒеӨ·еӨ§е°Ҷи»Қ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who gave the same character to Tokiyori. This suggests Tsunesuke underwent his coming-of-age ceremony (genpuku) between 1245 and 1246. There are varying theories regarding Tsunesuke's birth and death years. One theory suggests he was born in 1229 and died in 1292, while another suggests 1226 to 1289.[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B0%91%E5%BC%90%E7%B5%8C%E8%B3%87-1082512 гӮігғҲгғҗгғігӮҜ_е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ] However, as genpuku typically occurred in oneвҖҷs teens, the 1229 birth year is widely considered accurate. Tsunesuke shared responsibilities with his father in governing northern Kyushu and played a crucial role during the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|е…ғеҜҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. He oversaw the fortification of defenses, such as the {{ill|Ishizumi walls|ja|е…ғеҜҮйҳІеЎҒ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and coordinated efforts against the Yuan forces. His earliest documented activity appears in a record from 1269.жңҚйғЁгҖҒ2014th year гҖҒP.416гҖӮе…ёжӢ гҒҜгҖҺжӯҰйӣ„зҘһзӨҫж–ҮжӣёгҖҸпјҲгҖҺйҺҢеҖүйҒәж–ҮгҖҸз¬¬14е·»гғ»10503еҸ·ж–ҮжӣёпјүгҖӮ In 1274, during the {{ill|Battle of Bun'ei|ja|ж–Үж°ёгҒ®еҪ№|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tsunesuke's brother {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} led the Japanese forces. Tsunesuke's role during this campaign is unclear. By 1275, he inherited the familyвҖҷs headship from his father. In 1281, during the {{ill|Battle of KЕҚan|ja|ејҳе®үгҒ®еҪ№|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tsunesuke fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki|ja|еЈұеІҗеі¶гҒ®жҲҰгҒ„|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, where he expelled the Yuan forces but lost his son, {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
[[File:Mooko-SamuraiShips.jpg|300px|right|thumb|'''ShЕҚni Tsunesuke''' and other samurai ships during the Mongol invasion.

In 1286, Tsunesuke was appointed to the {{ill|Chinzei Dangi-sho|ja|йҺ®иҘҝи«Үиӯ°жүҖ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} alongside {{ill|ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|ja|еӨ§еҸӢй јжі°|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Utsunomiya Michifusa|ja|е®ҮйғҪе®®йҖҡжҲҝ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Shibuya Shigekuni|ja|жёӢи°·йҮҚйғ·|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. He passed away in 1292. His younger sons {{ill|ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷӮзөҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|ShЕҚni Morishige|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} later founded the {{ill|Shiga clan|ja|еҝ—иіҖж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Hirai clan|ja|е№ідә•ж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, respectively. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *[[Motohisa Yasuda]], ed. гҖҺйҺҢеҖүгғ»е®Өз”әдәәеҗҚдәӢе…ё гӮігғігғ‘гӮҜгғҲзүҲгҖҸпјҲ[[Shin Jinbutsu Oraisha]], 1990пјү *[[Hattori Hideo]] гҖҺи’ҷеҸӨиҘІжқҘгҖҸпјҲ[[Yamakawa Publishing]], 2014пјү *[[Kuroita Katsumi]], ed. гҖҺж–°иЁӮеў—иЈңеӣҪеҸІеӨ§зі»гғ»е°ҠеҚ‘еҲҶи„ү з¬¬2зҜҮгҖҸпјҲ[[Yoshikawa KЕҚbunkan]]пјү {{ShЕҚni clan heads}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ShЕҚni Kagesuke}} {{translated page|ja|е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ|version=101501413|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:pp to pp]] [[Category:ShЕҚni clan]] [[Category:Kamakura shogunate retainers]] [[Category:People of the Mongol invasions of Japan]] [[Category:1229 births]] [[Category:1292 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ]] [[ko:%EC%87%BC%EB%8B%88 %EC%93%B0%EB%84%A4%EC%8A%A4%EC%BC%80]] [[sn:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]] [[en:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]]

His younger sons {{ill|ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷӮзөҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|ShЕҚni Morishige|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} later founded the {{ill|Shiga clan|ja|еҝ—иіҖж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Hirai clan|ja|е№ідә•ж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, respectively. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *[[Motohisa Yasuda]], ed. гҖҺйҺҢеҖүгғ»е®Өз”әдәәеҗҚдәӢе…ё гӮігғігғ‘гӮҜгғҲзүҲгҖҸпјҲ[[Shin Jinbutsu Oraisha]], 1990пјү *[[Hattori Hideo]] гҖҺи’ҷеҸӨиҘІжқҘгҖҸпјҲ[[Yamakawa Publishing]], 2014пјү *[[Kuroita Katsumi]], ed. гҖҺж–°иЁӮеў—иЈңеӣҪеҸІеӨ§зі»гғ»е°ҠеҚ‘еҲҶи„ү з¬¬2зҜҮгҖҸпјҲ[[Yoshikawa KЕҚbunkan]]пјү {{ShЕҚni clan heads}} {{translated page|ja|е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ|version=101501413|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:pp to pp]] [[Category:ShЕҚni clan]] [[Category:Kamakura shogunate retainers]] [[Category:People of the Mongol invasions of Japan]] [[Category:1229 births]] [[Category:1292 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ]] [[ko:%EC%87%BC%EB%8B%88 %EC%93%B0%EB%84%A4%EC%8A%A4%EC%BC%80]] [[sn:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]] [[en:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]]

{{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesuke|ja|е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ) {{Infobox samurai | name = ShЕҚni Tsunesuke (е°‘ејҗ зөҢиіҮ) | image = | imagesize = | caption = | era = Mid-[[Kamakura period]] | birth_date = [[Kanki]] 1 (1229) вҖ» | death_date = [[ShЕҚЕҚ]] 5, [[8th month]], [[2nd day (lunar calendar)|2nd day]] (September 14, 1292) вҖ» | posthumous_name = JЕҚe (жө„жҒө; Dharma name)гҖҺе°ҠеҚ‘еҲҶи„үгҖҸз¬¬2зҜҮгғ»P.391гҖӮ | alias = Muto Tsunesuke | Rank = | allegiance = [[Kamakura shogunate]] | lord = [[Prince Koreyasu]] | clan = {{ill|ShЕҚni clan|ja|е°‘ејҗж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | parents = Father: {{ill|ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮиғҪ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | siblings = Tsunesuke, {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}жңҚйғЁгҖҒ2014th year гҖҒP.416гҖӮ, {{ill|ShЕҚni Morisue|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Munetsune|ja|е°‘ејҗе®—иіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (existence debated)гҖҺжұҺжө·е°ҸйҢІгҖҸгҒ«гҖҢеӨӘе®°и—ӨеҺҹе°‘еҚҝејҹе®—иіҮгҖҚгҒЁиЁҳгҒ•гӮҢгӮӢгҒҢгҖҒж—Ҙжң¬еҒҙеҸІж–ҷгҒ«гҒҜи©ІеҪ“иҖ…гҒҢгҒҠгӮүгҒҡгҖҒи«ёиӘ¬гҒӮгӮӢгҖӮе·қи¶Ҡжі°еҚҡгҖҢжұҺжө·е°ҸйҢІгҒ®ејҳе®үгҒ®еҪ№иЁҳдәӢгҒ«гҒӨгҒ„гҒҰгҖҚгҖҺи»ҚдәӢеҸІеӯҰгҖҸз¬¬ppе·»з¬¬1еҸ·гҖҒ1975th year 6жңҲгҖҒ26-34й ҒгҖӮ | spouse = | children = {{ill|ShЕҚni Moritsune|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣзөҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷӮзөҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Morishige|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Sadatsune|ja|е°‘ејҗиІһиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | notable = вҖ» Birth and death dates are debated. }} '''ShЕҚni Tsunesuke''' (е°‘ејҗ зөҢиіҮ) was a {{ill|samurai}} and retainer of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate}} during the mid-{{ill|Kamakura period|qq=draft}}. He was the son of {{ill|ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮиғҪ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the third head of the {{ill|ShЕҚni clan|ja|е°‘ејҗж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Overview == The character "зөҢ" in Tsunesuke's name was likely granted as a favor by the fourth {{ill|Shikken|ja|Shikken|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|HЕҚjЕҚ Tokiyori|ja|еҢ—жқЎзөҢжҷӮ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, or by {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoritsune|ja|и—ӨеҺҹй јзөҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the fourth {{ill|Seii Taishogun|ja|еҫҒеӨ·еӨ§е°Ҷи»Қ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who gave the same character to Tokiyori. This suggests Tsunesuke underwent his coming-of-age ceremony (genpuku) between 1245 and 1246. There are varying theories regarding Tsunesuke's birth and death years. One theory suggests he was born in 1229 and died in 1292, while another suggests 1226 to 1289.[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B0%91%E5%BC%90%E7%B5%8C%E8%B3%87-1082512 гӮігғҲгғҗгғігӮҜ_е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ] However, as genpuku typically occurred in oneвҖҷs teens, the 1229 birth year is widely considered accurate. Tsunesuke shared responsibilities with his father in governing northern Kyushu and played a crucial role during the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|е…ғеҜҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. He oversaw the fortification of defenses, such as the {{ill|Ishizumi walls|ja|е…ғеҜҮйҳІеЎҒ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and coordinated efforts against the Yuan forces. His earliest documented activity appears in a record from 1269.жңҚйғЁгҖҒ2014th year гҖҒP.416гҖӮе…ёжӢ гҒҜгҖҺжӯҰйӣ„зҘһзӨҫж–ҮжӣёгҖҸпјҲгҖҺйҺҢеҖүйҒәж–ҮгҖҸз¬¬14е·»гғ»10503еҸ·ж–ҮжӣёпјүгҖӮ In 1274, during the {{ill|Battle of Bun'ei|ja|ж–Үж°ёгҒ®еҪ№|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tsunesuke's brother {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} led the Japanese forces. Tsunesuke's role during this campaign is unclear. By 1275, he inherited the familyвҖҷs headship from his father. In 1281, during the {{ill|Battle of KЕҚan|ja|ејҳе®үгҒ®еҪ№|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tsunesuke fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki|ja|еЈұеІҗеі¶гҒ®жҲҰгҒ„|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, where he expelled the Yuan forces but lost his son, {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
[[File:Mooko-SamuraiShips.jpg|300px|right|thumb|'''ShЕҚni Tsunesuke''' and other samurai ships during the Mongol invasion.
{{small|гҖҺ{{ill|MЕҚko ShЕ«rai Ekotoba|ja|и’ҷеҸӨиҘІжқҘзөөи©һ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}гҖҸеҫҢе·»гғ»зөө15гғ»з¬¬19зҙҷ}} Despite this tragedy, he continued to serve as a {{ill|Chinzei BugyЕҚ|ja|йҺ®иҘҝеҘүиЎҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} with {{ill|ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|ja|еӨ§еҸӢй јжі°|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In 1284, following the death of the eighth {{ill|Shikken|ja|Shikken|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|HЕҚjЕҚ Tokimune|ja|еҢ—жқЎжҷӮе®—|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tsunesuke took Buddhist vows and adopted the Dharma name JЕҚe. During the {{ill|Shimozuki Disturbance|ja|йңңжңҲйЁ’еӢ•|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of 1285, he supported {{ill|Taira no Yoritsuna|ja|е№ій јз¶ұ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and defeated his brother {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, aligning with the ruling {{ill|Tokuso family|ja|еҫ—е®—е®¶|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. This led to a weakening of the ShЕҚni clanвҖҷs influence, as they lost control of several provinces. In 1286, Tsunesuke was appointed to the {{ill|Chinzei Dangi-sho|ja|йҺ®иҘҝи«Үиӯ°жүҖ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} alongside {{ill|ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|ja|еӨ§еҸӢй јжі°|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Utsunomiya Michifusa|ja|е®ҮйғҪе®®йҖҡжҲҝ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Shibuya Shigekuni|ja|жёӢи°·йҮҚйғ·|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. He passed away in 1292. His eldest son {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} having died in battle, Tsunesuke was succeeded by {{ill|ShЕҚni Moritsune|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣзөҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. His younger sons {{ill|ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷӮзөҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|ShЕҚni Morishige|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} later founded the {{ill|Shiga clan|ja|еҝ—иіҖж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Hirai clan|ja|е№ідә•ж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, respectively. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *[[Motohisa Yasuda]], ed. гҖҺйҺҢеҖүгғ»е®Өз”әдәәеҗҚдәӢе…ё гӮігғігғ‘гӮҜгғҲзүҲгҖҸпјҲ[[Shin Jinbutsu Oraisha]], 1990пјү *[[Hattori Hideo]] гҖҺи’ҷеҸӨиҘІжқҘгҖҸпјҲ[[Yamakawa Publishing]], 2014пјү *[[Kuroita Katsumi]], ed. гҖҺж–°иЁӮеў—иЈңеӣҪеҸІеӨ§зі»гғ»е°ҠеҚ‘еҲҶи„ү з¬¬2зҜҮгҖҸпјҲ[[Yoshikawa KЕҚbunkan]]пјү {{ShЕҚni clan heads}} {{translated page|ja|е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ|version=101501413|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:pp to pp]] [[Category:ShЕҚni clan]] [[Category:Kamakura shogunate retainers]] [[Category:People of the Mongol invasions of Japan]] [[Category:1229 births]] [[Category:1292 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]] }} [[ja:е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ]] [[ko:%EC%87%BC%EB%8B%88 %EC%93%B0%EB%84%A4%EC%8A%A4%EC%BC%80]] [[sn:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]] [[en:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]]

One theory suggests he was born in 1229 and died in 1292, while another suggests 1226 to 1289.[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B0%91%E5%BC%90%E7%B5%8C%E8%B3%87-1082512 гӮігғҲгғҗгғігӮҜ_е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ] However, as genpuku typically occurred in oneвҖҷs teens, the 1229 birth year is widely considered accurate. Tsunesuke shared responsibilities with his father in governing northern Kyushu and played a crucial role during the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|е…ғеҜҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mongol invasions of Japan|qq=}}. He oversaw the fortification of defenses, such as the {{ill|Ishizumi walls|ja|е…ғеҜҮйҳІеЎҒ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishizumi walls|qq=}}, and coordinated efforts against the Yuan forces. Tsunesuke's role during this campaign is unclear. By 1275, he inherited the familyвҖҷs headship from his father. In 1281, during the {{ill|Battle of KЕҚan|ja|ејҳе®үгҒ®еҪ№|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of KЕҚan|qq=}}, Tsunesuke fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki|ja|еЈұеІҗеі¶гҒ®жҲҰгҒ„|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Iki|qq=}}, where he expelled the Yuan forces but lost his son, {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Toshizane|qq=}}.
[[File:Mooko-SamuraiShips.jpg|300px|right|thumb|'''ShЕҚni Tsunesuke''' and other samurai ships during the Mongol invasion.

In 1284, following the death of the eighth {{ill|Shikken|ja|Shikken|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikken|qq=}}, {{ill|HЕҚjЕҚ Tokimune|ja|еҢ—жқЎжҷӮе®—|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/HЕҚjЕҚ Tokimune|qq=}}, Tsunesuke took Buddhist vows and adopted the Dharma name JЕҚe. During the {{ill|Shimozuki Disturbance|ja|йңңжңҲйЁ’еӢ•|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimozuki Disturbance|qq=}} of 1285, he supported {{ill|Taira no Yoritsuna|ja|е№ій јз¶ұ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Yoritsuna|qq=}} and defeated his brother {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Kagesue|qq=}}, aligning with the ruling {{ill|Tokuso family|ja|еҫ—е®—е®¶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokuso family|qq=}}. This led to a weakening of the ShЕҚni clanвҖҷs influence, as they lost control of several provinces. In 1286, Tsunesuke was appointed to the {{ill|Chinzei Dangi-sho|ja|йҺ®иҘҝи«Үиӯ°жүҖ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinzei Dangi-sho|qq=}} alongside {{ill|ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|ja|еӨ§еҸӢй јжі°|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|qq=}}, {{ill|Utsunomiya Michifusa|ja|е®ҮйғҪе®®йҖҡжҲҝ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Michifusa|qq=}}, and {{ill|Shibuya Shigekuni|ja|жёӢи°·йҮҚйғ·|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Shigekuni|qq=}}. He passed away in 1292. His eldest son {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Toshizane|qq=}} having died in battle, Tsunesuke was succeeded by {{ill|ShЕҚni Moritsune|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣзөҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Moritsune|qq=}}. His younger sons {{ill|ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷӮзөҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Tokitsune|qq=}} and {{ill|ShЕҚni Morishige|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Morishige|qq=}} later founded the {{ill|Shiga clan|ja|еҝ—иіҖж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiga clan|qq=}} and {{ill|Hirai clan|ja|е№ідә•ж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirai clan|qq=}}, respectively. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *[[Motohisa Yasuda]], ed. гҖҺйҺҢеҖүгғ»е®Өз”әдәәеҗҚдәӢе…ё гӮігғігғ‘гӮҜгғҲзүҲгҖҸпјҲ[[Shin Jinbutsu Oraisha]], 1990пјү *[[Hattori Hideo]] гҖҺи’ҷеҸӨиҘІжқҘгҖҸпјҲ[[Yamakawa Publishing]], 2014пјү *[[Kuroita Katsumi]], ed. гҖҺж–°иЁӮеў—иЈңеӣҪеҸІеӨ§зі»гғ»е°ҠеҚ‘еҲҶи„ү з¬¬2зҜҮгҖҸпјҲ[[Yoshikawa KЕҚbunkan]]пјү {{ShЕҚni clan heads}} {{translated page|ja|е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ|version=101501413|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:pp to pp]] [[Category:ShЕҚni clan]] [[Category:Kamakura shogunate retainers]] [[Category:People of the Mongol invasions of Japan]] [[Category:1229 births]] [[Category:1292 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]] }} [[ja:е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ]] [[ko:%EC%87%BC%EB%8B%88 %EC%93%B0%EB%84%A4%EC%8A%A4%EC%BC%80]] [[sn:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]] [[en:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]]

revision.text:
{{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesuke|ja|е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Kagesuke|qq=}} (е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ) {{Infobox samurai | name = ShЕҚni Tsunesuke (е°‘ејҗ зөҢиіҮ) | image = | imagesize = | caption = | era = Mid-[[Kamakura period]] | birth_date = [[Kanki]] 1 (1229) вҖ» | death_date = [[ShЕҚЕҚ]] 5, [[8th month]], [[2nd day (lunar calendar)|2nd day]] (September 14, 1292) вҖ» | posthumous_name = JЕҚe (жө„жҒө; Dharma name)гҖҺе°ҠеҚ‘еҲҶи„үгҖҸз¬¬2зҜҮгғ»P.391гҖӮ | alias = Muto Tsunesuke | Rank = | allegiance = [[Kamakura shogunate]] | lord = [[Prince Koreyasu]] | clan = {{ill|ShЕҚni clan|ja|е°‘ејҗж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni clan|qq=}} | parents = Father: {{ill|ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮиғҪ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|qq=}} | siblings = Tsunesuke, {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Kagesue|qq=}}жңҚйғЁгҖҒ2014th year гҖҒP.416гҖӮ, {{ill|ShЕҚni Morisue|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Morisue|qq=}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Munetsune|ja|е°‘ејҗе®—иіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Munetsune|qq=}} (existence debated)гҖҺжұҺжө·е°ҸйҢІгҖҸгҒ«гҖҢеӨӘе®°и—ӨеҺҹе°‘еҚҝејҹе®—иіҮгҖҚгҒЁиЁҳгҒ•гӮҢгӮӢгҒҢгҖҒж—Ҙжң¬еҒҙеҸІж–ҷгҒ«гҒҜи©ІеҪ“иҖ…гҒҢгҒҠгӮүгҒҡгҖҒи«ёиӘ¬гҒӮгӮӢгҖӮе·қи¶Ҡжі°еҚҡгҖҢжұҺжө·е°ҸйҢІгҒ®ејҳе®үгҒ®еҪ№иЁҳдәӢгҒ«гҒӨгҒ„гҒҰгҖҚгҖҺи»ҚдәӢеҸІеӯҰгҖҸз¬¬ppе·»з¬¬1еҸ·гҖҒ1975th year 6жңҲгҖҒ26-34й ҒгҖӮ | spouse = | children = {{ill|ShЕҚni Moritsune|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣзөҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Moritsune|qq=}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Toshizane|qq=}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷӮзөҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Tokitsune|qq=}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Morishige|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Morishige|qq=}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Sadatsune|ja|е°‘ејҗиІһиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Sadatsune|qq=}} | notable = вҖ» Birth and death dates are debated. }} '''ShЕҚni Tsunesuke''' (е°‘ејҗ зөҢиіҮ) was a {{ill|samurai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/samurai|qq=}} and retainer of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura shogunate|qq=}} during the mid-{{ill|Kamakura period|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura period|qq=}}. He was the son of {{ill|ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮиғҪ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|qq=}} and the third head of the {{ill|ShЕҚni clan|ja|е°‘ејҗж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni clan|qq=}}. == Overview == The character "зөҢ" in Tsunesuke's name was likely granted as a favor by the fourth {{ill|Shikken|ja|Shikken|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikken|qq=}}, {{ill|HЕҚjЕҚ Tokiyori|ja|еҢ—жқЎзөҢжҷӮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/HЕҚjЕҚ Tokiyori|qq=}}, or by {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoritsune|ja|и—ӨеҺҹй јзөҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Yoritsune|qq=}}, the fourth {{ill|Seii Taishogun|ja|еҫҒеӨ·еӨ§е°Ҷи»Қ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seii Taishogun|qq=}}, who gave the same character to Tokiyori. This suggests Tsunesuke underwent his coming-of-age ceremony (genpuku) between 1245 and 1246. There are varying theories regarding Tsunesuke's birth and death years. One theory suggests he was born in 1229 and died in 1292, while another suggests 1226 to 1289.[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B0%91%E5%BC%90%E7%B5%8C%E8%B3%87-1082512 гӮігғҲгғҗгғігӮҜ_е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ] However, as genpuku typically occurred in oneвҖҷs teens, the 1229 birth year is widely considered accurate. Tsunesuke shared responsibilities with his father in governing northern Kyushu and played a crucial role during the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|е…ғеҜҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mongol invasions of Japan|qq=}}. He oversaw the fortification of defenses, such as the {{ill|Ishizumi walls|ja|е…ғеҜҮйҳІеЎҒ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishizumi walls|qq=}}, and coordinated efforts against the Yuan forces. His earliest documented activity appears in a record from 1269.жңҚйғЁгҖҒ2014th year гҖҒP.416гҖӮе…ёжӢ гҒҜгҖҺжӯҰйӣ„зҘһзӨҫж–ҮжӣёгҖҸпјҲгҖҺйҺҢеҖүйҒәж–ҮгҖҸз¬¬14е·»гғ»10503еҸ·ж–ҮжӣёпјүгҖӮ In 1274, during the {{ill|Battle of Bun'ei|ja|ж–Үж°ёгҒ®еҪ№|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Bun'ei|qq=}}, Tsunesuke's brother {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Kagesue|qq=}} led the Japanese forces. Tsunesuke's role during this campaign is unclear. By 1275, he inherited the familyвҖҷs headship from his father. In 1281, during the {{ill|Battle of KЕҚan|ja|ејҳе®үгҒ®еҪ№|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of KЕҚan|qq=}}, Tsunesuke fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki|ja|еЈұеІҗеі¶гҒ®жҲҰгҒ„|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Iki|qq=}}, where he expelled the Yuan forces but lost his son, {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Toshizane|qq=}}.
[[File:Mooko-SamuraiShips.jpg|300px|right|thumb|'''ShЕҚni Tsunesuke''' and other samurai ships during the Mongol invasion.
{{small|гҖҺ{{ill|MЕҚko ShЕ«rai Ekotoba|ja|и’ҷеҸӨиҘІжқҘзөөи©һ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/MЕҚko ShЕ«rai Ekotoba|qq=}}гҖҸеҫҢе·»гғ»зөө15гғ»з¬¬19зҙҷ}} Despite this tragedy, he continued to serve as a {{ill|Chinzei BugyЕҚ|ja|йҺ®иҘҝеҘүиЎҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinzei BugyЕҚ|qq=}} with {{ill|ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|ja|еӨ§еҸӢй јжі°|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|qq=}}. In 1284, following the death of the eighth {{ill|Shikken|ja|Shikken|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikken|qq=}}, {{ill|HЕҚjЕҚ Tokimune|ja|еҢ—жқЎжҷӮе®—|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/HЕҚjЕҚ Tokimune|qq=}}, Tsunesuke took Buddhist vows and adopted the Dharma name JЕҚe. During the {{ill|Shimozuki Disturbance|ja|йңңжңҲйЁ’еӢ•|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimozuki Disturbance|qq=}} of 1285, he supported {{ill|Taira no Yoritsuna|ja|е№ій јз¶ұ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Yoritsuna|qq=}} and defeated his brother {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Kagesue|qq=}}, aligning with the ruling {{ill|Tokuso family|ja|еҫ—е®—е®¶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokuso family|qq=}}. This led to a weakening of the ShЕҚni clanвҖҷs influence, as they lost control of several provinces. In 1286, Tsunesuke was appointed to the {{ill|Chinzei Dangi-sho|ja|йҺ®иҘҝи«Үиӯ°жүҖ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinzei Dangi-sho|qq=}} alongside {{ill|ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|ja|еӨ§еҸӢй јжі°|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|qq=}}, {{ill|Utsunomiya Michifusa|ja|е®ҮйғҪе®®йҖҡжҲҝ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Michifusa|qq=}}, and {{ill|Shibuya Shigekuni|ja|жёӢи°·йҮҚйғ·|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Shigekuni|qq=}}. He passed away in 1292. His eldest son {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Toshizane|qq=}} having died in battle, Tsunesuke was succeeded by {{ill|ShЕҚni Moritsune|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣзөҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Moritsune|qq=}}. His younger sons {{ill|ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷӮзөҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Tokitsune|qq=}} and {{ill|ShЕҚni Morishige|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Morishige|qq=}} later founded the {{ill|Shiga clan|ja|еҝ—иіҖж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiga clan|qq=}} and {{ill|Hirai clan|ja|е№ідә•ж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirai clan|qq=}}, respectively. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *[[Motohisa Yasuda]], ed. гҖҺйҺҢеҖүгғ»е®Өз”әдәәеҗҚдәӢе…ё гӮігғігғ‘гӮҜгғҲзүҲгҖҸпјҲ[[Shin Jinbutsu Oraisha]], 1990пјү *[[Hattori Hideo]] гҖҺи’ҷеҸӨиҘІжқҘгҖҸпјҲ[[Yamakawa Publishing]], 2014пјү *[[Kuroita Katsumi]], ed. гҖҺж–°иЁӮеў—иЈңеӣҪеҸІеӨ§зі»гғ»е°ҠеҚ‘еҲҶи„ү з¬¬2зҜҮгҖҸпјҲ[[Yoshikawa KЕҚbunkan]]пјү {{ShЕҚni clan heads}} {{translated page|ja|е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ|version=101501413|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:ShЕҚni clan]] [[Category:Kamakura shogunate retainers]] [[Category:People of the Mongol invasions of Japan]] [[Category:1229 births]] [[Category:1292 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]] }} [[ja:е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ]] [[ko:%EC%87%BC%EB%8B%88 %EC%93%B0%EB%84%A4%EC%8A%A4%EC%BC%80]] [[sn:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]] [[en:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]]

{{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesuke|ja|е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Kagesuke|qq=}} (е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ) {{Infobox samurai | name = ShЕҚni Tsunesuke (е°‘ејҗ зөҢиіҮ) | image = | imagesize = | caption = | era = Mid-[[Kamakura period]] | birth_date = [[Kanki]] 1 (1229) вҖ» | death_date = [[ShЕҚЕҚ]] 5, [[8th month]], [[2nd day (lunar calendar)|2nd day]] (September 14, 1292) вҖ» | posthumous_name = JЕҚe (жө„жҒө; Dharma name)гҖҺе°ҠеҚ‘еҲҶи„үгҖҸз¬¬2зҜҮгғ»P.391гҖӮ | alias = Muto Tsunesuke | Rank = | allegiance = [[Kamakura shogunate]] | lord = [[Prince Koreyasu]] | clan = {{ill|ShЕҚni clan|ja|е°‘ејҗж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni clan|qq=}} | parents = Father: {{ill|ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮиғҪ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|qq=}} | siblings = Tsunesuke, {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Kagesue|qq=}}жңҚйғЁгҖҒ2014th year гҖҒP.416гҖӮ, {{ill|ShЕҚni Morisue|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Morisue|qq=}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Munetsune|ja|е°‘ејҗе®—иіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Munetsune|qq=}} (existence debated)гҖҺжұҺжө·е°ҸйҢІгҖҸгҒ«гҖҢеӨӘе®°и—ӨеҺҹе°‘еҚҝејҹе®—иіҮгҖҚгҒЁиЁҳгҒ•гӮҢгӮӢгҒҢгҖҒж—Ҙжң¬еҒҙеҸІж–ҷгҒ«гҒҜи©ІеҪ“иҖ…гҒҢгҒҠгӮүгҒҡгҖҒи«ёиӘ¬гҒӮгӮӢгҖӮе·қи¶Ҡжі°еҚҡгҖҢжұҺжө·е°ҸйҢІгҒ®ејҳе®үгҒ®еҪ№иЁҳдәӢгҒ«гҒӨгҒ„гҒҰгҖҚгҖҺи»ҚдәӢеҸІеӯҰгҖҸз¬¬ppе·»з¬¬1еҸ·гҖҒ1975th year 6жңҲгҖҒ26-34й ҒгҖӮ | spouse = | children = {{ill|ShЕҚni Moritsune|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣзөҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Moritsune|qq=}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Toshizane|qq=}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷӮзөҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Tokitsune|qq=}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Morishige|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Morishige|qq=}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Sadatsune|ja|е°‘ејҗиІһиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Sadatsune|qq=}} | notable = вҖ» Birth and death dates are debated. }} '''ShЕҚni Tsunesuke''' (е°‘ејҗ зөҢиіҮ) was a {{ill|samurai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/samurai|qq=}} and retainer of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura shogunate|qq=}} during the mid-{{ill|Kamakura period|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura period|qq=}}. He was the son of {{ill|ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮиғҪ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|qq=}} and the third head of the {{ill|ShЕҚni clan|ja|е°‘ејҗж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni clan|qq=}}. == Overview == The character "зөҢ" in Tsunesuke's name was likely granted as a favor by the fourth {{ill|Shikken|ja|Shikken|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikken|qq=}}, {{ill|HЕҚjЕҚ Tokiyori|ja|еҢ—жқЎзөҢжҷӮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/HЕҚjЕҚ Tokiyori|qq=}}, or by {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoritsune|ja|и—ӨеҺҹй јзөҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Yoritsune|qq=}}, the fourth {{ill|Seii Taishogun|ja|еҫҒеӨ·еӨ§е°Ҷи»Қ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seii Taishogun|qq=}}, who gave the same character to Tokiyori. This suggests Tsunesuke underwent his coming-of-age ceremony (genpuku) between 1245 and 1246. There are varying theories regarding Tsunesuke's birth and death years. One theory suggests he was born in 1229 and died in 1292, while another suggests 1226 to 1289.[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B0%91%E5%BC%90%E7%B5%8C%E8%B3%87-1082512 гӮігғҲгғҗгғігӮҜ_е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ] However, as genpuku typically occurred in oneвҖҷs teens, the 1229 birth year is widely considered accurate. Tsunesuke shared responsibilities with his father in governing northern Kyushu and played a crucial role during the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|е…ғеҜҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mongol invasions of Japan|qq=}}. He oversaw the fortification of defenses, such as the {{ill|Ishizumi walls|ja|е…ғеҜҮйҳІеЎҒ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishizumi walls|qq=}}, and coordinated efforts against the Yuan forces. His earliest documented activity appears in a record from 1269.жңҚйғЁгҖҒ2014th year гҖҒP.416гҖӮе…ёжӢ гҒҜгҖҺжӯҰйӣ„зҘһзӨҫж–ҮжӣёгҖҸпјҲгҖҺйҺҢеҖүйҒәж–ҮгҖҸз¬¬14е·»гғ»10503еҸ·ж–ҮжӣёпјүгҖӮ In 1274, during the {{ill|Battle of Bun'ei|ja|ж–Үж°ёгҒ®еҪ№|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Bun'ei|qq=}}, Tsunesuke's brother {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Kagesue|qq=}} led the Japanese forces. Tsunesuke's role during this campaign is unclear. By 1275, he inherited the familyвҖҷs headship from his father. In 1281, during the {{ill|Battle of KЕҚan|ja|ејҳе®үгҒ®еҪ№|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of KЕҚan|qq=}}, Tsunesuke fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki|ja|еЈұеІҗеі¶гҒ®жҲҰгҒ„|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Iki|qq=}}, where he expelled the Yuan forces but lost his son, {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Toshizane|qq=}}.
[[File:Mooko-SamuraiShips.jpg|300px|right|thumb|'''ShЕҚni Tsunesuke''' and other samurai ships during the Mongol invasion.
{{small|гҖҺ{{ill|MЕҚko ShЕ«rai Ekotoba|ja|и’ҷеҸӨиҘІжқҘзөөи©һ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/MЕҚko ShЕ«rai Ekotoba|qq=}}гҖҸеҫҢе·»гғ»зөө15гғ»з¬¬19зҙҷ}} Despite this tragedy, he continued to serve as a {{ill|Chinzei BugyЕҚ|ja|йҺ®иҘҝеҘүиЎҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinzei BugyЕҚ|qq=}} with {{ill|ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|ja|еӨ§еҸӢй јжі°|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|qq=}}. In 1284, following the death of the eighth {{ill|Shikken|ja|Shikken|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikken|qq=}}, {{ill|HЕҚjЕҚ Tokimune|ja|еҢ—жқЎжҷӮе®—|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/HЕҚjЕҚ Tokimune|qq=}}, Tsunesuke took Buddhist vows and adopted the Dharma name JЕҚe. During the {{ill|Shimozuki Disturbance|ja|йңңжңҲйЁ’еӢ•|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimozuki Disturbance|qq=}} of 1285, he supported {{ill|Taira no Yoritsuna|ja|е№ій јз¶ұ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Yoritsuna|qq=}} and defeated his brother {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Kagesue|qq=}}, aligning with the ruling {{ill|Tokuso family|ja|еҫ—е®—е®¶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokuso family|qq=}}. This led to a weakening of the ShЕҚni clanвҖҷs influence, as they lost control of several provinces. In 1286, Tsunesuke was appointed to the {{ill|Chinzei Dangi-sho|ja|йҺ®иҘҝи«Үиӯ°жүҖ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinzei Dangi-sho|qq=}} alongside {{ill|ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|ja|еӨ§еҸӢй јжі°|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|qq=}}, {{ill|Utsunomiya Michifusa|ja|е®ҮйғҪе®®йҖҡжҲҝ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Michifusa|qq=}}, and {{ill|Shibuya Shigekuni|ja|жёӢи°·йҮҚйғ·|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Shigekuni|qq=}}. He passed away in 1292. His eldest son {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Toshizane|qq=}} having died in battle, Tsunesuke was succeeded by {{ill|ShЕҚni Moritsune|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣзөҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Moritsune|qq=}}. His younger sons {{ill|ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷӮзөҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Tokitsune|qq=}} and {{ill|ShЕҚni Morishige|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Morishige|qq=}} later founded the {{ill|Shiga clan|ja|еҝ—иіҖж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiga clan|qq=}} and {{ill|Hirai clan|ja|е№ідә•ж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirai clan|qq=}}, respectively. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *[[Motohisa Yasuda]], ed. гҖҺйҺҢеҖүгғ»е®Өз”әдәәеҗҚдәӢе…ё гӮігғігғ‘гӮҜгғҲзүҲгҖҸпјҲ[[Shin Jinbutsu Oraisha]], 1990пјү *[[Hattori Hideo]] гҖҺи’ҷеҸӨиҘІжқҘгҖҸпјҲ[[Yamakawa Publishing]], 2014пјү *[[Kuroita Katsumi]], ed. гҖҺж–°иЁӮеў—иЈңеӣҪеҸІеӨ§зі»гғ»е°ҠеҚ‘еҲҶи„ү з¬¬2зҜҮгҖҸпјҲ[[Yoshikawa KЕҚbunkan]]пјү {{ShЕҚni clan heads}} {{translated page|ja|е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ|version=101501413|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:ShЕҚni clan]] [[Category:Kamakura shogunate retainers]] [[Category:People of the Mongol invasions of Japan]] [[Category:1229 births]] [[Category:1292 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ]] [[ko:%EC%87%BC%EB%8B%88 %EC%93%B0%EB%84%A4%EC%8A%A4%EC%BC%80]] [[sn:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]] [[en:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]]

He was the son of {{ill|ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮиғҪ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|qq=}} and the third head of the {{ill|ShЕҚni clan|ja|е°‘ејҗж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni clan|qq=}}. == Overview == The character "зөҢ" in Tsunesuke's name was likely granted as a favor by the fourth {{ill|Shikken|ja|Shikken|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikken|qq=}}, {{ill|HЕҚjЕҚ Tokiyori|ja|еҢ—жқЎзөҢжҷӮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/HЕҚjЕҚ Tokiyori|qq=}}, or by {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoritsune|ja|и—ӨеҺҹй јзөҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Yoritsune|qq=}}, the fourth {{ill|Seii Taishogun|ja|еҫҒеӨ·еӨ§е°Ҷи»Қ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seii Taishogun|qq=}}, who gave the same character to Tokiyori. This suggests Tsunesuke underwent his coming-of-age ceremony (genpuku) between 1245 and 1246. There are varying theories regarding Tsunesuke's birth and death years. One theory suggests he was born in 1229 and died in 1292, while another suggests 1226 to 1289.[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B0%91%E5%BC%90%E7%B5%8C%E8%B3%87-1082512 гӮігғҲгғҗгғігӮҜ_е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ] However, as genpuku typically occurred in oneвҖҷs teens, the 1229 birth year is widely considered accurate. Tsunesuke shared responsibilities with his father in governing northern Kyushu and played a crucial role during the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|е…ғеҜҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mongol invasions of Japan|qq=}}. He oversaw the fortification of defenses, such as the {{ill|Ishizumi walls|ja|е…ғеҜҮйҳІеЎҒ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishizumi walls|qq=}}, and coordinated efforts against the Yuan forces. His earliest documented activity appears in a record from 1269.жңҚйғЁгҖҒ2014th year гҖҒP.416гҖӮе…ёжӢ гҒҜгҖҺжӯҰйӣ„зҘһзӨҫж–ҮжӣёгҖҸпјҲгҖҺйҺҢеҖүйҒәж–ҮгҖҸз¬¬14е·»гғ»10503еҸ·ж–ҮжӣёпјүгҖӮ In 1274, during the {{ill|Battle of Bun'ei|ja|ж–Үж°ёгҒ®еҪ№|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Bun'ei|qq=}}, Tsunesuke's brother {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Kagesue|qq=}} led the Japanese forces. Tsunesuke's role during this campaign is unclear. By 1275, he inherited the familyвҖҷs headship from his father. In 1281, during the {{ill|Battle of KЕҚan|ja|ејҳе®үгҒ®еҪ№|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of KЕҚan|qq=}}, Tsunesuke fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki|ja|еЈұеІҗеі¶гҒ®жҲҰгҒ„|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Iki|qq=}}, where he expelled the Yuan forces but lost his son, {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Toshizane|qq=}}.

{{small|гҖҺ{{ill|MЕҚko ShЕ«rai Ekotoba|ja|и’ҷеҸӨиҘІжқҘзөөи©һ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/MЕҚko ShЕ«rai Ekotoba|qq=}}гҖҸеҫҢе·»гғ»зөө15гғ»з¬¬19зҙҷ}} Despite this tragedy, he continued to serve as a {{ill|Chinzei BugyЕҚ|ja|йҺ®иҘҝеҘүиЎҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinzei BugyЕҚ|qq=}} with {{ill|ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|ja|еӨ§еҸӢй јжі°|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|qq=}}. In 1284, following the death of the eighth {{ill|Shikken|ja|Shikken|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikken|qq=}}, {{ill|HЕҚjЕҚ Tokimune|ja|еҢ—жқЎжҷӮе®—|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/HЕҚjЕҚ Tokimune|qq=}}, Tsunesuke took Buddhist vows and adopted the Dharma name JЕҚe. During the {{ill|Shimozuki Disturbance|ja|йңңжңҲйЁ’еӢ•|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimozuki Disturbance|qq=}} of 1285, he supported {{ill|Taira no Yoritsuna|ja|е№ій јз¶ұ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Yoritsuna|qq=}} and defeated his brother {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Kagesue|qq=}}, aligning with the ruling {{ill|Tokuso family|ja|еҫ—е®—е®¶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokuso family|qq=}}. This led to a weakening of the ShЕҚni clanвҖҷs influence, as they lost control of several provinces. In 1286, Tsunesuke was appointed to the {{ill|Chinzei Dangi-sho|ja|йҺ®иҘҝи«Үиӯ°жүҖ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinzei Dangi-sho|qq=}} alongside {{ill|ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|ja|еӨ§еҸӢй јжі°|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|qq=}}, {{ill|Utsunomiya Michifusa|ja|е®ҮйғҪе®®йҖҡжҲҝ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Michifusa|qq=}}, and {{ill|Shibuya Shigekuni|ja|жёӢи°·йҮҚйғ·|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Shigekuni|qq=}}. He passed away in 1292. His eldest son {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Toshizane|qq=}} having died in battle, Tsunesuke was succeeded by {{ill|ShЕҚni Moritsune|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣзөҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Moritsune|qq=}}. His younger sons {{ill|ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷӮзөҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Tokitsune|qq=}} and {{ill|ShЕҚni Morishige|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Morishige|qq=}} later founded the {{ill|Shiga clan|ja|еҝ—иіҖж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiga clan|qq=}} and {{ill|Hirai clan|ja|е№ідә•ж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirai clan|qq=}}, respectively. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *[[Motohisa Yasuda]], ed. гҖҺйҺҢеҖүгғ»е®Өз”әдәәеҗҚдәӢе…ё гӮігғігғ‘гӮҜгғҲзүҲгҖҸпјҲ[[Shin Jinbutsu Oraisha]], 1990пјү *[[Hattori Hideo]] гҖҺи’ҷеҸӨиҘІжқҘгҖҸпјҲ[[Yamakawa Publishing]], 2014пјү *[[Kuroita Katsumi]], ed. гҖҺж–°иЁӮеў—иЈңеӣҪеҸІеӨ§зі»гғ»е°ҠеҚ‘еҲҶи„ү з¬¬2зҜҮгҖҸпјҲ[[Yoshikawa KЕҚbunkan]]пјү {{ShЕҚni clan heads}} {{translated page|ja|е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ|version=101501413|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:ShЕҚni clan]] [[Category:Kamakura shogunate retainers]] [[Category:People of the Mongol invasions of Japan]] [[Category:1229 births]] [[Category:1292 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ]] [[ko:%EC%87%BC%EB%8B%88 %EC%93%B0%EB%84%A4%EC%8A%A4%EC%BC%80]] [[sn:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]] [[en:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]]

Dharma name)гҖҺе°ҠеҚ‘еҲҶи„үгҖҸз¬¬2зҜҮгғ»P.391гҖӮ | alias = Muto Tsunesuke | Rank = | allegiance = [[Kamakura shogunate]] | lord = [[Prince Koreyasu]] | clan = {{ill|ShЕҚni clan|ja|е°‘ејҗж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni clan|qq=}} | parents = Father: {{ill|ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮиғҪ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|qq=}} | siblings = Tsunesuke, {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Kagesue|qq=}}жңҚйғЁгҖҒ2014th year гҖҒP.416гҖӮ, {{ill|ShЕҚni Morisue|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Morisue|qq=}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Munetsune|ja|е°‘ејҗе®—иіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Munetsune|qq=}} (existence debated)гҖҺжұҺжө·е°ҸйҢІгҖҸгҒ«гҖҢеӨӘе®°и—ӨеҺҹе°‘еҚҝејҹе®—иіҮгҖҚгҒЁиЁҳгҒ•гӮҢгӮӢгҒҢгҖҒж—Ҙжң¬еҒҙеҸІж–ҷгҒ«гҒҜи©ІеҪ“иҖ…гҒҢгҒҠгӮүгҒҡгҖҒи«ёиӘ¬гҒӮгӮӢгҖӮе·қи¶Ҡжі°еҚҡгҖҢжұҺжө·е°ҸйҢІгҒ®ејҳе®үгҒ®еҪ№иЁҳдәӢгҒ«гҒӨгҒ„гҒҰгҖҚгҖҺи»ҚдәӢеҸІеӯҰгҖҸз¬¬ppе·»з¬¬1еҸ·гҖҒ1975th year 6жңҲгҖҒ26-34й ҒгҖӮ | spouse = | children = {{ill|ShЕҚni Moritsune|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣзөҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Moritsune|qq=}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Toshizane|qq=}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷӮзөҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Tokitsune|qq=}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Morishige|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Morishige|qq=}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Sadatsune|ja|е°‘ејҗиІһиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Sadatsune|qq=}} | notable = вҖ» Birth and death dates are debated. }} '''ShЕҚni Tsunesuke''' (е°‘ејҗ зөҢиіҮ) was a {{ill|samurai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/samurai|qq=}} and retainer of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura shogunate|qq=}} during the mid-{{ill|Kamakura period|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura period|qq=}}. He was the son of {{ill|ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮиғҪ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|qq=}} and the third head of the {{ill|ShЕҚni clan|ja|е°‘ејҗж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni clan|qq=}}. == Overview == The character "зөҢ" in Tsunesuke's name was likely granted as a favor by the fourth {{ill|Shikken|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|ja|Shikken|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikken|qq=}}, {{ill|HЕҚjЕҚ Tokiyori|ja|еҢ—жқЎзөҢжҷӮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/HЕҚjЕҚ Tokiyori|qq=}}, or by {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoritsune|ja|и—ӨеҺҹй јзөҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Yoritsune|qq=}}, the fourth {{ill|Seii Taishogun|ja|еҫҒеӨ·еӨ§е°Ҷи»Қ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seii Taishogun|qq=}}, who gave the same character to Tokiyori. This suggests Tsunesuke underwent his coming-of-age ceremony (genpuku) between 1245 and 1246. There are varying theories regarding Tsunesuke's birth and death years. One theory suggests he was born in 1229 and died in 1292, while another suggests 1226 to 1289.[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B0%91%E5%BC%90%E7%B5%8C%E8%B3%87-1082512 гӮігғҲгғҗгғігӮҜ_е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ] However, as genpuku typically occurred in oneвҖҷs teens, the 1229 birth year is widely considered accurate. Tsunesuke shared responsibilities with his father in governing northern Kyushu and played a crucial role during the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|е…ғеҜҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mongol invasions of Japan|qq=}}. He oversaw the fortification of defenses, such as the {{ill|Ishizumi walls|ja|е…ғеҜҮйҳІеЎҒ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishizumi walls|qq=}}, and coordinated efforts against the Yuan forces. His earliest documented activity appears in a record from 1269.жңҚйғЁгҖҒ2014th year гҖҒP.416гҖӮе…ёжӢ гҒҜгҖҺжӯҰйӣ„зҘһзӨҫж–ҮжӣёгҖҸпјҲгҖҺйҺҢеҖүйҒәж–ҮгҖҸз¬¬14е·»гғ»10503еҸ·ж–ҮжӣёпјүгҖӮ In 1274, during the {{ill|Battle of Bun'ei|ja|ж–Үж°ёгҒ®еҪ№|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Bun'ei|qq=}}, Tsunesuke's brother {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Kagesue|qq=}} led the Japanese forces. Tsunesuke's role during this campaign is unclear. By 1275, he inherited the familyвҖҷs headship from his father. In 1281, during the {{ill|Battle of KЕҚan|ja|ејҳе®үгҒ®еҪ№|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of KЕҚan|qq=}}, Tsunesuke fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki|ja|еЈұеІҗеі¶гҒ®жҲҰгҒ„|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Iki|qq=}}, where he expelled the Yuan forces but lost his son, {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Toshizane|qq=}}.
[[File:Mooko-SamuraiShips.jpg|300px|right|thumb|'''ShЕҚni Tsunesuke''' and other samurai ships during the Mongol invasion.

In 1284, following the death of the eighth {{ill|Shikken|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|ja|Shikken|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikken|qq=}}, {{ill|HЕҚjЕҚ Tokimune|ja|еҢ—жқЎжҷӮе®—|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/HЕҚjЕҚ Tokimune|qq=}}, Tsunesuke took Buddhist vows and adopted the Dharma name JЕҚe. During the {{ill|Shimozuki Disturbance|ja|йңңжңҲйЁ’еӢ•|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimozuki Disturbance|qq=}} of 1285, he supported {{ill|Taira no Yoritsuna|ja|е№ій јз¶ұ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Yoritsuna|qq=}} and defeated his brother {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Kagesue|qq=}}, aligning with the ruling {{ill|Tokuso family|ja|еҫ—е®—е®¶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokuso family|qq=}}. This led to a weakening of the ShЕҚni clanвҖҷs influence, as they lost control of several provinces. In 1286, Tsunesuke was appointed to the {{ill|Chinzei Dangi-sho|ja|йҺ®иҘҝи«Үиӯ°жүҖ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinzei Dangi-sho|qq=}} alongside {{ill|ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|ja|еӨ§еҸӢй јжі°|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|qq=}}, {{ill|Utsunomiya Michifusa|ja|е®ҮйғҪе®®йҖҡжҲҝ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Michifusa|qq=}}, and {{ill|Shibuya Shigekuni|ja|жёӢи°·йҮҚйғ·|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Shigekuni|qq=}}. He passed away in 1292. His eldest son {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Toshizane|qq=}} having died in battle, Tsunesuke was succeeded by {{ill|ShЕҚni Moritsune|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣзөҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Moritsune|qq=}}. His younger sons {{ill|ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷӮзөҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Tokitsune|qq=}} and {{ill|ShЕҚni Morishige|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Morishige|qq=}} later founded the {{ill|Shiga clan|ja|еҝ—иіҖж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiga clan|qq=}} and {{ill|Hirai clan|ja|е№ідә•ж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirai clan|qq=}}, respectively. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *[[Motohisa Yasuda]], ed. гҖҺйҺҢеҖүгғ»е®Өз”әдәәеҗҚдәӢе…ё гӮігғігғ‘гӮҜгғҲзүҲгҖҸпјҲ[[Shin Jinbutsu Oraisha]], 1990пјү *[[Hattori Hideo]] гҖҺи’ҷеҸӨиҘІжқҘгҖҸпјҲ[[Yamakawa Publishing]], 2014пјү *[[Kuroita Katsumi]], ed. гҖҺж–°иЁӮеў—иЈңеӣҪеҸІеӨ§зі»гғ»е°ҠеҚ‘еҲҶи„ү з¬¬2зҜҮгҖҸпјҲ[[Yoshikawa KЕҚbunkan]]пјү {{ShЕҚni clan heads}} {{translated page|ja|е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ|version=101501413|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:ShЕҚni clan]] [[Category:Kamakura shogunate retainers]] [[Category:People of the Mongol invasions of Japan]] [[Category:1229 births]] [[Category:1292 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ]] [[ko:%EC%87%BC%EB%8B%88 %EC%93%B0%EB%84%A4%EC%8A%A4%EC%BC%80]] [[sn:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]] [[en:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]]

Dharma name)гҖҺе°ҠеҚ‘еҲҶи„үгҖҸз¬¬2зҜҮгғ»P.391гҖӮ | alias = Muto Tsunesuke | Rank = | allegiance = [[Kamakura shogunate]] | lord = [[Prince Koreyasu]] | clan = {{ill|ShЕҚni clan|ja|е°‘ејҗж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni clan|qq=}} | parents = Father: {{ill|ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮиғҪ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|qq=}} | siblings = Tsunesuke, {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Kagesue|qq=}}жңҚйғЁгҖҒ2014th year гҖҒP.416гҖӮ, {{ill|ShЕҚni Morisue|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Morisue|qq=}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Munetsune|ja|е°‘ејҗе®—иіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Munetsune|qq=}} (existence debated)гҖҺжұҺжө·е°ҸйҢІгҖҸгҒ«гҖҢеӨӘе®°и—ӨеҺҹе°‘еҚҝејҹе®—иіҮгҖҚгҒЁиЁҳгҒ•гӮҢгӮӢгҒҢгҖҒж—Ҙжң¬еҒҙеҸІж–ҷгҒ«гҒҜи©ІеҪ“иҖ…гҒҢгҒҠгӮүгҒҡгҖҒи«ёиӘ¬гҒӮгӮӢгҖӮе·қи¶Ҡжі°еҚҡгҖҢжұҺжө·е°ҸйҢІгҒ®ејҳе®үгҒ®еҪ№иЁҳдәӢгҒ«гҒӨгҒ„гҒҰгҖҚгҖҺи»ҚдәӢеҸІеӯҰгҖҸз¬¬ppе·»з¬¬1еҸ·гҖҒ1975th year 6жңҲгҖҒ26-34й ҒгҖӮ | spouse = | children = {{ill|ShЕҚni Moritsune|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣзөҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Moritsune|qq=}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Toshizane|qq=}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷӮзөҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Tokitsune|qq=}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Morishige|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Morishige|qq=}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Sadatsune|ja|е°‘ејҗиІһиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Sadatsune|qq=}} | notable = вҖ» Birth and death dates are debated. }} '''ShЕҚni Tsunesuke''' (е°‘ејҗ зөҢиіҮ) was a {{ill|samurai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/samurai|qq=}} and retainer of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura shogunate|qq=}} during the mid-{{ill|Kamakura period|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura period|qq=}}. He was the son of {{ill|ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮиғҪ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|qq=}} and the third head of the {{ill|ShЕҚni clan|ja|е°‘ејҗж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni clan|qq=}}. == Overview == The character "зөҢ" in Tsunesuke's name was likely granted as a favor by the fourth {{ill|Shikken|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|ja|Shikken|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikken|qq=}}, {{ill|HЕҚjЕҚ Tokiyori|ja|еҢ—жқЎзөҢжҷӮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/HЕҚjЕҚ Tokiyori|qq=}}, or by {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoritsune|ja|и—ӨеҺҹй јзөҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Yoritsune|qq=}}, the fourth {{ill|Seii Taishogun|ja|еҫҒеӨ·еӨ§е°Ҷи»Қ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seii Taishogun|qq=}}, who gave the same character to Tokiyori. This suggests Tsunesuke underwent his coming-of-age ceremony (genpuku) between 1245 and 1246. There are varying theories regarding Tsunesuke's birth and death years. One theory suggests he was born in 1229 and died in 1292, while another suggests 1226 to 1289.[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B0%91%E5%BC%90%E7%B5%8C%E8%B3%87-1082512 гӮігғҲгғҗгғігӮҜ_е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ] However, as genpuku typically occurred in oneвҖҷs teens, the 1229 birth year is widely considered accurate. Tsunesuke shared responsibilities with his father in governing northern Kyushu and played a crucial role during the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|е…ғеҜҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mongol invasions of Japan|qq=}}. He oversaw the fortification of defenses, such as the {{ill|Ishizumi walls|ja|е…ғеҜҮйҳІеЎҒ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishizumi walls|qq=}}, and coordinated efforts against the Yuan forces. His earliest documented activity appears in a record from 1269.жңҚйғЁгҖҒ2014th year гҖҒP.416гҖӮе…ёжӢ гҒҜгҖҺжӯҰйӣ„зҘһзӨҫж–ҮжӣёгҖҸпјҲгҖҺйҺҢеҖүйҒәж–ҮгҖҸз¬¬14е·»гғ»10503еҸ·ж–ҮжӣёпјүгҖӮ In 1274, during the {{ill|Battle of Bun'ei|ja|ж–Үж°ёгҒ®еҪ№|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Bun'ei|qq=}}, Tsunesuke's brother {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Kagesue|qq=}} led the Japanese forces. Tsunesuke's role during this campaign is unclear. By 1275, he inherited the familyвҖҷs headship from his father. In 1281, during the {{ill|Battle of KЕҚan|ja|ејҳе®үгҒ®еҪ№|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of KЕҚan|qq=}}, Tsunesuke fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki|ja|еЈұеІҗеі¶гҒ®жҲҰгҒ„|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Iki|qq=}}, where he expelled the Yuan forces but lost his son, {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Toshizane|qq=}}.
[[File:Mooko-SamuraiShips.jpg|300px|right|thumb|'''ShЕҚni Tsunesuke''' and other samurai ships during the Mongol invasion.
{{small|гҖҺ{{ill|MЕҚko ShЕ«rai Ekotoba|ja|и’ҷеҸӨиҘІжқҘзөөи©һ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/MЕҚko ShЕ«rai Ekotoba|qq=}}гҖҸеҫҢе·»гғ»зөө15гғ»з¬¬19зҙҷ}} Despite this tragedy, he continued to serve as a {{ill|Chinzei BugyЕҚ|ja|йҺ®иҘҝеҘүиЎҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinzei BugyЕҚ|qq=}} with {{ill|ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|ja|еӨ§еҸӢй јжі°|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|qq=}}. In 1284, following the death of the eighth {{ill|Shikken|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|ja|Shikken|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikken|qq=}}, {{ill|HЕҚjЕҚ Tokimune|ja|еҢ—жқЎжҷӮе®—|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/HЕҚjЕҚ Tokimune|qq=}}, Tsunesuke took Buddhist vows and adopted the Dharma name JЕҚe. During the {{ill|Shimozuki Disturbance|ja|йңңжңҲйЁ’еӢ•|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimozuki Disturbance|qq=}} of 1285, he supported {{ill|Taira no Yoritsuna|ja|е№ій јз¶ұ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Yoritsuna|qq=}} and defeated his brother {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Kagesue|qq=}}, aligning with the ruling {{ill|Tokuso family|ja|еҫ—е®—е®¶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokuso family|qq=}}. This led to a weakening of the ShЕҚni clanвҖҷs influence, as they lost control of several provinces. In 1286, Tsunesuke was appointed to the {{ill|Chinzei Dangi-sho|ja|йҺ®иҘҝи«Үиӯ°жүҖ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinzei Dangi-sho|qq=}} alongside {{ill|ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|ja|еӨ§еҸӢй јжі°|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|qq=}}, {{ill|Utsunomiya Michifusa|ja|е®ҮйғҪе®®йҖҡжҲҝ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Michifusa|qq=}}, and {{ill|Shibuya Shigekuni|ja|жёӢи°·йҮҚйғ·|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Shigekuni|qq=}}. He passed away in 1292. His eldest son {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Toshizane|qq=}} having died in battle, Tsunesuke was succeeded by {{ill|ShЕҚni Moritsune|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣзөҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Moritsune|qq=}}. His younger sons {{ill|ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷӮзөҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Tokitsune|qq=}} and {{ill|ShЕҚni Morishige|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Morishige|qq=}} later founded the {{ill|Shiga clan|ja|еҝ—иіҖж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiga clan|qq=}} and {{ill|Hirai clan|ja|е№ідә•ж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirai clan|qq=}}, respectively. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *[[Motohisa Yasuda]], ed. гҖҺйҺҢеҖүгғ»е®Өз”әдәәеҗҚдәӢе…ё гӮігғігғ‘гӮҜгғҲзүҲгҖҸпјҲ[[Shin Jinbutsu Oraisha]], 1990пјү *[[Hattori Hideo]] гҖҺи’ҷеҸӨиҘІжқҘгҖҸпјҲ[[Yamakawa Publishing]], 2014пјү *[[Kuroita Katsumi]], ed. гҖҺж–°иЁӮеў—иЈңеӣҪеҸІеӨ§зі»гғ»е°ҠеҚ‘еҲҶи„ү з¬¬2зҜҮгҖҸпјҲ[[Yoshikawa KЕҚbunkan]]пјү {{ShЕҚni clan heads}} {{translated page|ja|е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ|version=101501413|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:ShЕҚni clan]] [[Category:Kamakura shogunate retainers]] [[Category:People of the Mongol invasions of Japan]] [[Category:1229 births]] [[Category:1292 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ]] [[ko:%EC%87%BC%EB%8B%88 %EC%93%B0%EB%84%A4%EC%8A%A4%EC%BC%80]] [[sn:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]] [[en:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]]
