He was the son of {{ill|ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮиғҪ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|qq=}} and the third head of the {{ill|ShЕҚni clan|ja|е°‘ејҗж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni clan|qq=}}. == Overview == The character "зөҢ" in Tsunesuke's name was likely granted as a favor by the fourth {{ill|Shikken|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|ja|Shikken|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikken|qq=}}, {{ill|HЕҚjЕҚ Tokiyori|ja|еҢ—жқЎзөҢжҷӮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/HЕҚjЕҚ Tokiyori|qq=}}, or by {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoritsune|ja|и—ӨеҺҹй јзөҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Yoritsune|qq=}}, the fourth {{ill|Seii Taishogun|ja|еҫҒеӨ·еӨ§е°Ҷи»Қ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seii Taishogun|qq=}}, who gave the same character to Tokiyori. This suggests Tsunesuke underwent his coming-of-age ceremony (genpuku) between 1245 and 1246. There are varying theories regarding Tsunesuke's birth and death years. One theory suggests he was born in 1229 and died in 1292, while another suggests 1226 to 1289.[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B0%91%E5%BC%90%E7%B5%8C%E8%B3%87-1082512 гӮігғҲгғҗгғігӮҜ_е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ] However, as genpuku typically occurred in oneвҖҷs teens, the 1229 birth year is widely considered accurate. Tsunesuke shared responsibilities with his father in governing northern Kyushu and played a crucial role during the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|е…ғеҜҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mongol invasions of Japan|qq=}}. He oversaw the fortification of defenses, such as the {{ill|Ishizumi walls|ja|е…ғеҜҮйҳІеЎҒ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishizumi walls|qq=}}, and coordinated efforts against the Yuan forces. His earliest documented activity appears in a record from 1269.жңҚйғЁгҖҒ2014th year гҖҒP.416гҖӮе…ёжӢ гҒҜгҖҺжӯҰйӣ„зҘһзӨҫж–ҮжӣёгҖҸпјҲгҖҺйҺҢеҖүйҒәж–ҮгҖҸз¬¬14е·»гғ»10503еҸ·ж–ҮжӣёпјүгҖӮ In 1274, during the {{ill|Battle of Bun'ei|ja|ж–Үж°ёгҒ®еҪ№|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Bun'ei|qq=}}, Tsunesuke's brother {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Kagesue|qq=}} led the Japanese forces. Tsunesuke's role during this campaign is unclear. By 1275, he inherited the familyвҖҷs headship from his father. In 1281, during the {{ill|Battle of KЕҚan|ja|ејҳе®үгҒ®еҪ№|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of KЕҚan|qq=}}, Tsunesuke fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki|ja|еЈұеІҗеі¶гҒ®жҲҰгҒ„|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Iki|qq=}}, where he expelled the Yuan forces but lost his son, {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Toshizane|qq=}}.
[[File:Mooko-SamuraiShips.jpg|300px|right|thumb|'''ShЕҚni Tsunesuke''' and other samurai ships during the Mongol invasion.
{{small|гҖҺ{{ill|MЕҚko ShЕ«rai Ekotoba|ja|и’ҷеҸӨиҘІжқҘзөөи©һ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/MЕҚko ShЕ«rai Ekotoba|qq=}}гҖҸеҫҢе·»гғ»зөө15гғ»з¬¬19зҙҷ}} Despite this tragedy, he continued to serve as a {{ill|Chinzei BugyЕҚ|ja|йҺ®иҘҝеҘүиЎҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinzei BugyЕҚ|qq=}} with {{ill|ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|ja|еӨ§еҸӢй јжі°|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|qq=}}. In 1284, following the death of the eighth {{ill|Shikken|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|ja|Shikken|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikken|qq=}}, {{ill|HЕҚjЕҚ Tokimune|ja|еҢ—жқЎжҷӮе®—|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/HЕҚjЕҚ Tokimune|qq=}}, Tsunesuke took Buddhist vows and adopted the Dharma name JЕҚe. During the {{ill|Shimozuki Disturbance|ja|йңңжңҲйЁ’еӢ•|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimozuki Disturbance|qq=}} of 1285, he supported {{ill|Taira no Yoritsuna|ja|е№ій јз¶ұ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Yoritsuna|qq=}} and defeated his brother {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Kagesue|qq=}}, aligning with the ruling {{ill|Tokuso family|ja|еҫ—е®—е®¶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokuso family|qq=}}. This led to a weakening of the ShЕҚni clanвҖҷs influence, as they lost control of several provinces. In 1286, Tsunesuke was appointed to the {{ill|Chinzei Dangi-sho|ja|йҺ®иҘҝи«Үиӯ°жүҖ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinzei Dangi-sho|qq=}} alongside {{ill|ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|ja|еӨ§еҸӢй јжі°|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|qq=}}, {{ill|Utsunomiya Michifusa|ja|е®ҮйғҪе®®йҖҡжҲҝ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Michifusa|qq=}}, and {{ill|Shibuya Shigekuni|ja|жёӢи°·йҮҚйғ·|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Shigekuni|qq=}}. He passed away in 1292. His eldest son {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Toshizane|qq=}} having died in battle, Tsunesuke was succeeded by {{ill|ShЕҚni Moritsune|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣзөҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Moritsune|qq=}}. His younger sons {{ill|ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷӮзөҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Tokitsune|qq=}} and {{ill|ShЕҚni Morishige|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Morishige|qq=}} later founded the {{ill|Shiga clan|ja|еҝ—иіҖж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiga clan|qq=}} and {{ill|Hirai clan|ja|е№ідә•ж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirai clan|qq=}}, respectively. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *[[Motohisa Yasuda]], ed. гҖҺйҺҢеҖүгғ»е®Өз”әдәәеҗҚдәӢе…ё гӮігғігғ‘гӮҜгғҲзүҲгҖҸпјҲ[[Shin Jinbutsu Oraisha]], 1990пјү *[[Hattori Hideo]] гҖҺи’ҷеҸӨиҘІжқҘгҖҸпјҲ[[Yamakawa Publishing]], 2014пјү *[[Kuroita Katsumi]], ed. гҖҺж–°иЁӮеў—иЈңеӣҪеҸІеӨ§зі»гғ»е°ҠеҚ‘еҲҶи„ү з¬¬2зҜҮгҖҸпјҲ[[Yoshikawa KЕҚbunkan]]пјү {{ShЕҚni clan heads}} {{translated page|ja|е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ|version=101501413|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:ShЕҚni clan]] [[Category:Kamakura shogunate retainers]] [[Category:People of the Mongol invasions of Japan]] [[Category:1229 births]] [[Category:1292 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ]] [[ko:%EC%87%BC%EB%8B%88 %EC%93%B0%EB%84%A4%EC%8A%A4%EC%BC%80]] [[sn:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]] [[en:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]]

{{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesuke|ja|е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Kagesuke|qq=}} (е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ) {{Infobox samurai | name = ShЕҚni Tsunesuke (е°‘ејҗ зөҢиіҮ) | image = | imagesize = | caption = | era = Mid-[[Kamakura period]] | birth_date = [[Kanki]] 1 (1229) вҖ» | death_date = [[ShЕҚЕҚ]] 5, [[8th month]], [[2nd day (lunar calendar)|2nd day]] (September 14, 1292) вҖ» | posthumous_name = JЕҚe (жө„жҒө; Dharma name)гҖҺе°ҠеҚ‘еҲҶи„үгҖҸз¬¬2зҜҮгғ»P.391гҖӮ | alias = Muto Tsunesuke | Rank = | allegiance = [[Kamakura shogunate]] | lord = [[Prince Koreyasu]] | clan = [[:en:ShЕҚni clan|ShЕҚni clan]] | parents = Father: {{ill|ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮиғҪ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|qq=}} | siblings = Tsunesuke, {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Kagesue|qq=}}жңҚйғЁгҖҒ2014th year гҖҒP.416гҖӮ, {{ill|ShЕҚni Morisue|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Morisue|qq=}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Munetsune|ja|е°‘ејҗе®—иіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Munetsune|qq=}} (existence debated)гҖҺжұҺжө·е°ҸйҢІгҖҸгҒ«гҖҢеӨӘе®°и—ӨеҺҹе°‘еҚҝејҹе®—иіҮгҖҚгҒЁиЁҳгҒ•гӮҢгӮӢгҒҢгҖҒж—Ҙжң¬еҒҙеҸІж–ҷгҒ«гҒҜи©ІеҪ“иҖ…гҒҢгҒҠгӮүгҒҡгҖҒи«ёиӘ¬гҒӮгӮӢгҖӮе·қи¶Ҡжі°еҚҡгҖҢжұҺжө·е°ҸйҢІгҒ®ејҳе®үгҒ®еҪ№иЁҳдәӢгҒ«гҒӨгҒ„гҒҰгҖҚгҖҺи»ҚдәӢеҸІеӯҰгҖҸз¬¬ppе·»з¬¬1еҸ·гҖҒ1975th year 6жңҲгҖҒ26-34й ҒгҖӮ | spouse = | children = {{ill|ShЕҚni Moritsune|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣзөҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Moritsune|qq=}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Toshizane|qq=}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷӮзөҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Tokitsune|qq=}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Morishige|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Morishige|qq=}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Sadatsune|ja|е°‘ејҗиІһиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Sadatsune|qq=}} | notable = вҖ» Birth and death dates are debated. }} '''ShЕҚni Tsunesuke''' (е°‘ејҗ зөҢиіҮ) was a {{ill|samurai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/samurai|qq=}} and retainer of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura shogunate|qq=}} during the mid-{{ill|Kamakura period|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura period|qq=}}. He was the son of {{ill|ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮиғҪ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|qq=}} and the third head of the [[:en:ShЕҚni clan|ShЕҚni clan]]. == Overview == The character "зөҢ" in Tsunesuke's name was likely granted as a favor by the fourth {{ill|Shikken|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|ja|Shikken|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikken|qq=}}, [[:en:HЕҚjЕҚ Tsunetoki|HЕҚjЕҚ Tokiyori]], or by [[:en:KujЕҚ Yoritsune|Fujiwara no Yoritsune]], the fourth [[:en:Shogun|Seii Taishogun]], who gave the same character to Tokiyori. This suggests Tsunesuke underwent his coming-of-age ceremony (genpuku) between 1245 and 1246. There are varying theories regarding Tsunesuke's birth and death years. One theory suggests he was born in 1229 and died in 1292, while another suggests 1226 to 1289.[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B0%91%E5%BC%90%E7%B5%8C%E8%B3%87-1082512 гӮігғҲгғҗгғігӮҜ_е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ] However, as genpuku typically occurred in oneвҖҷs teens, the 1229 birth year is widely considered accurate. Tsunesuke shared responsibilities with his father in governing northern Kyushu and played a crucial role during the [[:en:Mongol invasions of Japan|Mongol invasions of Japan]]. He oversaw the fortification of defenses, such as the [[:en:GenkЕҚ BЕҚrui|Ishizumi walls]], and coordinated efforts against the Yuan forces. His earliest documented activity appears in a record from 1269.жңҚйғЁгҖҒ2014th year гҖҒP.416гҖӮе…ёжӢ гҒҜгҖҺжӯҰйӣ„зҘһзӨҫж–ҮжӣёгҖҸпјҲгҖҺйҺҢеҖүйҒәж–ҮгҖҸз¬¬14е·»гғ»10503еҸ·ж–ҮжӣёпјүгҖӮ In 1274, during the [[:en:Battle of Bun'ei|Battle of Bun'ei]], Tsunesuke's brother {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Kagesue|qq=}} led the Japanese forces. Tsunesuke's role during this campaign is unclear. By 1275, he inherited the familyвҖҷs headship from his father. In 1281, during the [[:en:Battle of KЕҚan|Battle of KЕҚan]], Tsunesuke fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki|ja|еЈұеІҗеі¶гҒ®жҲҰгҒ„|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Iki|qq=}}, where he expelled the Yuan forces but lost his son, {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Toshizane|qq=}}.
[[File:Mooko-SamuraiShips.jpg|300px|right|thumb|'''ShЕҚni Tsunesuke''' and other samurai ships during the Mongol invasion.
{{small|гҖҺ[[:en:MЕҚko ShЕ«rai Ekotoba|MЕҚko ShЕ«rai Ekotoba]]гҖҸеҫҢе·»гғ»зөө15гғ»з¬¬19зҙҷ}} Despite this tragedy, he continued to serve as a [[:en:Chinzei BugyЕҚ|Chinzei BugyЕҚ]] with {{ill|ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|ja|еӨ§еҸӢй јжі°|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|qq=}}. In 1284, following the death of the eighth {{ill|Shikken|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|ja|Shikken|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikken|qq=}}, [[:en:HЕҚjЕҚ Tokimune|HЕҚjЕҚ Tokimune]], Tsunesuke took Buddhist vows and adopted the Dharma name JЕҚe. During the {{ill|Shimozuki Disturbance|ja|йңңжңҲйЁ’еӢ•|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimozuki Disturbance|qq=}} of 1285, he supported {{ill|Taira no Yoritsuna|ja|е№ій јз¶ұ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Yoritsuna|qq=}} and defeated his brother {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Kagesue|qq=}}, aligning with the ruling {{ill|Tokuso family|ja|еҫ—е®—е®¶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokuso family|qq=}}. This led to a weakening of the ShЕҚni clanвҖҷs influence, as they lost control of several provinces. In 1286, Tsunesuke was appointed to the {{ill|Chinzei Dangi-sho|ja|йҺ®иҘҝи«Үиӯ°жүҖ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinzei Dangi-sho|qq=}} alongside {{ill|ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|ja|еӨ§еҸӢй јжі°|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|qq=}}, {{ill|Utsunomiya Michifusa|ja|е®ҮйғҪе®®йҖҡжҲҝ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Michifusa|qq=}}, and {{ill|Shibuya Shigekuni|ja|жёӢи°·йҮҚйғ·|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Shigekuni|qq=}}. He passed away in 1292. His eldest son {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Toshizane|qq=}} having died in battle, Tsunesuke was succeeded by {{ill|ShЕҚni Moritsune|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣзөҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Moritsune|qq=}}. His younger sons {{ill|ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷӮзөҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Tokitsune|qq=}} and {{ill|ShЕҚni Morishige|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Morishige|qq=}} later founded the {{ill|Shiga clan|ja|еҝ—иіҖж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiga clan|qq=}} and [[:en:Hirai clan|Hirai clan]], respectively. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *[[Motohisa Yasuda]], ed. гҖҺйҺҢеҖүгғ»е®Өз”әдәәеҗҚдәӢе…ё гӮігғігғ‘гӮҜгғҲзүҲгҖҸпјҲ[[Shin Jinbutsu Oraisha]], 1990пјү *[[Hattori Hideo]] гҖҺи’ҷеҸӨиҘІжқҘгҖҸпјҲ[[Yamakawa Publishing]], 2014пјү *[[Kuroita Katsumi]], ed. гҖҺж–°иЁӮеў—иЈңеӣҪеҸІеӨ§зі»гғ»е°ҠеҚ‘еҲҶи„ү з¬¬2зҜҮгҖҸпјҲ[[Yoshikawa KЕҚbunkan]]пјү {{ShЕҚni clan heads}} {{translated page|ja|е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ|version=101501413|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:ShЕҚni clan]] [[Category:Kamakura shogunate retainers]] [[Category:People of the Mongol invasions of Japan]] [[Category:1229 births]] [[Category:1292 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ]] [[ko:%EC%87%BC%EB%8B%88 %EC%93%B0%EB%84%A4%EC%8A%A4%EC%BC%80]] [[sn:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]] [[en:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]]

{{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesuke|ja|е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Kagesuke|qq=}} (е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ) {{Infobox samurai | name = ShЕҚni Tsunesuke (е°‘ејҗ зөҢиіҮ) | image = | imagesize = | caption = | era = Mid-[[Kamakura period]] | birth_date = [[Kanki]] 1 (1229) вҖ» | death_date = [[ShЕҚЕҚ]] 5, [[8th month]], [[2nd day (lunar calendar)|2nd day]] (September 14, 1292) вҖ» | posthumous_name = JЕҚe (жө„жҒө; Dharma name)гҖҺе°ҠеҚ‘еҲҶи„үгҖҸз¬¬2зҜҮгғ»P.391гҖӮ | alias = Muto Tsunesuke | Rank = | allegiance = [[Kamakura shogunate]] | lord = [[Prince Koreyasu]] | clan = [[:en:ShЕҚni clan|ShЕҚni clan]] | parents = Father: {{ill|ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮиғҪ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|qq=}} | siblings = Tsunesuke, {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Kagesue|qq=}}жңҚйғЁгҖҒ2014th year гҖҒP.416гҖӮ, {{ill|ShЕҚni Morisue|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Morisue|qq=}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Munetsune|ja|е°‘ејҗе®—иіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Munetsune|qq=}} (existence debated)гҖҺжұҺжө·е°ҸйҢІгҖҸгҒ«гҖҢеӨӘе®°и—ӨеҺҹе°‘еҚҝејҹе®—иіҮгҖҚгҒЁиЁҳгҒ•гӮҢгӮӢгҒҢгҖҒж—Ҙжң¬еҒҙеҸІж–ҷгҒ«гҒҜи©ІеҪ“иҖ…гҒҢгҒҠгӮүгҒҡгҖҒи«ёиӘ¬гҒӮгӮӢгҖӮе·қи¶Ҡжі°еҚҡгҖҢжұҺжө·е°ҸйҢІгҒ®ејҳе®үгҒ®еҪ№иЁҳдәӢгҒ«гҒӨгҒ„гҒҰгҖҚгҖҺи»ҚдәӢеҸІеӯҰгҖҸз¬¬ppе·»з¬¬1еҸ·гҖҒ1975th year 6жңҲгҖҒ26-34й ҒгҖӮ | spouse = | children = {{ill|ShЕҚni Moritsune|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣзөҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Moritsune|qq=}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Toshizane|qq=}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷӮзөҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Tokitsune|qq=}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Morishige|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Morishige|qq=}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Sadatsune|ja|е°‘ејҗиІһиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Sadatsune|qq=}} | notable = вҖ» Birth and death dates are debated. }} '''ShЕҚni Tsunesuke''' (е°‘ејҗ зөҢиіҮ) was a {{ill|samurai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/samurai|qq=}} and retainer of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura shogunate|qq=}} during the mid-{{ill|Kamakura period|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura period|qq=}}. He was the son of {{ill|ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮиғҪ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|qq=}} and the third head of the [[:en:ShЕҚni clan|ShЕҚni clan]]. == Overview == The character "зөҢ" in Tsunesuke's name was likely granted as a favor by the fourth {{ill|Shikken|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|ja|Shikken|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikken|qq=}}, [[:en:HЕҚjЕҚ Tsunetoki|HЕҚjЕҚ Tokiyori]], or by [[:en:KujЕҚ Yoritsune|Fujiwara no Yoritsune]], the fourth [[:en:Shogun|Seii Taishogun]], who gave the same character to Tokiyori. This suggests Tsunesuke underwent his coming-of-age ceremony (genpuku) between 1245 and 1246. There are varying theories regarding Tsunesuke's birth and death years. One theory suggests he was born in 1229 and died in 1292, while another suggests 1226 to 1289.[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B0%91%E5%BC%90%E7%B5%8C%E8%B3%87-1082512 гӮігғҲгғҗгғігӮҜ_е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ] However, as genpuku typically occurred in oneвҖҷs teens, the 1229 birth year is widely considered accurate. Tsunesuke shared responsibilities with his father in governing northern Kyushu and played a crucial role during the [[:en:Mongol invasions of Japan|Mongol invasions of Japan]]. He oversaw the fortification of defenses, such as the [[:en:GenkЕҚ BЕҚrui|Ishizumi walls]], and coordinated efforts against the Yuan forces. His earliest documented activity appears in a record from 1269.жңҚйғЁгҖҒ2014th year гҖҒP.416гҖӮе…ёжӢ гҒҜгҖҺжӯҰйӣ„зҘһзӨҫж–ҮжӣёгҖҸпјҲгҖҺйҺҢеҖүйҒәж–ҮгҖҸз¬¬14е·»гғ»10503еҸ·ж–ҮжӣёпјүгҖӮ In 1274, during the [[:en:Battle of Bun'ei|Battle of Bun'ei]], Tsunesuke's brother {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Kagesue|qq=}} led the Japanese forces. Tsunesuke's role during this campaign is unclear. By 1275, he inherited the familyвҖҷs headship from his father. In 1281, during the [[:en:Battle of KЕҚan|Battle of KЕҚan]], Tsunesuke fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki|ja|еЈұеІҗеі¶гҒ®жҲҰгҒ„|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Iki|qq=}}, where he expelled the Yuan forces but lost his son, {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Toshizane|qq=}}.
[[File:Mooko-SamuraiShips.jpg|300px|right|thumb|'''ShЕҚni Tsunesuke''' and other samurai ships during the Mongol invasion.
{{small|гҖҺ[[:en:MЕҚko ShЕ«rai Ekotoba|MЕҚko ShЕ«rai Ekotoba]]гҖҸеҫҢе·»гғ»зөө15гғ»з¬¬19зҙҷ}} Despite this tragedy, he continued to serve as a [[:en:Chinzei BugyЕҚ|Chinzei BugyЕҚ]] with {{ill|ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|ja|еӨ§еҸӢй јжі°|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|qq=}}. In 1284, following the death of the eighth {{ill|Shikken|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|ja|Shikken|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikken|qq=}}, [[:en:HЕҚjЕҚ Tokimune|HЕҚjЕҚ Tokimune]], Tsunesuke took Buddhist vows and adopted the Dharma name JЕҚe. During the {{ill|Shimozuki Disturbance|ja|йңңжңҲйЁ’еӢ•|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimozuki Disturbance|qq=}} of 1285, he supported {{ill|Taira no Yoritsuna|ja|е№ій јз¶ұ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Yoritsuna|qq=}} and defeated his brother {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Kagesue|qq=}}, aligning with the ruling {{ill|Tokuso family|ja|еҫ—е®—е®¶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokuso family|qq=}}. This led to a weakening of the ShЕҚni clanвҖҷs influence, as they lost control of several provinces. In 1286, Tsunesuke was appointed to the {{ill|Chinzei Dangi-sho|ja|йҺ®иҘҝи«Үиӯ°жүҖ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinzei Dangi-sho|qq=}} alongside {{ill|ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|ja|еӨ§еҸӢй јжі°|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|qq=}}, {{ill|Utsunomiya Michifusa|ja|е®ҮйғҪе®®йҖҡжҲҝ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Michifusa|qq=}}, and {{ill|Shibuya Shigekuni|ja|жёӢи°·йҮҚйғ·|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Shigekuni|qq=}}. He passed away in 1292. His eldest son {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Toshizane|qq=}} having died in battle, Tsunesuke was succeeded by {{ill|ShЕҚni Moritsune|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣзөҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Moritsune|qq=}}. His younger sons {{ill|ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷӮзөҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Tokitsune|qq=}} and {{ill|ShЕҚni Morishige|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Morishige|qq=}} later founded the {{ill|Shiga clan|ja|еҝ—иіҖж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiga clan|qq=}} and [[:en:Hirai clan|Hirai clan]], respectively. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *[[Motohisa Yasuda]], ed. гҖҺйҺҢеҖүгғ»е®Өз”әдәәеҗҚдәӢе…ё гӮігғігғ‘гӮҜгғҲзүҲгҖҸпјҲ[[Shin Jinbutsu Oraisha]], 1990пјү *[[Hattori Hideo]] гҖҺи’ҷеҸӨиҘІжқҘгҖҸпјҲ[[Yamakawa Publishing]], 2014пјү *[[Kuroita Katsumi]], ed. гҖҺж–°иЁӮеў—иЈңеӣҪеҸІеӨ§зі»гғ»е°ҠеҚ‘еҲҶи„ү з¬¬2зҜҮгҖҸпјҲ[[Yoshikawa KЕҚbunkan]]пјү {{ShЕҚni clan heads}} {{translated page|ja|е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ|version=101501413|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:ShЕҚni clan]] [[Category:Kamakura shogunate retainers]] [[Category:People of the Mongol invasions of Japan]] [[Category:1229 births]] [[Category:1292 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ]] [[ko:%EC%87%BC%EB%8B%88 %EC%93%B0%EB%84%A4%EC%8A%A4%EC%BC%80]] [[sn:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]] [[en:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]] [[Category:е…ғеҜҮгҒ®дәәзү©]]

[[File:Mooko-SamuraiShips.jpg|300px|right|thumb|'''ShЕҚni Tsunesuke''' and other samurai ships during the Mongol invasion.
{{small|гҖҺ[[:en:MЕҚko ShЕ«rai Ekotoba|MЕҚko ShЕ«rai Ekotoba]]гҖҸеҫҢе·»гғ»зөө15гғ»з¬¬19зҙҷ}} Despite this tragedy, he continued to serve as a [[:en:Chinzei BugyЕҚ|Chinzei BugyЕҚ]] with {{ill|ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|ja|еӨ§еҸӢй јжі°|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|qq=}}. In 1284, following the death of the eighth {{ill|Shikken|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|ja|Shikken|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikken|qq=}}, [[:en:HЕҚjЕҚ Tokimune|HЕҚjЕҚ Tokimune]], Tsunesuke took Buddhist vows and adopted the Dharma name JЕҚe. During the {{ill|Shimozuki Disturbance|ja|йңңжңҲйЁ’еӢ•|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimozuki Disturbance|qq=}} of 1285, he supported {{ill|Taira no Yoritsuna|ja|е№ій јз¶ұ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Yoritsuna|qq=}} and defeated his brother {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Kagesue|qq=}}, aligning with the ruling {{ill|Tokuso family|ja|еҫ—е®—е®¶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokuso family|qq=}}. This led to a weakening of the ShЕҚni clanвҖҷs influence, as they lost control of several provinces. In 1286, Tsunesuke was appointed to the {{ill|Chinzei Dangi-sho|ja|йҺ®иҘҝи«Үиӯ°жүҖ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinzei Dangi-sho|qq=}} alongside {{ill|ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|ja|еӨ§еҸӢй јжі°|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|qq=}}, {{ill|Utsunomiya Michifusa|ja|е®ҮйғҪе®®йҖҡжҲҝ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Michifusa|qq=}}, and {{ill|Shibuya Shigekuni|ja|жёӢи°·йҮҚйғ·|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Shigekuni|qq=}}. He passed away in 1292. His eldest son {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Toshizane|qq=}} having died in battle, Tsunesuke was succeeded by {{ill|ShЕҚni Moritsune|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣзөҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Moritsune|qq=}}. His younger sons {{ill|ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷӮзөҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Tokitsune|qq=}} and {{ill|ShЕҚni Morishige|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Morishige|qq=}} later founded the {{ill|Shiga clan|ja|еҝ—иіҖж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiga clan|qq=}} and [[:en:Hirai clan|Hirai clan]], respectively. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *[[Motohisa Yasuda]], ed. гҖҺйҺҢеҖүгғ»е®Өз”әдәәеҗҚдәӢе…ё гӮігғігғ‘гӮҜгғҲзүҲгҖҸпјҲ[[Shin Jinbutsu Oraisha]], 1990пјү *[[Hattori Hideo]] гҖҺи’ҷеҸӨиҘІжқҘгҖҸпјҲ[[Yamakawa Publishing]], 2014пјү *[[Kuroita Katsumi]], ed. гҖҺж–°иЁӮеў—иЈңеӣҪеҸІеӨ§зі»гғ»е°ҠеҚ‘еҲҶи„ү з¬¬2зҜҮгҖҸпјҲ[[Yoshikawa KЕҚbunkan]]пјү {{ShЕҚni clan heads}} {{translated page|ja|е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ|version=101501413|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:ShЕҚni clan]] [[Category:Kamakura shogunate retainers]] [[Category:People of the Mongol invasions of Japan]] [[Category:1229 births]] [[Category:1292 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ]] [[ko:%EC%87%BC%EB%8B%88 %EC%93%B0%EB%84%A4%EC%8A%A4%EC%BC%80]] [[sn:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]] [[en:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]] [[Category:е…ғеҜҮгҒ®дәәзү©]] [[Category:е°‘ејҗж°Ҹ]]

{{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesuke|ja|е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Kagesuke|qq=}} (е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ) {{Infobox samurai | name = ShЕҚni Tsunesuke (е°‘ејҗ зөҢиіҮ) | image = | imagesize = | caption = | era = Mid-[[Kamakura period]] | birth_date = [[Kanki]] 1 (1229) вҖ» | death_date = [[ShЕҚЕҚ]] 5, [[8th month]], [[2nd day (lunar calendar)|2nd day]] (September 14, 1292) вҖ» | posthumous_name = JЕҚe (жө„жҒө; Dharma name)гҖҺе°ҠеҚ‘еҲҶи„үгҖҸз¬¬2зҜҮгғ»P.391гҖӮ | alias = Muto Tsunesuke | Rank = | allegiance = [[Kamakura shogunate]] | lord = [[Prince Koreyasu]] | clan = [[:en:ShЕҚni clan|ShЕҚni clan]] | parents = Father: {{ill|ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮиғҪ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|qq=}} | siblings = Tsunesuke, {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Kagesue|qq=}}жңҚйғЁгҖҒ2014th year гҖҒP.416гҖӮ, {{ill|ShЕҚni Morisue|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Morisue|qq=}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Munetsune|ja|е°‘ејҗе®—иіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Munetsune|qq=}} (existence debated)гҖҺжұҺжө·е°ҸйҢІгҖҸгҒ«гҖҢеӨӘе®°и—ӨеҺҹе°‘еҚҝејҹе®—иіҮгҖҚгҒЁиЁҳгҒ•гӮҢгӮӢгҒҢгҖҒж—Ҙжң¬еҒҙеҸІж–ҷгҒ«гҒҜи©ІеҪ“иҖ…гҒҢгҒҠгӮүгҒҡгҖҒи«ёиӘ¬гҒӮгӮӢгҖӮе·қи¶Ҡжі°еҚҡгҖҢжұҺжө·е°ҸйҢІгҒ®ејҳе®үгҒ®еҪ№иЁҳдәӢгҒ«гҒӨгҒ„гҒҰгҖҚгҖҺи»ҚдәӢеҸІеӯҰгҖҸз¬¬ppе·»з¬¬1еҸ·гҖҒ1975th year 6жңҲгҖҒ26-34й ҒгҖӮ | spouse = | children = {{ill|ShЕҚni Moritsune|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣзөҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Moritsune|qq=}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Toshizane|qq=}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷӮзөҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Tokitsune|qq=}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Morishige|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Morishige|qq=}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Sadatsune|ja|е°‘ејҗиІһиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Sadatsune|qq=}} | notable = вҖ» Birth and death dates are debated. }} '''ShЕҚni Tsunesuke''' (е°‘ејҗ зөҢиіҮ) was a {{ill|samurai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/samurai|qq=}} and retainer of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura shogunate|qq=}} during the mid-{{ill|Kamakura period|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura period|qq=}}. He was the son of {{ill|ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮиғҪ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|qq=}} and the third head of the [[:en:ShЕҚni clan|ShЕҚni clan]]. == Overview == The character "зөҢ" in Tsunesuke's name was likely granted as a favor by the fourth {{ill|Shikken|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|ja|Shikken|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikken|qq=}}, [[:en:HЕҚjЕҚ Tsunetoki|HЕҚjЕҚ Tokiyori]], or by [[:en:KujЕҚ Yoritsune|Fujiwara no Yoritsune]], the fourth [[:en:Shogun|Seii Taishogun]], who gave the same character to Tokiyori. This suggests Tsunesuke underwent his coming-of-age ceremony (genpuku) between 1245 and 1246. There are varying theories regarding Tsunesuke's birth and death years. One theory suggests he was born in 1229 and died in 1292, while another suggests 1226 to 1289.[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B0%91%E5%BC%90%E7%B5%8C%E8%B3%87-1082512 гӮігғҲгғҗгғігӮҜ_е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ] However, as genpuku typically occurred in oneвҖҷs teens, the 1229 birth year is widely considered accurate. Tsunesuke shared responsibilities with his father in governing northern Kyushu and played a crucial role during the [[:en:Mongol invasions of Japan|Mongol invasions of Japan]]. He oversaw the fortification of defenses, such as the [[:en:GenkЕҚ BЕҚrui|Ishizumi walls]], and coordinated efforts against the Yuan forces. His earliest documented activity appears in a record from 1269.жңҚйғЁгҖҒ2014th year гҖҒP.416гҖӮе…ёжӢ гҒҜгҖҺжӯҰйӣ„зҘһзӨҫж–ҮжӣёгҖҸпјҲгҖҺйҺҢеҖүйҒәж–ҮгҖҸз¬¬14е·»гғ»10503еҸ·ж–ҮжӣёпјүгҖӮ In 1274, during the [[:en:Battle of Bun'ei|Battle of Bun'ei]], Tsunesuke's brother {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Kagesue|qq=}} led the Japanese forces. Tsunesuke's role during this campaign is unclear. By 1275, he inherited the familyвҖҷs headship from his father. In 1281, during the [[:en:Battle of KЕҚan|Battle of KЕҚan]], Tsunesuke fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki|ja|еЈұеІҗеі¶гҒ®жҲҰгҒ„|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Iki|qq=}}, where he expelled the Yuan forces but lost his son, {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Toshizane|qq=}}.
[[File:Mooko-SamuraiShips.jpg|300px|right|thumb|'''ShЕҚni Tsunesuke''' and other samurai ships during the Mongol invasion.
{{small|гҖҺ[[:en:MЕҚko ShЕ«rai Ekotoba|MЕҚko ShЕ«rai Ekotoba]]гҖҸеҫҢе·»гғ»зөө15гғ»з¬¬19зҙҷ}} Despite this tragedy, he continued to serve as a [[:en:Chinzei BugyЕҚ|Chinzei BugyЕҚ]] with {{ill|ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|ja|еӨ§еҸӢй јжі°|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|qq=}}. In 1284, following the death of the eighth {{ill|Shikken|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|ja|Shikken|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikken|qq=}}, [[:en:HЕҚjЕҚ Tokimune|HЕҚjЕҚ Tokimune]], Tsunesuke took Buddhist vows and adopted the Dharma name JЕҚe. During the {{ill|Shimozuki Disturbance|ja|йңңжңҲйЁ’еӢ•|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimozuki Disturbance|qq=}} of 1285, he supported {{ill|Taira no Yoritsuna|ja|е№ій јз¶ұ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Yoritsuna|qq=}} and defeated his brother {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Kagesue|qq=}}, aligning with the ruling {{ill|Tokuso family|ja|еҫ—е®—е®¶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokuso family|qq=}}. This led to a weakening of the ShЕҚni clanвҖҷs influence, as they lost control of several provinces. In 1286, Tsunesuke was appointed to the {{ill|Chinzei Dangi-sho|ja|йҺ®иҘҝи«Үиӯ°жүҖ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinzei Dangi-sho|qq=}} alongside {{ill|ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|ja|еӨ§еҸӢй јжі°|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|qq=}}, {{ill|Utsunomiya Michifusa|ja|е®ҮйғҪе®®йҖҡжҲҝ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Michifusa|qq=}}, and {{ill|Shibuya Shigekuni|ja|жёӢи°·йҮҚйғ·|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Shigekuni|qq=}}. He passed away in 1292. His eldest son {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Toshizane|qq=}} having died in battle, Tsunesuke was succeeded by {{ill|ShЕҚni Moritsune|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣзөҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Moritsune|qq=}}. His younger sons {{ill|ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷӮзөҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Tokitsune|qq=}} and {{ill|ShЕҚni Morishige|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Morishige|qq=}} later founded the {{ill|Shiga clan|ja|еҝ—иіҖж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiga clan|qq=}} and [[:en:Hirai clan|Hirai clan]], respectively. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *[[Motohisa Yasuda]], ed. гҖҺйҺҢеҖүгғ»е®Өз”әдәәеҗҚдәӢе…ё гӮігғігғ‘гӮҜгғҲзүҲгҖҸпјҲ[[Shin Jinbutsu Oraisha]], 1990пјү *[[Hattori Hideo]] гҖҺи’ҷеҸӨиҘІжқҘгҖҸпјҲ[[Yamakawa Publishing]], 2014пјү *[[Kuroita Katsumi]], ed. гҖҺж–°иЁӮеў—иЈңеӣҪеҸІеӨ§зі»гғ»е°ҠеҚ‘еҲҶи„ү з¬¬2зҜҮгҖҸпјҲ[[Yoshikawa KЕҚbunkan]]пјү {{ShЕҚni clan heads}} {{translated page|ja|е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ|version=101501413|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:ShЕҚni clan]] [[Category:Kamakura shogunate retainers]] [[Category:People of the Mongol invasions of Japan]] [[Category:1229 births]] [[Category:1292 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ]] [[ko:%EC%87%BC%EB%8B%88 %EC%93%B0%EB%84%A4%EC%8A%A4%EC%BC%80]] [[sn:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]] [[en:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]] [[Category:е…ғеҜҮгҒ®дәәзү©]] [[Category:е°‘ејҗж°Ҹ]] [[Category:йҺҢеҖүе№•еәңеҫЎе®¶дәә]]

Dharma name)гҖҺе°ҠеҚ‘еҲҶи„үгҖҸз¬¬2зҜҮгғ»P.391гҖӮ | alias = Muto Tsunesuke | Rank = | allegiance = [[Kamakura shogunate]] | lord = [[Prince Koreyasu]] | clan = [[:en:ShЕҚni clan|ShЕҚni clan]] | parents = Father: {{ill|ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮиғҪ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|qq=}} | siblings = Tsunesuke, {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Kagesue|qq=}}жңҚйғЁгҖҒ2014th year гҖҒP.416гҖӮ, {{ill|ShЕҚni Morisue|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Morisue|qq=}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Munetsune|ja|е°‘ејҗе®—иіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Munetsune|qq=}} (existence debated)гҖҺжұҺжө·е°ҸйҢІгҖҸгҒ«гҖҢеӨӘе®°и—ӨеҺҹе°‘еҚҝејҹе®—иіҮгҖҚгҒЁиЁҳгҒ•гӮҢгӮӢгҒҢгҖҒж—Ҙжң¬еҒҙеҸІж–ҷгҒ«гҒҜи©ІеҪ“иҖ…гҒҢгҒҠгӮүгҒҡгҖҒи«ёиӘ¬гҒӮгӮӢгҖӮе·қи¶Ҡжі°еҚҡгҖҢжұҺжө·е°ҸйҢІгҒ®ејҳе®үгҒ®еҪ№иЁҳдәӢгҒ«гҒӨгҒ„гҒҰгҖҚгҖҺи»ҚдәӢеҸІеӯҰгҖҸз¬¬ppе·»з¬¬1еҸ·гҖҒ1975th year 6жңҲгҖҒ26-34й ҒгҖӮ | spouse = | children = {{ill|ShЕҚni Moritsune|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣзөҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Moritsune|qq=}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Toshizane|qq=}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷӮзөҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Tokitsune|qq=}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Morishige|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Morishige|qq=}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Sadatsune|ja|е°‘ејҗиІһиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Sadatsune|qq=}} | notable = вҖ» Birth and death dates are debated. }} '''ShЕҚni Tsunesuke''' (е°‘ејҗ зөҢиіҮ) was a {{ill|samurai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/samurai|qq=}} and retainer of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura shogunate|qq=}} during the mid-{{ill|Kamakura period|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura period|qq=}}. He was the son of {{ill|ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮиғҪ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|qq=}} and the third head of the [[:en:ShЕҚni clan|ShЕҚni clan]]. == Overview == The character "зөҢ" in Tsunesuke's name was likely granted as a favor by the fourth {{ill|Shikken|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|ja|Shikken|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikken|qq=}}, [[:en:HЕҚjЕҚ Tsunetoki|HЕҚjЕҚ Tokiyori]], or by [[:en:KujЕҚ Yoritsune|Fujiwara no Yoritsune]], the fourth [[:en:Shogun|Seii Taishogun]], who gave the same character to Tokiyori. This suggests Tsunesuke underwent his coming-of-age ceremony (genpuku) between 1245 and 1246. There are varying theories regarding Tsunesuke's birth and death years. One theory suggests he was born in 1229 and died in 1292, while another suggests 1226 to 1289.[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B0%91%E5%BC%90%E7%B5%8C%E8%B3%87-1082512 гӮігғҲгғҗгғігӮҜ_е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ] However, as genpuku typically occurred in oneвҖҷs teens, the 1229 birth year is widely considered accurate. Tsunesuke shared responsibilities with his father in governing northern Kyushu and played a crucial role during the [[:en:Mongol invasions of Japan|Mongol invasions of Japan]]. He oversaw the fortification of defenses, such as the [[:en:GenkЕҚ BЕҚrui|Ishizumi walls]], and coordinated efforts against the Yuan forces. His earliest documented activity appears in a record from 1269.жңҚйғЁгҖҒ2014th year гҖҒP.416гҖӮе…ёжӢ гҒҜгҖҺжӯҰйӣ„зҘһзӨҫж–ҮжӣёгҖҸпјҲгҖҺйҺҢеҖүйҒәж–ҮгҖҸз¬¬14е·»гғ»10503еҸ·ж–ҮжӣёпјүгҖӮ In 1274, during the [[:en:Battle of Bun'ei|Battle of Bun'ei]], Tsunesuke's brother {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Kagesue|qq=}} led the Japanese forces. Tsunesuke's role during this campaign is unclear. By 1275, he inherited the familyвҖҷs headship from his father. In 1281, during the [[:en:Battle of KЕҚan|Battle of KЕҚan]], Tsunesuke fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki|ja|еЈұеІҗеі¶гҒ®жҲҰгҒ„|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Iki|qq=}}, where he expelled the Yuan forces but lost his son, {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Toshizane|qq=}}.
[[File:Mooko-SamuraiShips.jpg|300px|right|thumb|'''ShЕҚni Tsunesuke''' and other samurai ships during the Mongol invasion.
{{small|гҖҺ[[:en:MЕҚko ShЕ«rai Ekotoba|MЕҚko ShЕ«rai Ekotoba]]гҖҸеҫҢе·»гғ»зөө15гғ»з¬¬19зҙҷ}} Despite this tragedy, he continued to serve as a [[:en:Chinzei BugyЕҚ|Chinzei BugyЕҚ]] with {{ill|ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|ja|еӨ§еҸӢй јжі°|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|qq=}}. In 1284, following the death of the eighth {{ill|Shikken|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|ja|Shikken|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikken|qq=}}, [[:en:HЕҚjЕҚ Tokimune|HЕҚjЕҚ Tokimune]], Tsunesuke took Buddhist vows and adopted the Dharma name JЕҚe. During the {{ill|Shimozuki Disturbance|ja|йңңжңҲйЁ’еӢ•|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimozuki Disturbance|qq=}} of 1285, he supported {{ill|Taira no Yoritsuna|ja|е№ій јз¶ұ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Yoritsuna|qq=}} and defeated his brother {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Kagesue|qq=}}, aligning with the ruling {{ill|Tokuso family|ja|еҫ—е®—е®¶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokuso family|qq=}}. This led to a weakening of the ShЕҚni clanвҖҷs influence, as they lost control of several provinces. In 1286, Tsunesuke was appointed to the {{ill|Chinzei Dangi-sho|ja|йҺ®иҘҝи«Үиӯ°жүҖ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinzei Dangi-sho|qq=}} alongside {{ill|ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|ja|еӨ§еҸӢй јжі°|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|qq=}}, {{ill|Utsunomiya Michifusa|ja|е®ҮйғҪе®®йҖҡжҲҝ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Michifusa|qq=}}, and {{ill|Shibuya Shigekuni|ja|жёӢи°·йҮҚйғ·|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Shigekuni|qq=}}. He passed away in 1292. His eldest son {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Toshizane|qq=}} having died in battle, Tsunesuke was succeeded by {{ill|ShЕҚni Moritsune|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣзөҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Moritsune|qq=}}. His younger sons {{ill|ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷӮзөҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Tokitsune|qq=}} and {{ill|ShЕҚni Morishige|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Morishige|qq=}} later founded the {{ill|Shiga clan|ja|еҝ—иіҖж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiga clan|qq=}} and [[:en:Hirai clan|Hirai clan]], respectively. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *[[Motohisa Yasuda]], ed. гҖҺйҺҢеҖүгғ»е®Өз”әдәәеҗҚдәӢе…ё гӮігғігғ‘гӮҜгғҲзүҲгҖҸпјҲ[[Shin Jinbutsu Oraisha]], 1990пјү *[[Hattori Hideo]] гҖҺи’ҷеҸӨиҘІжқҘгҖҸпјҲ[[Yamakawa Publishing]], 2014пјү *[[Kuroita Katsumi]], ed. гҖҺж–°иЁӮеў—иЈңеӣҪеҸІеӨ§зі»гғ»е°ҠеҚ‘еҲҶи„ү з¬¬2зҜҮгҖҸпјҲ[[Yoshikawa KЕҚbunkan]]пјү {{ShЕҚni clan heads}} {{translated page|ja|е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ|version=101501413|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:ShЕҚni clan]] [[Category:Kamakura shogunate retainers]] [[Category:People of the Mongol invasions of Japan]] [[Category:1229 births]] [[Category:1292 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ]] [[ko:%EC%87%BC%EB%8B%88 %EC%93%B0%EB%84%A4%EC%8A%A4%EC%BC%80]] [[sn:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]] [[en:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]] [[Category:е…ғеҜҮгҒ®дәәзү©]] [[Category:е°‘ејҗж°Ҹ]] [[Category:Gokenin of the Kamakura shogunate]]

He was the son of {{ill|ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮиғҪ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|qq=}} and the third head of the [[:en:ShЕҚni clan|ShЕҚni clan]]. == Overview == The character "зөҢ" in Tsunesuke's name was likely granted as a favor by the fourth {{ill|Shikken|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|ja|Shikken|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikken|qq=}}, [[:en:HЕҚjЕҚ Tsunetoki|HЕҚjЕҚ Tokiyori]], or by [[:en:KujЕҚ Yoritsune|Fujiwara no Yoritsune]], the fourth [[:en:Shogun|Seii Taishogun]], who gave the same character to Tokiyori. This suggests Tsunesuke underwent his coming-of-age ceremony (genpuku) between 1245 and 1246. There are varying theories regarding Tsunesuke's birth and death years. One theory suggests he was born in 1229 and died in 1292, while another suggests 1226 to 1289.[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B0%91%E5%BC%90%E7%B5%8C%E8%B3%87-1082512 гӮігғҲгғҗгғігӮҜ_е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ] However, as genpuku typically occurred in oneвҖҷs teens, the 1229 birth year is widely considered accurate. Tsunesuke shared responsibilities with his father in governing northern Kyushu and played a crucial role during the [[:en:Mongol invasions of Japan|Mongol invasions of Japan]]. He oversaw the fortification of defenses, such as the [[:en:GenkЕҚ BЕҚrui|Ishizumi walls]], and coordinated efforts against the Yuan forces. His earliest documented activity appears in a record from 1269.жңҚйғЁгҖҒ2014th year гҖҒP.416гҖӮе…ёжӢ гҒҜгҖҺжӯҰйӣ„зҘһзӨҫж–ҮжӣёгҖҸпјҲгҖҺйҺҢеҖүйҒәж–ҮгҖҸз¬¬14е·»гғ»10503еҸ·ж–ҮжӣёпјүгҖӮ In 1274, during the [[:en:Battle of Bun'ei|Battle of Bun'ei]], Tsunesuke's brother {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Kagesue|qq=}} led the Japanese forces. Tsunesuke's role during this campaign is unclear. By 1275, he inherited the familyвҖҷs headship from his father. In 1281, during the [[:en:Battle of KЕҚan|Battle of KЕҚan]], Tsunesuke fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki|ja|еЈұеІҗеі¶гҒ®жҲҰгҒ„|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Iki|qq=}}, where he expelled the Yuan forces but lost his son, {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Toshizane|qq=}}.
[[File:Mooko-SamuraiShips.jpg|300px|right|thumb|'''ShЕҚni Tsunesuke''' and other samurai ships during the Mongol invasion.
{{small|гҖҺ[[:en:MЕҚko ShЕ«rai Ekotoba|MЕҚko ShЕ«rai Ekotoba]]гҖҸеҫҢе·»гғ»зөө15гғ»з¬¬19зҙҷ}} Despite this tragedy, he continued to serve as a [[:en:Chinzei BugyЕҚ|Chinzei BugyЕҚ]] with {{ill|ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|ja|еӨ§еҸӢй јжі°|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|qq=}}. In 1284, following the death of the eighth {{ill|Shikken|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|ja|Shikken|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikken|qq=}}, [[:en:HЕҚjЕҚ Tokimune|HЕҚjЕҚ Tokimune]], Tsunesuke took Buddhist vows and adopted the Dharma name JЕҚe. During the {{ill|Shimozuki Disturbance|ja|йңңжңҲйЁ’еӢ•|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimozuki Disturbance|qq=}} of 1285, he supported {{ill|Taira no Yoritsuna|ja|е№ій јз¶ұ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Yoritsuna|qq=}} and defeated his brother {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Kagesue|qq=}}, aligning with the ruling {{ill|Tokuso family|ja|еҫ—е®—е®¶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokuso family|qq=}}. This led to a weakening of the ShЕҚni clanвҖҷs influence, as they lost control of several provinces. In 1286, Tsunesuke was appointed to the {{ill|Chinzei Dangi-sho|ja|йҺ®иҘҝи«Үиӯ°жүҖ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinzei Dangi-sho|qq=}} alongside {{ill|ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|ja|еӨ§еҸӢй јжі°|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|qq=}}, {{ill|Utsunomiya Michifusa|ja|е®ҮйғҪе®®йҖҡжҲҝ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Michifusa|qq=}}, and {{ill|Shibuya Shigekuni|ja|жёӢи°·йҮҚйғ·|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Shigekuni|qq=}}. He passed away in 1292. His eldest son {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Toshizane|qq=}} having died in battle, Tsunesuke was succeeded by {{ill|ShЕҚni Moritsune|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣзөҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Moritsune|qq=}}. His younger sons {{ill|ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷӮзөҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Tokitsune|qq=}} and {{ill|ShЕҚni Morishige|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Morishige|qq=}} later founded the {{ill|Shiga clan|ja|еҝ—иіҖж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiga clan|qq=}} and [[:en:Hirai clan|Hirai clan]], respectively. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *[[Motohisa Yasuda]], ed. гҖҺйҺҢеҖүгғ»е®Өз”әдәәеҗҚдәӢе…ё гӮігғігғ‘гӮҜгғҲзүҲгҖҸпјҲ[[Shin Jinbutsu Oraisha]], 1990пјү *[[Hattori Hideo]] гҖҺи’ҷеҸӨиҘІжқҘгҖҸпјҲ[[Yamakawa Publishing]], 2014пјү *[[Kuroita Katsumi]], ed. гҖҺж–°иЁӮеў—иЈңеӣҪеҸІеӨ§зі»гғ»е°ҠеҚ‘еҲҶи„ү з¬¬2зҜҮгҖҸпјҲ[[Yoshikawa KЕҚbunkan]]пјү {{ShЕҚni clan heads}} {{translated page|ja|е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ|version=101501413|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:ShЕҚni clan]] [[Category:Kamakura shogunate retainers]] [[Category:People of the Mongol invasions of Japan]] [[Category:1229 births]] [[Category:1292 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ]] [[ko:%EC%87%BC%EB%8B%88 %EC%93%B0%EB%84%A4%EC%8A%A4%EC%BC%80]] [[sn:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]] [[Category:е…ғеҜҮгҒ®дәәзү©]] [[Category:е°‘ејҗж°Ҹ]] [[Category:Gokenin of the Kamakura shogunate]]

{{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesuke|ja|е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Kagesuke|qq=}} (е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ) {{Infobox samurai | name = ShЕҚni Tsunesuke (е°‘ејҗ зөҢиіҮ) | image = | imagesize = | caption = | era = Mid-[[Kamakura period]] | birth_date = [[Kanki]] 1 (1229) вҖ» | death_date = [[ShЕҚЕҚ]] 5, [[8th month]], [[2nd day (lunar calendar)|2nd day]] (September 14, 1292) вҖ» | posthumous_name = JЕҚe (жө„жҒө; Dharma name)гҖҺе°ҠеҚ‘еҲҶи„үгҖҸз¬¬2зҜҮгғ»P.391гҖӮ | alias = Muto Tsunesuke | Rank = | allegiance = [[Kamakura shogunate]] | lord = [[Prince Koreyasu]] | clan = [[:en:ShЕҚni clan|ShЕҚni clan]] | parents = Father: {{ill|ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮиғҪ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|qq=}} | siblings = Tsunesuke, {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Kagesue|qq=}}жңҚйғЁгҖҒ2014th year гҖҒP.416гҖӮ, {{ill|ShЕҚni Morisue|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Morisue|qq=}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Munetsune|ja|е°‘ејҗе®—иіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Munetsune|qq=}} (existence debated)гҖҺжұҺжө·е°ҸйҢІгҖҸгҒ«гҖҢеӨӘе®°и—ӨеҺҹе°‘еҚҝејҹе®—иіҮгҖҚгҒЁиЁҳгҒ•гӮҢгӮӢгҒҢгҖҒж—Ҙжң¬еҒҙеҸІж–ҷгҒ«гҒҜи©ІеҪ“иҖ…гҒҢгҒҠгӮүгҒҡгҖҒи«ёиӘ¬гҒӮгӮӢгҖӮе·қи¶Ҡжі°еҚҡгҖҢжұҺжө·е°ҸйҢІгҒ®ејҳе®үгҒ®еҪ№иЁҳдәӢгҒ«гҒӨгҒ„гҒҰгҖҚгҖҺи»ҚдәӢеҸІеӯҰгҖҸз¬¬ppе·»з¬¬1еҸ·гҖҒ1975th year 6жңҲгҖҒ26-34й ҒгҖӮ | spouse = | children = {{ill|ShЕҚni Moritsune|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣзөҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Moritsune|qq=}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Toshizane|qq=}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷӮзөҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Tokitsune|qq=}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Morishige|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Morishige|qq=}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Sadatsune|ja|е°‘ејҗиІһиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Sadatsune|qq=}} | notable = вҖ» Birth and death dates are debated. }} '''ShЕҚni Tsunesuke''' (е°‘ејҗ зөҢиіҮ) was a {{ill|samurai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/samurai|qq=}} and retainer of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura shogunate|qq=}} during the mid-{{ill|Kamakura period|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura period|qq=}}. He was the son of {{ill|ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮиғҪ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|qq=}} and the third head of the [[:en:ShЕҚni clan|ShЕҚni clan]]. == Overview == The character "зөҢ" in Tsunesuke's name was likely granted as a favor by the fourth {{ill|Shikken|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|ja|Shikken|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikken|qq=}}, [[:en:HЕҚjЕҚ Tsunetoki|HЕҚjЕҚ Tokiyori]], or by [[:en:KujЕҚ Yoritsune|Fujiwara no Yoritsune]], the fourth [[:en:Shogun|Seii Taishogun]], who gave the same character to Tokiyori. This suggests Tsunesuke underwent his coming-of-age ceremony (genpuku) between 1245 and 1246. There are varying theories regarding Tsunesuke's birth and death years. One theory suggests he was born in 1229 and died in 1292, while another suggests 1226 to 1289.[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B0%91%E5%BC%90%E7%B5%8C%E8%B3%87-1082512 гӮігғҲгғҗгғігӮҜ_е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ] However, as genpuku typically occurred in oneвҖҷs teens, the 1229 birth year is widely considered accurate. Tsunesuke shared responsibilities with his father in governing northern Kyushu and played a crucial role during the [[:en:Mongol invasions of Japan|Mongol invasions of Japan]]. He oversaw the fortification of defenses, such as the [[:en:GenkЕҚ BЕҚrui|Ishizumi walls]], and coordinated efforts against the Yuan forces. His earliest documented activity appears in a record from 1269.жңҚйғЁгҖҒ2014th year гҖҒP.416гҖӮе…ёжӢ гҒҜгҖҺжӯҰйӣ„зҘһзӨҫж–ҮжӣёгҖҸпјҲгҖҺйҺҢеҖүйҒәж–ҮгҖҸз¬¬14е·»гғ»10503еҸ·ж–ҮжӣёпјүгҖӮ In 1274, during the [[:en:Battle of Bun'ei|Battle of Bun'ei]], Tsunesuke's brother {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Kagesue|qq=}} led the Japanese forces. Tsunesuke's role during this campaign is unclear. By 1275, he inherited the familyвҖҷs headship from his father. In 1281, during the [[:en:Battle of KЕҚan|Battle of KЕҚan]], Tsunesuke fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki|ja|еЈұеІҗеі¶гҒ®жҲҰгҒ„|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Iki|qq=}}, where he expelled the Yuan forces but lost his son, {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Toshizane|qq=}}.
[[File:Mooko-SamuraiShips.jpg|300px|right|thumb|'''ShЕҚni Tsunesuke''' and other samurai ships during the Mongol invasion.
{{small|гҖҺ[[:en:MЕҚko ShЕ«rai Ekotoba|MЕҚko ShЕ«rai Ekotoba]]гҖҸеҫҢе·»гғ»зөө15гғ»з¬¬19зҙҷ}} Despite this tragedy, he continued to serve as a [[:en:Chinzei BugyЕҚ|Chinzei BugyЕҚ]] with {{ill|ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|ja|еӨ§еҸӢй јжі°|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|qq=}}. In 1284, following the death of the eighth {{ill|Shikken|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|ja|Shikken|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikken|qq=}}, [[:en:HЕҚjЕҚ Tokimune|HЕҚjЕҚ Tokimune]], Tsunesuke took Buddhist vows and adopted the Dharma name JЕҚe. During the {{ill|Shimozuki Disturbance|ja|йңңжңҲйЁ’еӢ•|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimozuki Disturbance|qq=}} of 1285, he supported {{ill|Taira no Yoritsuna|ja|е№ій јз¶ұ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Yoritsuna|qq=}} and defeated his brother {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Kagesue|qq=}}, aligning with the ruling {{ill|Tokuso family|ja|еҫ—е®—е®¶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokuso family|qq=}}. This led to a weakening of the ShЕҚni clanвҖҷs influence, as they lost control of several provinces. In 1286, Tsunesuke was appointed to the {{ill|Chinzei Dangi-sho|ja|йҺ®иҘҝи«Үиӯ°жүҖ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinzei Dangi-sho|qq=}} alongside {{ill|ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|ja|еӨ§еҸӢй јжі°|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|qq=}}, {{ill|Utsunomiya Michifusa|ja|е®ҮйғҪе®®йҖҡжҲҝ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Michifusa|qq=}}, and {{ill|Shibuya Shigekuni|ja|жёӢи°·йҮҚйғ·|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Shigekuni|qq=}}. He passed away in 1292. His eldest son {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Toshizane|qq=}} having died in battle, Tsunesuke was succeeded by {{ill|ShЕҚni Moritsune|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣзөҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Moritsune|qq=}}. His younger sons {{ill|ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷӮзөҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Tokitsune|qq=}} and {{ill|ShЕҚni Morishige|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Morishige|qq=}} later founded the {{ill|Shiga clan|ja|еҝ—иіҖж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiga clan|qq=}} and [[:en:Hirai clan|Hirai clan]], respectively. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *[[Motohisa Yasuda]], ed. гҖҺйҺҢеҖүгғ»е®Өз”әдәәеҗҚдәӢе…ё гӮігғігғ‘гӮҜгғҲзүҲгҖҸпјҲ[[Shin Jinbutsu Oraisha]], 1990пјү *[[Hattori Hideo]] гҖҺи’ҷеҸӨиҘІжқҘгҖҸпјҲ[[Yamakawa Publishing]], 2014пјү *[[Kuroita Katsumi]], ed. гҖҺж–°иЁӮеў—иЈңеӣҪеҸІеӨ§зі»гғ»е°ҠеҚ‘еҲҶи„ү з¬¬2зҜҮгҖҸпјҲ[[Yoshikawa KЕҚbunkan]]пјү {{ShЕҚni clan heads}} {{translated page|ja|е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ|version=101501413|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:ShЕҚni clan]] [[Category:Kamakura shogunate retainers]] [[Category:People of the Mongol invasions of Japan]] [[Category:1229 births]] [[Category:1292 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ]] [[ko:%EC%87%BC%EB%8B%88 %EC%93%B0%EB%84%A4%EC%8A%A4%EC%BC%80]] [[sn:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]] [[Category:е…ғеҜҮгҒ®дәәзү©]] [[Category:е°‘ејҗж°Ҹ]] [[Category:Gokenin of the Kamakura shogunate]] {{Commons category|ShЕҚni Tsunesuke}} [[ko:мҮјлӢҲ м“°л„ӨмҠӨмјҖ]]

His earliest documented activity appears in a record from 1269.жңҚйғЁгҖҒ2014th year гҖҒP.416гҖӮе…ёжӢ гҒҜгҖҺжӯҰйӣ„зҘһзӨҫж–ҮжӣёгҖҸпјҲгҖҺйҺҢеҖүйҒәж–ҮгҖҸз¬¬14е·»гғ»10503еҸ·ж–ҮжӣёпјүгҖӮ In 1274, during the [[:en:Battle of Bun'ei|Battle of Bun'ei]], Tsunesuke's brother {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Kagesue|qq=}} led the Japanese forces. Tsunesuke's role during this campaign is unclear. By 1275, he inherited the familyвҖҷs headship from his father. In 1281, during the [[:en:Battle of KЕҚan|Battle of KЕҚan]], Tsunesuke fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki|ja|еЈұеІҗеі¶гҒ®жҲҰгҒ„|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Iki|qq=}}, where he expelled the Yuan forces but lost his son, {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Toshizane|qq=}}.
[[File:Mooko-SamuraiShips.jpg|300px|right|thumb|'''ShЕҚni Tsunesuke''' and other samurai ships during the Mongol invasion.
{{small|гҖҺ[[:en:MЕҚko ShЕ«rai Ekotoba|MЕҚko ShЕ«rai Ekotoba]]гҖҸеҫҢе·»гғ»зөө15гғ»з¬¬19зҙҷ}} Despite this tragedy, he continued to serve as a [[:en:Chinzei BugyЕҚ|Chinzei BugyЕҚ]] with {{ill|ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|ja|еӨ§еҸӢй јжі°|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|qq=}}. In 1284, following the death of the eighth {{ill|Shikken|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|ja|Shikken|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikken|qq=}}, [[:en:HЕҚjЕҚ Tokimune|HЕҚjЕҚ Tokimune]], Tsunesuke took Buddhist vows and adopted the Dharma name JЕҚe. During the {{ill|Shimozuki Disturbance|ja|йңңжңҲйЁ’еӢ•|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimozuki Disturbance|qq=}} of 1285, he supported {{ill|Taira no Yoritsuna|ja|е№ій јз¶ұ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Yoritsuna|qq=}} and defeated his brother {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Kagesue|qq=}}, aligning with the ruling {{ill|Tokuso family|ja|еҫ—е®—е®¶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokuso family|qq=}}. This led to a weakening of the ShЕҚni clanвҖҷs influence, as they lost control of several provinces. In 1286, Tsunesuke was appointed to the {{ill|Chinzei Dangi-sho|ja|йҺ®иҘҝи«Үиӯ°жүҖ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinzei Dangi-sho|qq=}} alongside {{ill|ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|ja|еӨ§еҸӢй јжі°|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|qq=}}, {{ill|Utsunomiya Michifusa|ja|е®ҮйғҪе®®йҖҡжҲҝ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Michifusa|qq=}}, and {{ill|Shibuya Shigekuni|ja|жёӢи°·йҮҚйғ·|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Shigekuni|qq=}}. He passed away in 1292. His eldest son {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Toshizane|qq=}} having died in battle, Tsunesuke was succeeded by {{ill|ShЕҚni Moritsune|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣзөҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Moritsune|qq=}}. His younger sons {{ill|ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷӮзөҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Tokitsune|qq=}} and {{ill|ShЕҚni Morishige|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Morishige|qq=}} later founded the {{ill|Shiga clan|ja|еҝ—иіҖж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiga clan|qq=}} and [[:en:Hirai clan|Hirai clan]], respectively. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *[[Motohisa Yasuda]], ed. гҖҺйҺҢеҖүгғ»е®Өз”әдәәеҗҚдәӢе…ё гӮігғігғ‘гӮҜгғҲзүҲгҖҸпјҲ[[Shin Jinbutsu Oraisha]], 1990пјү *[[Hattori Hideo]] гҖҺи’ҷеҸӨиҘІжқҘгҖҸпјҲ[[Yamakawa Publishing]], 2014пјү *[[Kuroita Katsumi]], ed. гҖҺж–°иЁӮеў—иЈңеӣҪеҸІеӨ§зі»гғ»е°ҠеҚ‘еҲҶи„ү з¬¬2зҜҮгҖҸпјҲ[[Yoshikawa KЕҚbunkan]]пјү {{ShЕҚni clan heads}} {{translated page|ja|е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ|version=101501413|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:ShЕҚni clan]] [[Category:Kamakura shogunate retainers]] [[Category:People of the Mongol invasions of Japan]] [[Category:1229 births]] [[Category:1292 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ]] [[ko:%EC%87%BC%EB%8B%88 %EC%93%B0%EB%84%A4%EC%8A%A4%EC%BC%80]] [[sn:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]] [[Category:People of the Mongol invasions of JapanпҝҪ]] [[Category:е°‘ејҗж°Ҹ]] [[Category:Gokenin of the Kamakura shogunate]] {{Commons category|ShЕҚni Tsunesuke}} [[ko:мҮјлӢҲ м“°л„ӨмҠӨмјҖ]]

{{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesuke|ja|е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Kagesuke|qq=}} (е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ) {{Infobox samurai | name = ShЕҚni Tsunesuke (е°‘ејҗ зөҢиіҮ) | image = | imagesize = | caption = | era = Mid-[[Kamakura period]] | birth_date = [[Kanki]] 1 (1229) вҖ» | death_date = [[ShЕҚЕҚ]] 5, [[8th month]], [[2nd day (lunar calendar)|2nd day]] (September 14, 1292) вҖ» | posthumous_name = JЕҚe (жө„жҒө; Dharma name)гҖҺе°ҠеҚ‘еҲҶи„үгҖҸз¬¬2зҜҮгғ»P.391гҖӮ | alias = Muto Tsunesuke | Rank = | allegiance = [[Kamakura shogunate]] | lord = [[Prince Koreyasu]] | clan = [[:en:ShЕҚni clan|ShЕҚni clan]] | parents = Father: {{ill|ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮиғҪ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|qq=}} | siblings = Tsunesuke, {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Kagesue|qq=}}жңҚйғЁгҖҒ2014th year гҖҒP.416гҖӮ, {{ill|ShЕҚni Morisue|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Morisue|qq=}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Munetsune|ja|е°‘ејҗе®—иіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Munetsune|qq=}} (existence debated)гҖҺжұҺжө·е°ҸйҢІгҖҸгҒ«гҖҢеӨӘе®°и—ӨеҺҹе°‘еҚҝејҹе®—иіҮгҖҚгҒЁиЁҳгҒ•гӮҢгӮӢгҒҢгҖҒж—Ҙжң¬еҒҙеҸІж–ҷгҒ«гҒҜи©ІеҪ“иҖ…гҒҢгҒҠгӮүгҒҡгҖҒи«ёиӘ¬гҒӮгӮӢгҖӮе·қи¶Ҡжі°еҚҡгҖҢжұҺжө·е°ҸйҢІгҒ®ејҳе®үгҒ®еҪ№иЁҳдәӢгҒ«гҒӨгҒ„гҒҰгҖҚгҖҺи»ҚдәӢеҸІеӯҰгҖҸз¬¬ppе·»з¬¬1еҸ·гҖҒ1975th year 6жңҲгҖҒ26-34й ҒгҖӮ | spouse = | children = {{ill|ShЕҚni Moritsune|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣзөҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Moritsune|qq=}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Toshizane|qq=}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷӮзөҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Tokitsune|qq=}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Morishige|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Morishige|qq=}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Sadatsune|ja|е°‘ејҗиІһиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Sadatsune|qq=}} | notable = вҖ» Birth and death dates are debated. }} '''ShЕҚni Tsunesuke''' (е°‘ејҗ зөҢиіҮ) was a {{ill|samurai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/samurai|qq=}} and retainer of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura shogunate|qq=}} during the mid-{{ill|Kamakura period|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura period|qq=}}. He was the son of {{ill|ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮиғҪ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|qq=}} and the third head of the [[:en:ShЕҚni clan|ShЕҚni clan]]. == Overview == The character "зөҢ" in Tsunesuke's name was likely granted as a favor by the fourth {{ill|Shikken|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|ja|Shikken|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikken|qq=}}, [[:en:HЕҚjЕҚ Tsunetoki|HЕҚjЕҚ Tokiyori]], or by [[:en:KujЕҚ Yoritsune|Fujiwara no Yoritsune]], the fourth [[:en:Shogun|Seii Taishogun]], who gave the same character to Tokiyori. This suggests Tsunesuke underwent his coming-of-age ceremony (genpuku) between 1245 and 1246. There are varying theories regarding Tsunesuke's birth and death years. One theory suggests he was born in 1229 and died in 1292, while another suggests 1226 to 1289.[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B0%91%E5%BC%90%E7%B5%8C%E8%B3%87-1082512 гӮігғҲгғҗгғігӮҜ_е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ] However, as genpuku typically occurred in oneвҖҷs teens, the 1229 birth year is widely considered accurate. Tsunesuke shared responsibilities with his father in governing northern Kyushu and played a crucial role during the [[:en:Mongol invasions of Japan|Mongol invasions of Japan]]. He oversaw the fortification of defenses, such as the [[:en:GenkЕҚ BЕҚrui|Ishizumi walls]], and coordinated efforts against the Yuan forces. His earliest documented activity appears in a record from 1269.жңҚйғЁгҖҒ2014th year гҖҒP.416гҖӮе…ёжӢ гҒҜгҖҺжӯҰйӣ„зҘһзӨҫж–ҮжӣёгҖҸпјҲгҖҺйҺҢеҖүйҒәж–ҮгҖҸз¬¬14е·»гғ»10503еҸ·ж–ҮжӣёпјүгҖӮ In 1274, during the [[:en:Battle of Bun'ei|Battle of Bun'ei]], Tsunesuke's brother {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Kagesue|qq=}} led the Japanese forces. Tsunesuke's role during this campaign is unclear. By 1275, he inherited the familyвҖҷs headship from his father. In 1281, during the [[:en:Battle of KЕҚan|Battle of KЕҚan]], Tsunesuke fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki|ja|еЈұеІҗеі¶гҒ®жҲҰгҒ„|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Iki|qq=}}, where he expelled the Yuan forces but lost his son, {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Toshizane|qq=}}.
[[File:Mooko-SamuraiShips.jpg|300px|right|thumb|'''ShЕҚni Tsunesuke''' and other samurai ships during the Mongol invasion.
{{small|гҖҺ[[:en:MЕҚko ShЕ«rai Ekotoba|MЕҚko ShЕ«rai Ekotoba]]гҖҸеҫҢе·»гғ»зөө15гғ»з¬¬19зҙҷ}} Despite this tragedy, he continued to serve as a [[:en:Chinzei BugyЕҚ|Chinzei BugyЕҚ]] with {{ill|ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|ja|еӨ§еҸӢй јжі°|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|qq=}}. In 1284, following the death of the eighth {{ill|Shikken|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|ja|Shikken|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikken|qq=}}, [[:en:HЕҚjЕҚ Tokimune|HЕҚjЕҚ Tokimune]], Tsunesuke took Buddhist vows and adopted the Dharma name JЕҚe. During the {{ill|Shimozuki Disturbance|ja|йңңжңҲйЁ’еӢ•|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimozuki Disturbance|qq=}} of 1285, he supported {{ill|Taira no Yoritsuna|ja|е№ій јз¶ұ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Yoritsuna|qq=}} and defeated his brother {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Kagesue|qq=}}, aligning with the ruling {{ill|Tokuso family|ja|еҫ—е®—е®¶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokuso family|qq=}}. This led to a weakening of the ShЕҚni clanвҖҷs influence, as they lost control of several provinces. In 1286, Tsunesuke was appointed to the {{ill|Chinzei Dangi-sho|ja|йҺ®иҘҝи«Үиӯ°жүҖ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinzei Dangi-sho|qq=}} alongside {{ill|ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|ja|еӨ§еҸӢй јжі°|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|qq=}}, {{ill|Utsunomiya Michifusa|ja|е®ҮйғҪе®®йҖҡжҲҝ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Michifusa|qq=}}, and {{ill|Shibuya Shigekuni|ja|жёӢи°·йҮҚйғ·|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Shigekuni|qq=}}. He passed away in 1292. His eldest son {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Toshizane|qq=}} having died in battle, Tsunesuke was succeeded by {{ill|ShЕҚni Moritsune|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣзөҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Moritsune|qq=}}. His younger sons {{ill|ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷӮзөҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Tokitsune|qq=}} and {{ill|ShЕҚni Morishige|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Morishige|qq=}} later founded the {{ill|Shiga clan|ja|еҝ—иіҖж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiga clan|qq=}} and [[:en:Hirai clan|Hirai clan]], respectively. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *[[Motohisa Yasuda]], ed. гҖҺйҺҢеҖүгғ»е®Өз”әдәәеҗҚдәӢе…ё гӮігғігғ‘гӮҜгғҲзүҲгҖҸпјҲ[[Shin Jinbutsu Oraisha]], 1990пјү *[[Hattori Hideo]] гҖҺи’ҷеҸӨиҘІжқҘгҖҸпјҲ[[Yamakawa Publishing]], 2014пјү *[[Kuroita Katsumi]], ed. гҖҺж–°иЁӮеў—иЈңеӣҪеҸІеӨ§зі»гғ»е°ҠеҚ‘еҲҶи„ү з¬¬2зҜҮгҖҸпјҲ[[Yoshikawa KЕҚbunkan]]пјү {{ShЕҚni clan heads}} {{translated page|ja|е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ|version=101501413|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:ShЕҚni clan]] [[Category:Kamakura shogunate retainers]] [[Category:People of the Mongol invasions of Japan]] [[Category:1229 births]] [[Category:1292 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ]] [[ko:%EC%87%BC%EB%8B%88 %EC%93%B0%EB%84%A4%EC%8A%A4%EC%BC%80]] [[sn:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]] [[Category:People of the Mongol invasions of Japan]] [[Category:е°‘ејҗж°Ҹ]] [[Category:Gokenin of the Kamakura shogunate]] {{Commons category|ShЕҚni Tsunesuke}} [[ko:мҮјлӢҲ м“°л„ӨмҠӨмјҖ]]

{{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesuke|ja|е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Kagesuke|qq=}} (е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ) {{Infobox samurai | name = ShЕҚni Tsunesuke (е°‘ејҗ зөҢиіҮ) | image = | imagesize = | caption = | era = Mid-[[Kamakura period]] | birth_date = [[Kanki]] 1 (1229) вҖ» | death_date = [[ShЕҚЕҚ]] 5, [[8th month]], [[2nd day (lunar calendar)|2nd day]] (September 14, 1292) вҖ» | posthumous_name = JЕҚe (жө„жҒө; Dharma name)гҖҺе°ҠеҚ‘еҲҶи„үгҖҸз¬¬2зҜҮгғ»P.391гҖӮ | alias = Muto Tsunesuke | Rank = | allegiance = [[Kamakura shogunate]] | lord = [[Prince Koreyasu]] | clan = [[:en:ShЕҚni clan|ShЕҚni clan]] | parents = Father: {{ill|ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮиғҪ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|qq=}} | siblings = Tsunesuke, {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Kagesue|qq=}}жңҚйғЁгҖҒ2014th year гҖҒP.416гҖӮ, {{ill|ShЕҚni Morisue|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Morisue|qq=}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Munetsune|ja|е°‘ејҗе®—иіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Munetsune|qq=}} (existence debated)гҖҺжұҺжө·е°ҸйҢІгҖҸгҒ«гҖҢеӨӘе®°и—ӨеҺҹе°‘еҚҝејҹе®—иіҮгҖҚгҒЁиЁҳгҒ•гӮҢгӮӢгҒҢгҖҒж—Ҙжң¬еҒҙеҸІж–ҷгҒ«гҒҜи©ІеҪ“иҖ…гҒҢгҒҠгӮүгҒҡгҖҒи«ёиӘ¬гҒӮгӮӢгҖӮе·қи¶Ҡжі°еҚҡгҖҢжұҺжө·е°ҸйҢІгҒ®ејҳе®үгҒ®еҪ№иЁҳдәӢгҒ«гҒӨгҒ„гҒҰгҖҚгҖҺи»ҚдәӢеҸІеӯҰгҖҸз¬¬ppе·»з¬¬1еҸ·гҖҒ1975th year 6жңҲгҖҒ26-34й ҒгҖӮ | spouse = | children = {{ill|ShЕҚni Moritsune|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣзөҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Moritsune|qq=}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Toshizane|qq=}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷӮзөҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Tokitsune|qq=}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Morishige|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Morishige|qq=}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Sadatsune|ja|е°‘ејҗиІһиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Sadatsune|qq=}} | notable = вҖ» Birth and death dates are debated. }} '''ShЕҚni Tsunesuke''' (е°‘ејҗ зөҢиіҮ) was a {{ill|samurai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/samurai|qq=}} and retainer of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura shogunate|qq=}} during the mid-{{ill|Kamakura period|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura period|qq=}}. He was the son of {{ill|ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮиғҪ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|qq=}} and the third head of the [[:en:ShЕҚni clan|ShЕҚni clan]]. == Overview == The character "зөҢ" in Tsunesuke's name was likely granted as a favor by the fourth {{ill|Shikken|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|ja|Shikken|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikken|qq=}}, [[:en:HЕҚjЕҚ Tsunetoki|HЕҚjЕҚ Tokiyori]], or by [[:en:KujЕҚ Yoritsune|Fujiwara no Yoritsune]], the fourth [[:en:Shogun|Seii Taishogun]], who gave the same character to Tokiyori. This suggests Tsunesuke underwent his coming-of-age ceremony (genpuku) between 1245 and 1246. There are varying theories regarding Tsunesuke's birth and death years. One theory suggests he was born in 1229 and died in 1292, while another suggests 1226 to 1289.[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B0%91%E5%BC%90%E7%B5%8C%E8%B3%87-1082512 гӮігғҲгғҗгғігӮҜ_е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ] However, as genpuku typically occurred in oneвҖҷs teens, the 1229 birth year is widely considered accurate. Tsunesuke shared responsibilities with his father in governing northern Kyushu and played a crucial role during the [[:en:Mongol invasions of Japan|Mongol invasions of Japan]]. He oversaw the fortification of defenses, such as the [[:en:GenkЕҚ BЕҚrui|Ishizumi walls]], and coordinated efforts against the Yuan forces. His earliest documented activity appears in a record from 1269.жңҚйғЁгҖҒ2014th year гҖҒP.416гҖӮе…ёжӢ гҒҜгҖҺжӯҰйӣ„зҘһзӨҫж–ҮжӣёгҖҸпјҲгҖҺйҺҢеҖүйҒәж–ҮгҖҸз¬¬14е·»гғ»10503еҸ·ж–ҮжӣёпјүгҖӮ In 1274, during the [[:en:Battle of Bun'ei|Battle of Bun'ei]], Tsunesuke's brother {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Kagesue|qq=}} led the Japanese forces. Tsunesuke's role during this campaign is unclear. By 1275, he inherited the familyвҖҷs headship from his father. In 1281, during the [[:en:Battle of KЕҚan|Battle of KЕҚan]], Tsunesuke fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki|ja|еЈұеІҗеі¶гҒ®жҲҰгҒ„|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Iki|qq=}}, where he expelled the Yuan forces but lost his son, {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Toshizane|qq=}}.
[[File:Mooko-SamuraiShips.jpg|300px|right|thumb|'''ShЕҚni Tsunesuke''' and other samurai ships during the Mongol invasion.
{{small|гҖҺ[[:en:MЕҚko ShЕ«rai Ekotoba|MЕҚko ShЕ«rai Ekotoba]]гҖҸеҫҢе·»гғ»зөө15гғ»з¬¬19зҙҷ}} Despite this tragedy, he continued to serve as a [[:en:Chinzei BugyЕҚ|Chinzei BugyЕҚ]] with {{ill|ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|ja|еӨ§еҸӢй јжі°|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|qq=}}. In 1284, following the death of the eighth {{ill|Shikken|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|ja|Shikken|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|1=Shikken|2=ja|3=еҹ·жЁ©|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikken|qq=}}, [[:en:HЕҚjЕҚ Tokimune|HЕҚjЕҚ Tokimune]], Tsunesuke took Buddhist vows and adopted the Dharma name JЕҚe. During the {{ill|Shimozuki Disturbance|ja|йңңжңҲйЁ’еӢ•|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimozuki Disturbance|qq=}} of 1285, he supported {{ill|Taira no Yoritsuna|ja|е№ій јз¶ұ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Yoritsuna|qq=}} and defeated his brother {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Kagesue|qq=}}, aligning with the ruling {{ill|Tokuso family|ja|еҫ—е®—е®¶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokuso family|qq=}}. This led to a weakening of the ShЕҚni clanвҖҷs influence, as they lost control of several provinces. In 1286, Tsunesuke was appointed to the {{ill|Chinzei Dangi-sho|ja|йҺ®иҘҝи«Үиӯ°жүҖ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinzei Dangi-sho|qq=}} alongside {{ill|ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|ja|еӨ§еҸӢй јжі°|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|qq=}}, {{ill|Utsunomiya Michifusa|ja|е®ҮйғҪе®®йҖҡжҲҝ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Michifusa|qq=}}, and {{ill|Shibuya Shigekuni|ja|жёӢи°·йҮҚйғ·|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Shigekuni|qq=}}. He passed away in 1292. His eldest son {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Toshizane|qq=}} having died in battle, Tsunesuke was succeeded by {{ill|ShЕҚni Moritsune|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣзөҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Moritsune|qq=}}. His younger sons {{ill|ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷӮзөҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Tokitsune|qq=}} and {{ill|ShЕҚni Morishige|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Morishige|qq=}} later founded the {{ill|Shiga clan|ja|еҝ—иіҖж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiga clan|qq=}} and [[:en:Hirai clan|Hirai clan]], respectively. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *[[Motohisa Yasuda]], ed. гҖҺйҺҢеҖүгғ»е®Өз”әдәәеҗҚдәӢе…ё гӮігғігғ‘гӮҜгғҲзүҲгҖҸпјҲ[[Shin Jinbutsu Oraisha]], 1990пјү *[[Hattori Hideo]] гҖҺи’ҷеҸӨиҘІжқҘгҖҸпјҲ[[Yamakawa Publishing]], 2014пјү *[[Kuroita Katsumi]], ed. гҖҺж–°иЁӮеў—иЈңеӣҪеҸІеӨ§зі»гғ»е°ҠеҚ‘еҲҶи„ү з¬¬2зҜҮгҖҸпјҲ[[Yoshikawa KЕҚbunkan]]пјү {{ShЕҚni clan heads}} {{translated page|ja|е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ|version=101501413|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:ShЕҚni clan]] [[Category:Kamakura shogunate retainers]] [[Category:People of the Mongol invasions of Japan]] [[Category:1229 births]] [[Category:1292 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ]] [[ko:%EC%87%BC%EB%8B%88 %EC%93%B0%EB%84%A4%EC%8A%A4%EC%BC%80]] [[sn:ShЕҚni Kagesuke]] [[Category:People of the Mongol invasions of Japan]] [[Category:ShЕҚni clanпҝҪ]] [[Category:Gokenin of the Kamakura shogunate]] {{Commons category|ShЕҚni Tsunesuke}} [[ko:мҮјлӢҲ м“°л„ӨмҠӨмјҖ]]
