Munetsune|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (existence debated)гҖҺжұҺжө·е°ҸйҢІгҖҸгҒ«гҖҢеӨӘе®°и—ӨеҺҹе°‘еҚҝејҹе®—иіҮгҖҚгҒЁиЁҳгҒ•гӮҢгӮӢгҒҢгҖҒж—Ҙжң¬еҒҙеҸІж–ҷгҒ«гҒҜи©ІеҪ“иҖ…гҒҢгҒҠгӮүгҒҡгҖҒи«ёиӘ¬гҒӮгӮӢгҖӮе·қи¶Ҡжі°еҚҡгҖҢжұҺжө·е°ҸйҢІгҒ®ејҳе®үгҒ®еҪ№иЁҳдәӢгҒ«гҒӨгҒ„гҒҰгҖҚгҖҺи»ҚдәӢеҸІеӯҰгҖҸз¬¬ppе·»з¬¬1еҸ·гҖҒ1975th year 6жңҲгҖҒ26-34й ҒгҖӮ | spouse = | children = {{ill|ShЕҚni Moritsune|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣзөҢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Moritsune|1=ShЕҚni Moritsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q93783030}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Toshizane|1=ShЕҚni Suketoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464745}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷӮзөҢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Morishige|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣж°Ҹ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Morishige|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Sadatsune|ja|е°‘ејҗиІһиіҮ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Sadatsune|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | notable = вҖ» Birth and death dates are debated. }} '''ShЕҚni Tsunesuke''' (е°‘ејҗ зөҢиіҮ) was a {{ill|samurai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/samurai|qq=}} and retainer of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura shogunate|qq=}} during the mid-{{ill|Kamakura period|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura period|qq=}}. He was the son of {{ill|ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮиғҪ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|1=ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464746}} and the third head of the {{ill|ShЕҚni clan|en|ShЕҚni clan|WD=Q2975368|lt=ShЕҚni clan}}. == Overview == The character "зөҢ" in Tsunesuke's name was likely granted as a favor by the fourth {{ill|Shikken|en|Shikken|WD=Q1045904|lt=Shikken}}, {{ill|HЕҚjЕҚ Tsunetoki|en|HЕҚjЕҚ Tsunetoki|WD=Q2008076|lt=HЕҚjЕҚ Tokiyori}}, or by {{ill|KujЕҚ Yoritsune|en|KujЕҚ Yoritsune|WD=Q471780|lt=Fujiwara no Yoritsune}}, the fourth {{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|WD=Q131767|lt=Seii Taishogun}}, who gave the same character to Tokiyori. This suggests Tsunesuke underwent his coming-of-age ceremony (genpuku) between 1245 and 1246. There are varying theories regarding Tsunesuke's birth and death years. One theory suggests he was born in 1229 and died in 1292, while another suggests 1226 to 1289.[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B0%91%E5%BC%90%E7%B5%8C%E8%B3%87-1082512 гӮігғҲгғҗгғігӮҜ_е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ] However, as genpuku typically occurred in oneвҖҷs teens, the 1229 birth year is widely considered accurate. Tsunesuke shared responsibilities with his father in governing northern Kyushu and played a crucial role during the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|en|Mongol invasions of Japan|WD=Q208156|lt=Mongol invasions of Japan}}. He oversaw the fortification of defenses, such as the {{ill|GenkЕҚ BЕҚrui|en|GenkЕҚ BЕҚrui|WD=Q2154769|lt=Ishizumi walls}}, and coordinated efforts against the Yuan forces. His earliest documented activity appears in a record from 1269.жңҚйғЁгҖҒ2014th year гҖҒP.416гҖӮе…ёжӢ гҒҜгҖҺжӯҰйӣ„зҘһзӨҫж–ҮжӣёгҖҸпјҲгҖҺйҺҢеҖүйҒәж–ҮгҖҸз¬¬14е·»гғ»10503еҸ·ж–ҮжӣёпјүгҖӮ In 1274, during the {{ill|Battle of Bun'ei|en|Battle of Bun'ei|WD=Q2237386|lt=Battle of Bun'ei}}, Tsunesuke's brother {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Kagesue|1=ShЕҚni Kagesuke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10263912}} led the Japanese forces. Tsunesuke's role during this campaign is unclear. By 1275, he inherited the familyвҖҷs headship from his father. In 1281, during the {{ill|Battle of KЕҚan|en|Battle of KЕҚan|WD=Q2237622|lt=Battle of KЕҚan}}, Tsunesuke fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki|ja|еЈұеІҗеі¶гҒ®жҲҰгҒ„|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Iki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, where he expelled the Yuan forces but lost his son, {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Toshizane|1=ShЕҚni Suketoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464745}}.
[[File:Mooko-SamuraiShips.jpg|300px|right|thumb|'''ShЕҚni Tsunesuke''' and other samurai ships during the Mongol invasion.
{{small|гҖҺ{{ill|MЕҚko ShЕ«rai Ekotoba|en|MЕҚko ShЕ«rai Ekotoba|WD=Q3087790|lt=MЕҚko ShЕ«rai Ekotoba}}гҖҸеҫҢе·»гғ»зөө15гғ»з¬¬19зҙҷ}} Despite this tragedy, he continued to serve as a {{ill|Chinzei BugyЕҚ|en|Chinzei BugyЕҚ|WD=Q440978|lt=Chinzei BugyЕҚ}} with {{ill|ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|ja|еӨ§еҸӢй јжі°|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|1=ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433274}}. In 1284, following the death of the eighth {{ill|Shikken|en|Shikken|WD=Q1045904|lt=Shikken}}, {{ill|HЕҚjЕҚ Tokimune|en|HЕҚjЕҚ Tokimune|WD=Q430964|lt=HЕҚjЕҚ Tokimune}}, Tsunesuke took Buddhist vows and adopted the Dharma name JЕҚe. During the {{ill|Shimozuki Disturbance|ja|йңңжңҲйЁ’еӢ•|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimozuki Disturbance|1=Shimotsuki incident|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10565914}} of 1285, he supported {{ill|Taira no Yoritsuna|ja|е№ій јз¶ұ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Yoritsuna|1=Taira no Yoritsuna|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483787}} and defeated his brother {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Kagesue|1=ShЕҚni Kagesuke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10263912}}, aligning with the ruling {{ill|Tokuso family|ja|еҫ—е®—е®¶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokuso family|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to еҫ—е®—|1=TokusЕҚ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1759237}}. This led to a weakening of the ShЕҚni clanвҖҷs influence, as they lost control of several provinces. In 1286, Tsunesuke was appointed to the {{ill|Chinzei Dangi-sho|ja|йҺ®иҘҝи«Үиӯ°жүҖ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinzei Dangi-sho|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to йҺ®иҘҝжҺўйЎҢ|1=Chinzei tandai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8191592}} alongside {{ill|ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|ja|еӨ§еҸӢй јжі°|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|1=ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433274}}, {{ill|Utsunomiya Michifusa|ja|е®ҮйғҪе®®йҖҡжҲҝ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Michifusa|1=Utsunomiya Michifusa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119380431}}, and {{ill|Shibuya Shigekuni|ja|жёӢи°·йҮҚйғ·|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Shigekuni|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. He passed away in 1292. His eldest son {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Toshizane|1=ShЕҚni Suketoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464745}} having died in battle, Tsunesuke was succeeded by {{ill|ShЕҚni Moritsune|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣзөҢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Moritsune|1=ShЕҚni Moritsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q93783030}}. His younger sons {{ill|ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷӮзөҢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|ShЕҚni Morishige|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣж°Ҹ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Morishige|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} later founded the {{ill|Shiga clan|ja|еҝ—иіҖж°Ҹ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiga clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Hirai clan|en|Hirai clan|WD=Q29526001|lt=Hirai clan}}, respectively. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *[[Motohisa Yasuda]], ed. гҖҺйҺҢеҖүгғ»е®Өз”әдәәеҗҚдәӢе…ё гӮігғігғ‘гӮҜгғҲзүҲгҖҸпјҲ[[Shin Jinbutsu Oraisha]], 1990пјү *[[Hattori Hideo]] гҖҺи’ҷеҸӨиҘІжқҘгҖҸпјҲ[[Yamakawa Publishing]], 2014пјү *[[Kuroita Katsumi]], ed. гҖҺж–°иЁӮеў—иЈңеӣҪеҸІеӨ§зі»гғ»е°ҠеҚ‘еҲҶи„ү з¬¬2зҜҮгҖҸпјҲ[[Yoshikawa KЕҚbunkan]]пјү {{ShЕҚni clan heads}} {{translated page|ja|е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ|version=101501413|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ]] [[ko:%EC%87%BC%EB%8B%88 %EC%93%B0%EB%84%A4%EC%8A%A4%EC%BC%80]] {{Commons category|ShЕҚni Tsunesuke}} [[ko:мҮјлӢҲ м“°л„ӨмҠӨмјҖ]] {{wikidata link|Q11464740}} [[Category:1229 births]] [[Category:1292 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Gokenin of the Kamakura shogunate]] [[Category:People of the Mongol invasions of Japan]] [[Category:ShЕҚni clan]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]]

wikidata|WD=Q93783030}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Toshizane|1=ShЕҚni Suketoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464745}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷӮзөҢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Morishige|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣж°Ҹ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Morishige|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Sadatsune|ja|е°‘ејҗиІһиіҮ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Sadatsune|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | notable = вҖ» Birth and death dates are debated. }} '''ShЕҚni Tsunesuke''' (е°‘ејҗ зөҢиіҮ) was a {{ill|samurai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/samurai|qq=}} and retainer of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura shogunate|qq=}} during the mid-{{ill|Kamakura period|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura period|qq=}}. He was the son of {{ill|ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮиғҪ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|1=ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464746}} and the third head of the {{ill|ShЕҚni clan|en|ShЕҚni clan|WD=Q2975368|lt=ShЕҚni clan}}. == Overview == The character "зөҢ" in Tsunesuke's name was likely granted as a favor by the fourth {{ill|Shikken|en|Shikken|WD=Q1045904|lt=Shikken}}, {{ill|HЕҚjЕҚ Tsunetoki|en|HЕҚjЕҚ Tsunetoki|WD=Q2008076|lt=HЕҚjЕҚ Tokiyori}}, or by {{ill|KujЕҚ Yoritsune|en|KujЕҚ Yoritsune|WD=Q471780|lt=Fujiwara no Yoritsune}}, the fourth {{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|WD=Q131767|lt=Seii Taishogun}}, who gave the same character to Tokiyori. This suggests Tsunesuke underwent his coming-of-age ceremony (genpuku) between 1245 and 1246. There are varying theories regarding Tsunesuke's birth and death years. One theory suggests he was born in 1229 and died in 1292, while another suggests 1226 to 1289.[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B0%91%E5%BC%90%E7%B5%8C%E8%B3%87-1082512 гӮігғҲгғҗгғігӮҜ_е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ] However, as genpuku typically occurred in oneвҖҷs teens, the 1229 birth year is widely considered accurate. Tsunesuke shared responsibilities with his father in governing northern Kyushu and played a crucial role during the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|en|Mongol invasions of Japan|WD=Q208156|lt=Mongol invasions of Japan}}. He oversaw the fortification of defenses, such as the {{ill|GenkЕҚ BЕҚrui|en|GenkЕҚ BЕҚrui|WD=Q2154769|lt=Ishizumi walls}}, and coordinated efforts against the Yuan forces. His earliest documented activity appears in a record from 1269.жңҚйғЁгҖҒ2014th year гҖҒP.416гҖӮе…ёжӢ гҒҜгҖҺжӯҰйӣ„зҘһзӨҫж–ҮжӣёгҖҸпјҲгҖҺйҺҢеҖүйҒәж–ҮгҖҸз¬¬14е·»гғ»10503еҸ·ж–ҮжӣёпјүгҖӮ In 1274, during the {{ill|Battle of Bun'ei|en|Battle of Bun'ei|WD=Q2237386|lt=Battle of Bun'ei}}, Tsunesuke's brother {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Kagesue|1=ShЕҚni Kagesuke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10263912}} led the Japanese forces. Tsunesuke's role during this campaign is unclear. By 1275, he inherited the familyвҖҷs headship from his father. In 1281, during the {{ill|Battle of KЕҚan|en|Battle of KЕҚan|WD=Q2237622|lt=Battle of KЕҚan}}, Tsunesuke fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki|ja|еЈұеІҗеі¶гҒ®жҲҰгҒ„|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Iki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, where he expelled the Yuan forces but lost his son, {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Toshizane|1=ShЕҚni Suketoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464745}}.
[[File:Mooko-SamuraiShips.jpg|300px|right|thumb|'''ShЕҚni Tsunesuke''' and other samurai ships during the Mongol invasion.
{{small|гҖҺ{{ill|MЕҚko ShЕ«rai Ekotoba|en|MЕҚko ShЕ«rai Ekotoba|WD=Q3087790|lt=MЕҚko ShЕ«rai Ekotoba}}гҖҸеҫҢе·»гғ»зөө15гғ»з¬¬19зҙҷ}} Despite this tragedy, he continued to serve as a {{ill|Chinzei BugyЕҚ|en|Chinzei BugyЕҚ|WD=Q440978|lt=Chinzei BugyЕҚ}} with {{ill|ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|ja|еӨ§еҸӢй јжі°|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|1=ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433274}}. In 1284, following the death of the eighth {{ill|Shikken|en|Shikken|WD=Q1045904|lt=Shikken}}, {{ill|HЕҚjЕҚ Tokimune|en|HЕҚjЕҚ Tokimune|WD=Q430964|lt=HЕҚjЕҚ Tokimune}}, Tsunesuke took Buddhist vows and adopted the Dharma name JЕҚe. During the {{ill|Shimozuki Disturbance|ja|йңңжңҲйЁ’еӢ•|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimozuki Disturbance|1=Shimotsuki incident|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10565914}} of 1285, he supported {{ill|Taira no Yoritsuna|ja|е№ій јз¶ұ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Yoritsuna|1=Taira no Yoritsuna|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483787}} and defeated his brother {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Kagesue|1=ShЕҚni Kagesuke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10263912}}, aligning with the ruling {{ill|Tokuso family|ja|еҫ—е®—е®¶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokuso family|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to еҫ—е®—|1=TokusЕҚ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1759237}}. This led to a weakening of the ShЕҚni clanвҖҷs influence, as they lost control of several provinces. In 1286, Tsunesuke was appointed to the {{ill|Chinzei Dangi-sho|ja|йҺ®иҘҝи«Үиӯ°жүҖ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinzei Dangi-sho|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to йҺ®иҘҝжҺўйЎҢ|1=Chinzei tandai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8191592}} alongside {{ill|ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|ja|еӨ§еҸӢй јжі°|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|1=ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433274}}, {{ill|Utsunomiya Michifusa|ja|е®ҮйғҪе®®йҖҡжҲҝ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Michifusa|1=Utsunomiya Michifusa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119380431}}, and {{ill|Shibuya Shigekuni|ja|жёӢи°·йҮҚйғ·|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Shigekuni|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. He passed away in 1292. His eldest son {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Toshizane|1=ShЕҚni Suketoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464745}} having died in battle, Tsunesuke was succeeded by {{ill|ShЕҚni Moritsune|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣзөҢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Moritsune|1=ShЕҚni Moritsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q93783030}}. His younger sons {{ill|ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷӮзөҢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|ShЕҚni Morishige|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣж°Ҹ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Morishige|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} later founded the {{ill|Shiga clan|ja|еҝ—иіҖж°Ҹ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiga clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Hirai clan|en|Hirai clan|WD=Q29526001|lt=Hirai clan}}, respectively. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *[[Motohisa Yasuda]], ed. гҖҺйҺҢеҖүгғ»е®Өз”әдәәеҗҚдәӢе…ё гӮігғігғ‘гӮҜгғҲзүҲгҖҸпјҲ[[Shin Jinbutsu Oraisha]], 1990пјү *[[Hattori Hideo]] гҖҺи’ҷеҸӨиҘІжқҘгҖҸпјҲ[[Yamakawa Publishing]], 2014пјү *[[Kuroita Katsumi]], ed. гҖҺж–°иЁӮеў—иЈңеӣҪеҸІеӨ§зі»гғ»е°ҠеҚ‘еҲҶи„ү з¬¬2зҜҮгҖҸпјҲ[[Yoshikawa KЕҚbunkan]]пјү {{ShЕҚni clan heads}} {{translated page|ja|е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ|version=101501413|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ]] [[ko:%EC%87%BC%EB%8B%88 %EC%93%B0%EB%84%A4%EC%8A%A4%EC%BC%80]] {{Commons category|ShЕҚni Tsunesuke}} [[ko:мҮјлӢҲ м“°л„ӨмҠӨмјҖ]] {{wikidata link|Q11464740}} [[Category:1229 births]] [[Category:1292 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Gokenin of the Kamakura shogunate]] [[Category:People of the Mongol invasions of Japan]] [[Category:ShЕҚni clan]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]]

are debated. }} '''ShЕҚni Tsunesuke''' (е°‘ејҗ зөҢиіҮ) was a {{ill|samurai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/samurai|qq=}} and retainer of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura shogunate|qq=}} during the mid-{{ill|Kamakura period|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura period|qq=}}. He was the son of {{ill|ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮиғҪ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|1=ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464746}} and the third head of the {{ill|ShЕҚni clan|en|ShЕҚni clan|WD=Q2975368|lt=ShЕҚni clan}}. == Overview == The character "зөҢ" in Tsunesuke's name was likely granted as a favor by the fourth {{ill|Shikken|en|Shikken|WD=Q1045904|lt=Shikken}}, {{ill|HЕҚjЕҚ Tsunetoki|en|HЕҚjЕҚ Tsunetoki|WD=Q2008076|lt=HЕҚjЕҚ Tokiyori}}, or by {{ill|KujЕҚ Yoritsune|en|KujЕҚ Yoritsune|WD=Q471780|lt=Fujiwara no Yoritsune}}, the fourth {{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|WD=Q131767|lt=Seii Taishogun}}, who gave the same character to Tokiyori. This suggests Tsunesuke underwent his coming-of-age ceremony (genpuku) between 1245 and 1246. There are varying theories regarding Tsunesuke's birth and death years. One theory suggests he was born in 1229 and died in 1292, while another suggests 1226 to 1289.[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B0%91%E5%BC%90%E7%B5%8C%E8%B3%87-1082512 гӮігғҲгғҗгғігӮҜ_е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ] However, as genpuku typically occurred in oneвҖҷs teens, the 1229 birth year is widely considered accurate. Tsunesuke shared responsibilities with his father in governing northern Kyushu and played a crucial role during the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|en|Mongol invasions of Japan|WD=Q208156|lt=Mongol invasions of Japan}}. He oversaw the fortification of defenses, such as the {{ill|GenkЕҚ BЕҚrui|en|GenkЕҚ BЕҚrui|WD=Q2154769|lt=Ishizumi walls}}, and coordinated efforts against the Yuan forces. His earliest documented activity appears in a record from 1269.жңҚйғЁгҖҒ2014th year гҖҒP.416гҖӮе…ёжӢ гҒҜгҖҺжӯҰйӣ„зҘһзӨҫж–ҮжӣёгҖҸпјҲгҖҺйҺҢеҖүйҒәж–ҮгҖҸз¬¬14е·»гғ»10503еҸ·ж–ҮжӣёпјүгҖӮ In 1274, during the {{ill|Battle of Bun'ei|en|Battle of Bun'ei|WD=Q2237386|lt=Battle of Bun'ei}}, Tsunesuke's brother {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Kagesue|1=ShЕҚni Kagesuke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10263912}} led the Japanese forces. Tsunesuke's role during this campaign is unclear. By 1275, he inherited the familyвҖҷs headship from his father. In 1281, during the {{ill|Battle of KЕҚan|en|Battle of KЕҚan|WD=Q2237622|lt=Battle of KЕҚan}}, Tsunesuke fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki|ja|еЈұеІҗеі¶гҒ®жҲҰгҒ„|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Iki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, where he expelled the Yuan forces but lost his son, {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Toshizane|1=ShЕҚni Suketoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464745}}.
[[File:Mooko-SamuraiShips.jpg|300px|right|thumb|'''ShЕҚni Tsunesuke''' and other samurai ships during the Mongol invasion.
{{small|гҖҺ{{ill|MЕҚko ShЕ«rai Ekotoba|en|MЕҚko ShЕ«rai Ekotoba|WD=Q3087790|lt=MЕҚko ShЕ«rai Ekotoba}}гҖҸеҫҢе·»гғ»зөө15гғ»з¬¬19зҙҷ}} Despite this tragedy, he continued to serve as a {{ill|Chinzei BugyЕҚ|en|Chinzei BugyЕҚ|WD=Q440978|lt=Chinzei BugyЕҚ}} with {{ill|ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|ja|еӨ§еҸӢй јжі°|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|1=ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433274}}. In 1284, following the death of the eighth {{ill|Shikken|en|Shikken|WD=Q1045904|lt=Shikken}}, {{ill|HЕҚjЕҚ Tokimune|en|HЕҚjЕҚ Tokimune|WD=Q430964|lt=HЕҚjЕҚ Tokimune}}, Tsunesuke took Buddhist vows and adopted the Dharma name JЕҚe. During the {{ill|Shimozuki Disturbance|ja|йңңжңҲйЁ’еӢ•|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimozuki Disturbance|1=Shimotsuki incident|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10565914}} of 1285, he supported {{ill|Taira no Yoritsuna|ja|е№ій јз¶ұ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Yoritsuna|1=Taira no Yoritsuna|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483787}} and defeated his brother {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Kagesue|1=ShЕҚni Kagesuke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10263912}}, aligning with the ruling {{ill|Tokuso family|ja|еҫ—е®—е®¶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokuso family|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to еҫ—е®—|1=TokusЕҚ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1759237}}. This led to a weakening of the ShЕҚni clanвҖҷs influence, as they lost control of several provinces. In 1286, Tsunesuke was appointed to the {{ill|Chinzei Dangi-sho|ja|йҺ®иҘҝи«Үиӯ°жүҖ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinzei Dangi-sho|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to йҺ®иҘҝжҺўйЎҢ|1=Chinzei tandai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8191592}} alongside {{ill|ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|ja|еӨ§еҸӢй јжі°|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|1=ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433274}}, {{ill|Utsunomiya Michifusa|ja|е®ҮйғҪе®®йҖҡжҲҝ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Michifusa|1=Utsunomiya Michifusa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119380431}}, and {{ill|Shibuya Shigekuni|ja|жёӢи°·йҮҚйғ·|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Shigekuni|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. He passed away in 1292. His eldest son {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Toshizane|1=ShЕҚni Suketoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464745}} having died in battle, Tsunesuke was succeeded by {{ill|ShЕҚni Moritsune|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣзөҢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Moritsune|1=ShЕҚni Moritsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q93783030}}. His younger sons {{ill|ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷӮзөҢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|ShЕҚni Morishige|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣж°Ҹ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Morishige|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} later founded the {{ill|Shiga clan|ja|еҝ—иіҖж°Ҹ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiga clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Hirai clan|en|Hirai clan|WD=Q29526001|lt=Hirai clan}}, respectively. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *[[Motohisa Yasuda]], ed. гҖҺйҺҢеҖүгғ»е®Өз”әдәәеҗҚдәӢе…ё гӮігғігғ‘гӮҜгғҲзүҲгҖҸпјҲ[[Shin Jinbutsu Oraisha]], 1990пјү *[[Hattori Hideo]] гҖҺи’ҷеҸӨиҘІжқҘгҖҸпјҲ[[Yamakawa Publishing]], 2014пјү *[[Kuroita Katsumi]], ed. гҖҺж–°иЁӮеў—иЈңеӣҪеҸІеӨ§зі»гғ»е°ҠеҚ‘еҲҶи„ү з¬¬2зҜҮгҖҸпјҲ[[Yoshikawa KЕҚbunkan]]пјү {{ShЕҚni clan heads}} {{translated page|ja|е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ|version=101501413|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ]] [[ko:%EC%87%BC%EB%8B%88 %EC%93%B0%EB%84%A4%EC%8A%A4%EC%BC%80]] {{Commons category|ShЕҚni Tsunesuke}} [[ko:мҮјлӢҲ м“°л„ӨмҠӨмјҖ]] {{wikidata link|Q11464740}} [[Category:1229 births]] [[Category:1292 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Gokenin of the Kamakura shogunate]] [[Category:People of the Mongol invasions of Japan]] [[Category:ShЕҚni clan]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]]

debated)гҖҺжұҺжө·е°ҸйҢІгҖҸгҒ«гҖҢеӨӘе®°и—ӨеҺҹе°‘еҚҝејҹе®—иіҮгҖҚгҒЁиЁҳгҒ•гӮҢгӮӢгҒҢгҖҒж—Ҙжң¬еҒҙеҸІж–ҷгҒ«гҒҜи©ІеҪ“иҖ…гҒҢгҒҠгӮүгҒҡгҖҒи«ёиӘ¬гҒӮгӮӢгҖӮе·қи¶Ҡжі°еҚҡгҖҢжұҺжө·е°ҸйҢІгҒ®ејҳе®үгҒ®еҪ№иЁҳдәӢгҒ«гҒӨгҒ„гҒҰгҖҚгҖҺи»ҚдәӢеҸІеӯҰгҖҸз¬¬ppе·»з¬¬1еҸ·гҖҒ1975th year 6жңҲгҖҒ26-34й ҒгҖӮ | spouse = | children = {{ill|ShЕҚni Moritsune|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣзөҢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Moritsune|1=ShЕҚni Moritsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q93783030}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Toshizane|1=ShЕҚni Suketoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464745}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷӮзөҢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Morishige|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣж°Ҹ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Morishige|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Sadatsune|ja|е°‘ејҗиІһиіҮ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Sadatsune|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | notable = вҖ» Birth and death dates are debated. }} '''ShЕҚni Tsunesuke''' (е°‘ејҗ зөҢиіҮ) was a {{ill|samurai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/samurai|qq=}} and retainer of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura shogunate|qq=}} during the mid-{{ill|Kamakura period|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura period|qq=}}. He was the son of {{ill|ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮиғҪ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|1=ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464746}} and the third head of the {{ill|ShЕҚni clan|en|ShЕҚni clan|WD=Q2975368|lt=ShЕҚni clan}}. == Overview == The character "зөҢ" in Tsunesuke's name was likely granted as a favor by the fourth {{ill|Shikken|en|Shikken|WD=Q1045904|lt=Shikken}}, {{ill|HЕҚjЕҚ Tsunetoki|en|HЕҚjЕҚ Tsunetoki|WD=Q2008076|lt=HЕҚjЕҚ Tokiyori}}, or by {{ill|KujЕҚ Yoritsune|en|KujЕҚ Yoritsune|WD=Q471780|lt=Fujiwara no Yoritsune}}, the fourth {{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|WD=Q131767|lt=Seii Taishogun}}, who gave the same character to Tokiyori. This suggests Tsunesuke underwent his coming-of-age ceremony (genpuku) between 1245 and 1246. There are varying theories regarding Tsunesuke's birth and death years. One theory suggests he was born in 1229 and died in 1292, while another suggests 1226 to 1289.[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B0%91%E5%BC%90%E7%B5%8C%E8%B3%87-1082512 гӮігғҲгғҗгғігӮҜ_е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ] However, as genpuku typically occurred in oneвҖҷs teens, the 1229 birth year is widely considered accurate. Tsunesuke shared responsibilities with his father in governing northern Kyushu and played a crucial role during the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|en|Mongol invasions of Japan|WD=Q208156|lt=Mongol invasions of Japan}}. He oversaw the fortification of defenses, such as the {{ill|GenkЕҚ BЕҚrui|en|GenkЕҚ BЕҚrui|WD=Q2154769|lt=Ishizumi walls}}, and coordinated efforts against the Yuan forces. His earliest documented activity appears in a record from 1269.жңҚйғЁгҖҒ2014th year гҖҒP.416гҖӮе…ёжӢ гҒҜгҖҺжӯҰйӣ„зҘһзӨҫж–ҮжӣёгҖҸпјҲгҖҺйҺҢеҖүйҒәж–ҮгҖҸз¬¬14е·»гғ»10503еҸ·ж–ҮжӣёпјүгҖӮ In 1274, during the {{ill|Battle of Bun'ei|en|Battle of Bun'ei|WD=Q2237386|lt=Battle of Bun'ei}}, Tsunesuke's brother {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Kagesue|1=ShЕҚni Kagesuke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10263912}} led the Japanese forces. Tsunesuke's role during this campaign is unclear. By 1275, he inherited the familyвҖҷs headship from his father. In 1281, during the {{ill|Battle of KЕҚan|en|Battle of KЕҚan|WD=Q2237622|lt=Battle of KЕҚan}}, Tsunesuke fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki|ja|еЈұеІҗеі¶гҒ®жҲҰгҒ„|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Iki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, where he expelled the Yuan forces but lost his son, {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Toshizane|1=ShЕҚni Suketoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464745}}.
[[File:Mooko-SamuraiShips.jpg|300px|right|thumb|'''ShЕҚni Tsunesuke''' and other samurai ships during the Mongol invasion.
{{small|гҖҺ{{ill|MЕҚko ShЕ«rai Ekotoba|en|MЕҚko ShЕ«rai Ekotoba|WD=Q3087790|lt=MЕҚko ShЕ«rai Ekotoba}}гҖҸеҫҢе·»гғ»зөө15гғ»з¬¬19зҙҷ}} Despite this tragedy, he continued to serve as a {{ill|Chinzei BugyЕҚ|en|Chinzei BugyЕҚ|WD=Q440978|lt=Chinzei BugyЕҚ}} with {{ill|ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|ja|еӨ§еҸӢй јжі°|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|1=ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433274}}. In 1284, following the death of the eighth {{ill|Shikken|en|Shikken|WD=Q1045904|lt=Shikken}}, {{ill|HЕҚjЕҚ Tokimune|en|HЕҚjЕҚ Tokimune|WD=Q430964|lt=HЕҚjЕҚ Tokimune}}, Tsunesuke took Buddhist vows and adopted the Dharma name JЕҚe. During the {{ill|Shimozuki Disturbance|ja|йңңжңҲйЁ’еӢ•|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimozuki Disturbance|1=Shimotsuki incident|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10565914}} of 1285, he supported {{ill|Taira no Yoritsuna|ja|е№ій јз¶ұ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Yoritsuna|1=Taira no Yoritsuna|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483787}} and defeated his brother {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Kagesue|1=ShЕҚni Kagesuke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10263912}}, aligning with the ruling {{ill|Tokuso family|ja|еҫ—е®—е®¶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokuso family|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to еҫ—е®—|1=TokusЕҚ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1759237}}. This led to a weakening of the ShЕҚni clanвҖҷs influence, as they lost control of several provinces. In 1286, Tsunesuke was appointed to the {{ill|Chinzei Dangi-sho|ja|йҺ®иҘҝи«Үиӯ°жүҖ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinzei Dangi-sho|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to йҺ®иҘҝжҺўйЎҢ|1=Chinzei tandai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8191592}} alongside {{ill|ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|ja|еӨ§еҸӢй јжі°|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|1=ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433274}}, {{ill|Utsunomiya Michifusa|ja|е®ҮйғҪе®®йҖҡжҲҝ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Michifusa|1=Utsunomiya Michifusa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119380431}}, and {{ill|Shibuya Shigekuni|ja|жёӢи°·йҮҚйғ·|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Shigekuni|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. He passed away in 1292. His eldest son {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Toshizane|1=ShЕҚni Suketoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464745}} having died in battle, Tsunesuke was succeeded by {{ill|ShЕҚni Moritsune|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣзөҢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Moritsune|1=ShЕҚni Moritsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q93783030}}. His younger sons {{ill|ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷӮзөҢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|ShЕҚni Morishige|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣж°Ҹ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Morishige|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} later founded the {{ill|Shiga clan|ja|еҝ—иіҖж°Ҹ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiga clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Hirai clan|en|Hirai clan|WD=Q29526001|lt=Hirai clan}}, respectively. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *[[Motohisa Yasuda]], ed. гҖҺйҺҢеҖүгғ»е®Өз”әдәәеҗҚдәӢе…ё гӮігғігғ‘гӮҜгғҲзүҲгҖҸпјҲ[[Shin Jinbutsu Oraisha]], 1990пјү *[[Hattori Hideo]] гҖҺи’ҷеҸӨиҘІжқҘгҖҸпјҲ[[Yamakawa Publishing]], 2014пјү *[[Kuroita Katsumi]], ed. гҖҺж–°иЁӮеў—иЈңеӣҪеҸІеӨ§зі»гғ»е°ҠеҚ‘еҲҶи„ү з¬¬2зҜҮгҖҸпјҲ[[Yoshikawa KЕҚbunkan]]пјү {{ShЕҚni clan heads}} {{translated page|ja|е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ|version=101501413|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ]] [[ko:%EC%87%BC%EB%8B%88 %EC%93%B0%EB%84%A4%EC%8A%A4%EC%BC%80]] {{Commons category|ShЕҚni Tsunesuke}} [[ko:мҮјлӢҲ м“°л„ӨмҠӨмјҖ]] {{wikidata link|Q11464740}} [[Category:1229 births]] [[Category:1292 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Gokenin of the Kamakura shogunate]] [[Category:People of the Mongol invasions of Japan]] [[Category:ShЕҚni clan]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]] [[Category:pages]]

are debated. }} '''ShЕҚni Tsunesuke''' (е°‘ејҗ зөҢиіҮ) was a {{ill|samurai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/samurai|qq=}} and retainer of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura shogunate|qq=}} during the mid-{{ill|Kamakura period|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura period|qq=}}. He was the son of {{ill|ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮиғҪ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|1=ShЕҚni Sukenaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464746}} and the third head of the {{ill|ShЕҚni clan|en|ShЕҚni clan|WD=Q2975368|lt=ShЕҚni clan|1=ShЕҚni clan}}. == Overview == The character "зөҢ" in Tsunesuke's name was likely granted as a favor by the fourth {{ill|Shikken|en|Shikken|WD=Q1045904|lt=Shikken|1=Shikken}}, {{ill|HЕҚjЕҚ Tsunetoki|en|HЕҚjЕҚ Tsunetoki|WD=Q2008076|lt=HЕҚjЕҚ Tokiyori|1=HЕҚjЕҚ Tsunetoki}}, or by {{ill|KujЕҚ Yoritsune|en|KujЕҚ Yoritsune|WD=Q471780|lt=Fujiwara no Yoritsune|1=KujЕҚ Yoritsune}}, the fourth {{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|WD=Q131767|lt=Seii Taishogun|1=Shogun}}, who gave the same character to Tokiyori. This suggests Tsunesuke underwent his coming-of-age ceremony (genpuku) between 1245 and 1246. There are varying theories regarding Tsunesuke's birth and death years. One theory suggests he was born in 1229 and died in 1292, while another suggests 1226 to 1289.[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B0%91%E5%BC%90%E7%B5%8C%E8%B3%87-1082512 гӮігғҲгғҗгғігӮҜ_е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ] However, as genpuku typically occurred in oneвҖҷs teens, the 1229 birth year is widely considered accurate. Tsunesuke shared responsibilities with his father in governing northern Kyushu and played a crucial role during the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|en|Mongol invasions of Japan|WD=Q208156|lt=Mongol invasions of Japan|1=Mongol invasions of Japan}}. He oversaw the fortification of defenses, such as the {{ill|GenkЕҚ BЕҚrui|en|GenkЕҚ BЕҚrui|WD=Q2154769|lt=Ishizumi walls|1=GenkЕҚ BЕҚrui}}, and coordinated efforts against the Yuan forces. His earliest documented activity appears in a record from 1269.жңҚйғЁгҖҒ2014th year гҖҒP.416гҖӮе…ёжӢ гҒҜгҖҺжӯҰйӣ„зҘһзӨҫж–ҮжӣёгҖҸпјҲгҖҺйҺҢеҖүйҒәж–ҮгҖҸз¬¬14е·»гғ»10503еҸ·ж–ҮжӣёпјүгҖӮ In 1274, during the {{ill|Battle of Bun'ei|en|Battle of Bun'ei|WD=Q2237386|lt=Battle of Bun'ei|1=Battle of Bun'ei}}, Tsunesuke's brother {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Kagesue|1=ShЕҚni Kagesuke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10263912}} led the Japanese forces. Tsunesuke's role during this campaign is unclear. By 1275, he inherited the familyвҖҷs headship from his father. In 1281, during the {{ill|Battle of KЕҚan|en|Battle of KЕҚan|WD=Q2237622|lt=Battle of KЕҚan|1=Battle of KЕҚan}}, Tsunesuke fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki|ja|еЈұеІҗеі¶гҒ®жҲҰгҒ„|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Iki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, where he expelled the Yuan forces but lost his son, {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Toshizane|1=ShЕҚni Suketoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464745}}.
[[File:Mooko-SamuraiShips.jpg|300px|right|thumb|'''ShЕҚni Tsunesuke''' and other samurai ships during the Mongol invasion.
{{small|гҖҺ{{ill|MЕҚko ShЕ«rai Ekotoba|en|MЕҚko ShЕ«rai Ekotoba|WD=Q3087790|lt=MЕҚko ShЕ«rai Ekotoba|1=MЕҚko ShЕ«rai Ekotoba}}гҖҸеҫҢе·»гғ»зөө15гғ»з¬¬19зҙҷ}} Despite this tragedy, he continued to serve as a {{ill|Chinzei BugyЕҚ|en|Chinzei BugyЕҚ|WD=Q440978|lt=Chinzei BugyЕҚ|1=Chinzei BugyЕҚ}} with {{ill|ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|ja|еӨ§еҸӢй јжі°|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|1=ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433274}}. In 1284, following the death of the eighth {{ill|Shikken|en|Shikken|WD=Q1045904|lt=Shikken|1=Shikken}}, {{ill|HЕҚjЕҚ Tokimune|en|HЕҚjЕҚ Tokimune|WD=Q430964|lt=HЕҚjЕҚ Tokimune|1=HЕҚjЕҚ Tokimune}}, Tsunesuke took Buddhist vows and adopted the Dharma name JЕҚe. During the {{ill|Shimozuki Disturbance|ja|йңңжңҲйЁ’еӢ•|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimozuki Disturbance|1=Shimotsuki incident|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10565914}} of 1285, he supported {{ill|Taira no Yoritsuna|ja|е№ій јз¶ұ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Yoritsuna|1=Taira no Yoritsuna|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483787}} and defeated his brother {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Kagesue|1=ShЕҚni Kagesuke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10263912}}, aligning with the ruling {{ill|Tokuso family|ja|еҫ—е®—е®¶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokuso family|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to еҫ—е®—|1=TokusЕҚ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1759237}}. This led to a weakening of the ShЕҚni clanвҖҷs influence, as they lost control of several provinces. In 1286, Tsunesuke was appointed to the {{ill|Chinzei Dangi-sho|ja|йҺ®иҘҝи«Үиӯ°жүҖ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinzei Dangi-sho|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to йҺ®иҘҝжҺўйЎҢ|1=Chinzei tandai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8191592}} alongside {{ill|ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|ja|еӨ§еҸӢй јжі°|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|1=ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433274}}, {{ill|Utsunomiya Michifusa|ja|е®ҮйғҪе®®йҖҡжҲҝ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Michifusa|1=Q119380431|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119380431}}, and {{ill|Shibuya Shigekuni|ja|жёӢи°·йҮҚйғ·|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Shigekuni|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. He passed away in 1292. His eldest son {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Toshizane|1=ShЕҚni Suketoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464745}} having died in battle, Tsunesuke was succeeded by {{ill|ShЕҚni Moritsune|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣзөҢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Moritsune|1=ShЕҚni Moritsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q93783030}}. His younger sons {{ill|ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷӮзөҢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|ShЕҚni Morishige|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣж°Ҹ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Morishige|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} later founded the {{ill|Shiga clan|ja|еҝ—иіҖж°Ҹ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiga clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Hirai clan|en|Hirai clan|WD=Q29526001|lt=Hirai clan|1=Hirai clan}}, respectively. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *[[Motohisa Yasuda]], ed. гҖҺйҺҢеҖүгғ»е®Өз”әдәәеҗҚдәӢе…ё гӮігғігғ‘гӮҜгғҲзүҲгҖҸпјҲ[[Shin Jinbutsu Oraisha]], 1990пјү *[[Hattori Hideo]] гҖҺи’ҷеҸӨиҘІжқҘгҖҸпјҲ[[Yamakawa Publishing]], 2014пјү *[[Kuroita Katsumi]], ed. гҖҺж–°иЁӮеў—иЈңеӣҪеҸІеӨ§зі»гғ»е°ҠеҚ‘еҲҶи„ү з¬¬2зҜҮгҖҸпјҲ[[Yoshikawa KЕҚbunkan]]пјү {{ShЕҚni clan heads}} {{translated page|ja|е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ|version=101501413|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ]] [[ko:%EC%87%BC%EB%8B%88 %EC%93%B0%EB%84%A4%EC%8A%A4%EC%BC%80]] {{Commons category|ShЕҚni Tsunesuke}} [[ko:мҮјлӢҲ м“°л„ӨмҠӨмјҖ]] {{wikidata link|Q11464740}} [[Category:1229 births]] [[Category:1292 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Gokenin of the Kamakura shogunate]] [[Category:People of the Mongol invasions of Japan]] [[Category:ShЕҚni clan]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]] [[Category:pages]]

are debated. }} '''ShЕҚni Tsunesuke''' (е°‘ејҗ зөҢиіҮ) was a {{ill|samurai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/samurai|qq=}} and retainer of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura shogunate|qq=}} during the mid-{{ill|Kamakura period|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura period|qq=}}. He was the son of {{ill|ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮиғҪ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|1=ShЕҚni Sukenaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464746}} and the third head of the {{ill|ShЕҚni clan|en|ShЕҚni clan|WD=Q2975368|lt=ShЕҚni clan|1=ShЕҚni clan}}. == Overview == The character "зөҢ" in Tsunesuke's name was likely granted as a favor by the fourth {{ill|Shikken|en|Shikken|WD=Q1045904|lt=Shikken|1=Shikken}}, {{ill|HЕҚjЕҚ Tsunetoki|en|HЕҚjЕҚ Tsunetoki|WD=Q2008076|lt=HЕҚjЕҚ Tokiyori|1=HЕҚjЕҚ Tsunetoki}}, or by {{ill|KujЕҚ Yoritsune|en|KujЕҚ Yoritsune|WD=Q471780|lt=Fujiwara no Yoritsune|1=KujЕҚ Yoritsune}}, the fourth {{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|WD=Q131767|lt=Seii Taishogun|1=Shogun}}, who gave the same character to Tokiyori. This suggests Tsunesuke underwent his coming-of-age ceremony (genpuku) between 1245 and 1246. There are varying theories regarding Tsunesuke's birth and death years. One theory suggests he was born in 1229 and died in 1292, while another suggests 1226 to 1289.[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B0%91%E5%BC%90%E7%B5%8C%E8%B3%87-1082512 гӮігғҲгғҗгғігӮҜ_е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ] However, as genpuku typically occurred in oneвҖҷs teens, the 1229 birth year is widely considered accurate. Tsunesuke shared responsibilities with his father in governing northern Kyushu and played a crucial role during the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|en|Mongol invasions of Japan|WD=Q208156|lt=Mongol invasions of Japan|1=Mongol invasions of Japan}}. He oversaw the fortification of defenses, such as the {{ill|GenkЕҚ BЕҚrui|en|GenkЕҚ BЕҚrui|WD=Q2154769|lt=Ishizumi walls|1=GenkЕҚ BЕҚrui}}, and coordinated efforts against the Yuan forces. In 1281, during the {{ill|Battle of KЕҚan|en|Battle of KЕҚan|WD=Q2237622|lt=Battle of KЕҚan|1=Battle of KЕҚan}}, Tsunesuke fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki|ja|еЈұеІҗеі¶гҒ®жҲҰгҒ„|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Iki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, where he expelled the Yuan forces but lost his son, {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Toshizane|1=ShЕҚni Suketoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464745}}.
[[File:Mooko-SamuraiShips.jpg|300px|right|thumb|'''ShЕҚni Tsunesuke''' and other samurai ships during the Mongol invasion.
{{small|гҖҺ{{ill|MЕҚko ShЕ«rai Ekotoba|en|MЕҚko ShЕ«rai Ekotoba|WD=Q3087790|lt=MЕҚko ShЕ«rai Ekotoba|1=MЕҚko ShЕ«rai Ekotoba}}гҖҸеҫҢе·»гғ»зөө15гғ»з¬¬19зҙҷ}} Despite this tragedy, he continued to serve as a {{ill|Chinzei BugyЕҚ|en|Chinzei BugyЕҚ|WD=Q440978|lt=Chinzei BugyЕҚ|1=Chinzei BugyЕҚ}} with {{ill|ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|ja|еӨ§еҸӢй јжі°|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|1=ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433274}}. In 1284, following the death of the eighth {{ill|Shikken|en|Shikken|WD=Q1045904|lt=Shikken|1=Shikken}}, {{ill|HЕҚjЕҚ Tokimune|en|HЕҚjЕҚ Tokimune|WD=Q430964|lt=HЕҚjЕҚ Tokimune|1=HЕҚjЕҚ Tokimune}}, Tsunesuke took Buddhist vows and adopted the Dharma name JЕҚe. During the {{ill|Shimozuki Disturbance|ja|йңңжңҲйЁ’еӢ•|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimozuki Disturbance|1=Shimotsuki incident|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10565914}} of 1285, he supported {{ill|Taira no Yoritsuna|ja|е№ій јз¶ұ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Yoritsuna|1=Taira no Yoritsuna|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483787}} and defeated his brother {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Kagesue|1=ShЕҚni Kagesuke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10263912}}, aligning with the ruling {{ill|Tokuso family|ja|еҫ—е®—е®¶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokuso family|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to еҫ—е®—|1=TokusЕҚ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1759237}}. This led to a weakening of the ShЕҚni clanвҖҷs influence, as they lost control of several provinces. In 1286, Tsunesuke was appointed to the {{ill|Chinzei Dangi-sho|ja|йҺ®иҘҝи«Үиӯ°жүҖ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinzei Dangi-sho|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to йҺ®иҘҝжҺўйЎҢ|1=Chinzei tandai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8191592}} alongside {{ill|ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|ja|еӨ§еҸӢй јжі°|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|1=ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433274}}, {{ill|Utsunomiya Michifusa|ja|е®ҮйғҪе®®йҖҡжҲҝ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Michifusa|1=Q119380431|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119380431}}, and {{ill|Shibuya Shigekuni|ja|жёӢи°·йҮҚйғ·|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Shigekuni|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. He passed away in 1292. His eldest son {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Toshizane|1=ShЕҚni Suketoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464745}} having died in battle, Tsunesuke was succeeded by {{ill|ShЕҚni Moritsune|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣзөҢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Moritsune|1=ShЕҚni Moritsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q93783030}}. His younger sons {{ill|ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷӮзөҢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|ShЕҚni Morishige|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣж°Ҹ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Morishige|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} later founded the {{ill|Shiga clan|ja|еҝ—иіҖж°Ҹ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiga clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Hirai clan|en|Hirai clan|WD=Q29526001|lt=Hirai clan|1=Hirai clan}}, respectively. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *[[Motohisa Yasuda]], ed. гҖҺйҺҢеҖүгғ»е®Өз”әдәәеҗҚдәӢе…ё гӮігғігғ‘гӮҜгғҲзүҲгҖҸпјҲ[[Shin Jinbutsu Oraisha]], 1990пјү *[[Hattori Hideo]] гҖҺи’ҷеҸӨиҘІжқҘгҖҸпјҲ[[Yamakawa Publishing]], 2014пјү *[[Kuroita Katsumi]], ed. гҖҺж–°иЁӮеў—иЈңеӣҪеҸІеӨ§зі»гғ»е°ҠеҚ‘еҲҶи„ү з¬¬2зҜҮгҖҸпјҲ[[Yoshikawa KЕҚbunkan]]пјү {{ShЕҚni clan heads}} {{translated page|ja|е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ|version=101501413|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ]] [[ko:%EC%87%BC%EB%8B%88 %EC%93%B0%EB%84%A4%EC%8A%A4%EC%BC%80]] {{Commons category|ShЕҚni Tsunesuke}} [[ko:мҮјлӢҲ м“°л„ӨмҠӨмјҖ]] {{wikidata link|Q11464740}} [[Category:1229 births]] [[Category:1292 deaths]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Gokenin of the Kamakura shogunate]] [[Category:People of the Mongol invasions of Japan]] [[Category:ShЕҚni clan]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]] [[Category:pages]]

are debated. }} '''ShЕҚni Tsunesuke''' (е°‘ејҗ зөҢиіҮ) was a {{ill|samurai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/samurai|qq=}} and retainer of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura shogunate|qq=}} during the mid-{{ill|Kamakura period|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura period|qq=}}. He was the son of {{ill|ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮиғҪ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|1=ShЕҚni Sukenaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464746}} and the third head of the {{ill|ShЕҚni clan|en|ShЕҚni clan|WD=Q2975368|lt=ShЕҚni clan|1=ShЕҚni clan}}. == Overview == The character "зөҢ" in Tsunesuke's name was likely granted as a favor by the fourth {{ill|Shikken|en|Shikken|WD=Q1045904|lt=Shikken|1=Shikken}}, {{ill|HЕҚjЕҚ Tsunetoki|en|HЕҚjЕҚ Tsunetoki|WD=Q2008076|lt=HЕҚjЕҚ Tokiyori|1=HЕҚjЕҚ Tsunetoki}}, or by {{ill|KujЕҚ Yoritsune|en|KujЕҚ Yoritsune|WD=Q471780|lt=Fujiwara no Yoritsune|1=KujЕҚ Yoritsune}}, the fourth {{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|WD=Q131767|lt=Seii Taishogun|1=Shogun}}, who gave the same character to Tokiyori. This suggests Tsunesuke underwent his coming-of-age ceremony (genpuku) between 1245 and 1246. There are varying theories regarding Tsunesuke's birth and death years. One theory suggests he was born in 1229 and died in 1292, while another suggests 1226 to 1289.[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B0%91%E5%BC%90%E7%B5%8C%E8%B3%87-1082512 гӮігғҲгғҗгғігӮҜ_е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ] However, as genpuku typically occurred in oneвҖҷs teens, the 1229 birth year is widely considered accurate. Tsunesuke shared responsibilities with his father in governing northern Kyushu and played a crucial role during the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|en|Mongol invasions of Japan|WD=Q208156|lt=Mongol invasions of Japan|1=Mongol invasions of Japan}}. He oversaw the fortification of defenses, such as the {{ill|GenkЕҚ BЕҚrui|en|GenkЕҚ BЕҚrui|WD=Q2154769|lt=Ishizumi walls|1=GenkЕҚ BЕҚrui}}, and coordinated efforts against the Yuan forces. His earliest documented activity appears in a record from 1269.жңҚйғЁгҖҒ2014th year гҖҒP.416гҖӮе…ёжӢ гҒҜгҖҺжӯҰйӣ„зҘһзӨҫж–ҮжӣёгҖҸпјҲгҖҺйҺҢеҖүйҒәж–ҮгҖҸз¬¬14е·»гғ»10503еҸ·ж–ҮжӣёпјүгҖӮ In 1274, during the {{ill|Battle of Bun'ei|en|Battle of Bun'ei|WD=Q2237386|lt=Battle of Bun'ei|1=Battle of Bun'ei}}, Tsunesuke's brother {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Kagesue|1=ShЕҚni Kagesuke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10263912}} led the Japanese forces. Tsunesuke's role during this campaign is unclear. By 1275, he inherited the familyвҖҷs headship from his father. In 1281, during the {{ill|Battle of KЕҚan|en|Battle of KЕҚan|WD=Q2237622|lt=Battle of KЕҚan|1=Battle of KЕҚan}}, Tsunesuke fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki|ja|еЈұеІҗеі¶гҒ®жҲҰгҒ„|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Iki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, where he expelled the Yuan forces but lost his son, {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Toshizane|1=ShЕҚni Suketoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464745}}.
[[File:Mooko-SamuraiShips.jpg|300px|right|thumb|'''ShЕҚni Tsunesuke''' and other samurai ships during the Mongol invasion.

In 1284, following the death of the eighth {{ill|Shikken|en|Shikken|WD=Q1045904|lt=Shikken|1=Shikken}}, {{ill|HЕҚjЕҚ Tokimune|en|HЕҚjЕҚ Tokimune|WD=Q430964|lt=HЕҚjЕҚ Tokimune|1=HЕҚjЕҚ Tokimune}}, Tsunesuke took Buddhist vows and adopted the Dharma name JЕҚe. This led to a weakening of the ShЕҚni clanвҖҷs influence, as they lost control of several provinces. In 1286, Tsunesuke was appointed to the {{ill|Chinzei Dangi-sho|ja|йҺ®иҘҝи«Үиӯ°жүҖ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinzei Dangi-sho|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to йҺ®иҘҝжҺўйЎҢ|1=Chinzei tandai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8191592}} alongside {{ill|ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|ja|еӨ§еҸӢй јжі°|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|1=ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433274}}, {{ill|Utsunomiya Michifusa|ja|е®ҮйғҪе®®йҖҡжҲҝ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Michifusa|1=Q119380431|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119380431}}, and {{ill|Shibuya Shigekuni|ja|жёӢи°·йҮҚйғ·|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Shigekuni|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. He passed away in 1292. His eldest son {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Toshizane|1=ShЕҚni Suketoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464745}} having died in battle, Tsunesuke was succeeded by {{ill|ShЕҚni Moritsune|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣзөҢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Moritsune|1=ShЕҚni Moritsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q93783030}}. His younger sons {{ill|ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷӮзөҢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|ShЕҚni Morishige|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣж°Ҹ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Morishige|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} later founded the {{ill|Shiga clan|ja|еҝ—иіҖж°Ҹ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiga clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Hirai clan|en|Hirai clan|WD=Q29526001|lt=Hirai clan|1=Hirai clan}}, respectively. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *[[Motohisa Yasuda]], ed. гҖҺйҺҢеҖүгғ»е®Өз”әдәәеҗҚдәӢе…ё гӮігғігғ‘гӮҜгғҲзүҲгҖҸпјҲ[[Shin Jinbutsu Oraisha]], 1990пјү *[[Hattori Hideo]] гҖҺи’ҷеҸӨиҘІжқҘгҖҸпјҲ[[Yamakawa Publishing]], 2014пјү *[[Kuroita Katsumi]], ed. гҖҺж–°иЁӮеў—иЈңеӣҪеҸІеӨ§зі»гғ»е°ҠеҚ‘еҲҶи„ү з¬¬2зҜҮгҖҸпјҲ[[Yoshikawa KЕҚbunkan]]пјү {{ShЕҚni clan heads}} {{translated page|ja|е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ|version=101501413|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ]] [[ko:%EC%87%BC%EB%8B%88 %EC%93%B0%EB%84%A4%EC%8A%A4%EC%BC%80]] {{Commons category|ShЕҚni Tsunesuke}} [[ko:мҮјлӢҲ м“°л„ӨмҠӨмјҖ]] {{wikidata link|Q11464740}} [[Category:1229 births]] [[Category:1292 deaths]] [[Category:Gokenin of the Kamakura shogunate]] [[Category:People of the Mongol invasions of Japan]] [[Category:ShЕҚni clan]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]] [[Category:pages]]

year 6жңҲгҖҒ26-34й ҒгҖӮ | spouse = | children = {{ill|ShЕҚni Moritsune|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣзөҢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Moritsune|1=ShЕҚni Moritsune|comment=changed title based on are debated. }} '''ShЕҚni Tsunesuke''' (е°‘ејҗ зөҢиіҮ) was a {{ill|samurai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/samurai|qq=}} and retainer of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura shogunate|qq=}} during the mid-{{ill|Kamakura period|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura period|qq=}}. He was the son of {{ill|ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮиғҪ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|1=ShЕҚni Sukenaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464746}} and the third head of the {{ill|ShЕҚni clan|en|ShЕҚni clan|WD=Q2975368|lt=ShЕҚni clan|1=ShЕҚni clan}}. == Overview == The character "зөҢ" in Tsunesuke's name was likely granted as a favor by the fourth {{ill|Shikken|en|Shikken|WD=Q1045904|lt=Shikken|1=Shikken}}, {{ill|HЕҚjЕҚ Tsunetoki|en|HЕҚjЕҚ Tsunetoki|WD=Q2008076|lt=HЕҚjЕҚ Tokiyori|1=HЕҚjЕҚ Tsunetoki}}, or by {{ill|KujЕҚ Yoritsune|en|KujЕҚ Yoritsune|WD=Q471780|lt=Fujiwara no Yoritsune|1=KujЕҚ Yoritsune}}, the fourth {{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|WD=Q131767|lt=Seii Taishogun|1=Shogun}}, who gave the same character to Tokiyori. This suggests Tsunesuke underwent his coming-of-age ceremony (genpuku) between 1245 and 1246. There are varying theories regarding Tsunesuke's birth and death years. One theory suggests he was born in 1229 and died in 1292, while another suggests 1226 to 1289.[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B0%91%E5%BC%90%E7%B5%8C%E8%B3%87-1082512 гӮігғҲгғҗгғігӮҜ_е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ] However, as genpuku typically occurred in oneвҖҷs teens, the 1229 birth year is widely considered accurate. Tsunesuke shared responsibilities with his father in governing northern Kyushu and played a crucial role during the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|en|Mongol invasions of Japan|WD=Q208156|lt=Mongol invasions of Japan|1=Mongol invasions of Japan}}. He oversaw the fortification of defenses, such as the {{ill|GenkЕҚ BЕҚrui|en|GenkЕҚ BЕҚrui|WD=Q2154769|lt=Ishizumi walls|1=GenkЕҚ BЕҚrui}}, and coordinated efforts against the Yuan forces. His earliest documented activity appears in a record from 1269.жңҚйғЁгҖҒ2014th year гҖҒP.416гҖӮе…ёжӢ гҒҜгҖҺжӯҰйӣ„зҘһзӨҫж–ҮжӣёгҖҸпјҲгҖҺйҺҢеҖүйҒәж–ҮгҖҸз¬¬14е·»гғ»10503еҸ·ж–ҮжӣёпјүгҖӮ In 1274, during the {{ill|Battle of Bun'ei|en|Battle of Bun'ei|WD=Q2237386|lt=Battle of Bun'ei|1=Battle of Bun'ei}}, Tsunesuke's brother {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Kagesue|1=ShЕҚni Kagesuke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10263912}} led the Japanese forces. Tsunesuke's role during this campaign is unclear. By 1275, he inherited the familyвҖҷs headship from his father. In 1281, during the {{ill|Battle of KЕҚan|en|Battle of KЕҚan|WD=Q2237622|lt=Battle of KЕҚan|1=Battle of KЕҚan}}, Tsunesuke fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki|ja|еЈұеІҗеі¶гҒ®жҲҰгҒ„|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Iki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, where he expelled the Yuan forces but lost his son, {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Toshizane|1=ShЕҚni Suketoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464745}}.
[[File:Mooko-SamuraiShips.jpg|300px|right|thumb|'''ShЕҚni Tsunesuke''' and other samurai ships during the Mongol invasion.
{{small|гҖҺ{{ill|MЕҚko ShЕ«rai Ekotoba|en|MЕҚko ShЕ«rai Ekotoba|WD=Q3087790|lt=MЕҚko ShЕ«rai Ekotoba|1=MЕҚko ShЕ«rai Ekotoba}}гҖҸеҫҢе·»гғ»зөө15гғ»з¬¬19зҙҷ}} Despite this tragedy, he continued to serve as a {{ill|Chinzei BugyЕҚ|en|Chinzei BugyЕҚ|WD=Q440978|lt=Chinzei BugyЕҚ|1=Chinzei BugyЕҚ}} with {{ill|ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|ja|еӨ§еҸӢй јжі°|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|1=ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433274}}. In 1284, following the death of the eighth {{ill|Shikken|en|Shikken|WD=Q1045904|lt=Shikken|1=Shikken}}, {{ill|HЕҚjЕҚ Tokimune|en|HЕҚjЕҚ Tokimune|WD=Q430964|lt=HЕҚjЕҚ Tokimune|1=HЕҚjЕҚ Tokimune}}, Tsunesuke took Buddhist vows and adopted the Dharma name JЕҚe. During the {{ill|Shimozuki Disturbance|ja|йңңжңҲйЁ’еӢ•|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimozuki Disturbance|1=Shimotsuki incident|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10565914}} of 1285, he supported {{ill|Taira no Yoritsuna|ja|е№ій јз¶ұ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Yoritsuna|1=Taira no Yoritsuna|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483787}} and defeated his brother {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Kagesue|1=ShЕҚni Kagesuke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10263912}}, aligning with the ruling {{ill|Tokuso family|ja|еҫ—е®—е®¶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokuso family|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to еҫ—е®—|1=TokusЕҚ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1759237}}. This led to a weakening of the ShЕҚni clanвҖҷs influence, as they lost control of several provinces. In 1286, Tsunesuke was appointed to the {{ill|Chinzei Dangi-sho|ja|йҺ®иҘҝи«Үиӯ°жүҖ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinzei Dangi-sho|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to йҺ®иҘҝжҺўйЎҢ|1=Chinzei tandai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8191592}} alongside {{ill|ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|ja|еӨ§еҸӢй јжі°|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|1=ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433274}}, {{ill|Utsunomiya Michifusa|ja|е®ҮйғҪе®®йҖҡжҲҝ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Michifusa|1=Q119380431|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119380431}}, and {{ill|Shibuya Shigekuni|ja|жёӢи°·йҮҚйғ·|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Shigekuni|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. He passed away in 1292. His eldest son {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Toshizane|1=ShЕҚni Suketoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464745}} having died in battle, Tsunesuke was succeeded by {{ill|ShЕҚni Moritsune|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣзөҢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Moritsune|1=ShЕҚni Moritsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q93783030}}. His younger sons {{ill|ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷӮзөҢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|ShЕҚni Morishige|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣж°Ҹ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Morishige|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} later founded the {{ill|Shiga clan|ja|еҝ—иіҖж°Ҹ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiga clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Hirai clan|en|Hirai clan|WD=Q29526001|lt=Hirai clan|1=Hirai clan}}, respectively. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *[[Motohisa Yasuda]], ed. гҖҺйҺҢеҖүгғ»е®Өз”әдәәеҗҚдәӢе…ё гӮігғігғ‘гӮҜгғҲзүҲгҖҸпјҲ[[Shin Jinbutsu Oraisha]], 1990пјү *[[Hattori Hideo]] гҖҺи’ҷеҸӨиҘІжқҘгҖҸпјҲ[[Yamakawa Publishing]], 2014пјү *[[Kuroita Katsumi]], ed. гҖҺж–°иЁӮеў—иЈңеӣҪеҸІеӨ§зі»гғ»е°ҠеҚ‘еҲҶи„ү з¬¬2зҜҮгҖҸпјҲ[[Yoshikawa KЕҚbunkan]]пјү {{ShЕҚni clan heads}} {{translated page|ja|е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ|version=101501413|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ]] [[ko:%EC%87%BC%EB%8B%88 %EC%93%B0%EB%84%A4%EC%8A%A4%EC%BC%80]] {{Commons category|ShЕҚni Tsunesuke}} [[ko:мҮјлӢҲ м“°л„ӨмҠӨмјҖ]] {{wikidata link|Q11464740}} [[Category:1229 births]] [[Category:1292 deaths]] [[Category:Gokenin of the Kamakura shogunate]] [[Category:People of the Mongol invasions of Japan]] [[Category:ShЕҚni clan]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]]

are debated. }} '''ShЕҚni Tsunesuke''' (е°‘ејҗ зөҢиіҮ) was a {{ill|samurai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/samurai|qq=}} and retainer of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura shogunate|qq=}} during the mid-{{ill|Kamakura period|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura period|qq=}}. He was the son of {{ill|ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮиғҪ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|1=ShЕҚni Sukenaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464746}} and the third head of the {{ill|ShЕҚni clan|en|ShЕҚni clan|WD=Q2975368|lt=ShЕҚni clan|1=ShЕҚni clan}}. == Overview == The character "зөҢ" in Tsunesuke's name was likely granted as a favor by the fourth {{ill|Shikken|en|Shikken|WD=Q1045904|lt=Shikken|1=Shikken}}, {{ill|HЕҚjЕҚ Tsunetoki|en|HЕҚjЕҚ Tsunetoki|WD=Q2008076|lt=HЕҚjЕҚ Tokiyori|1=HЕҚjЕҚ Tsunetoki}}, or by {{ill|KujЕҚ Yoritsune|en|KujЕҚ Yoritsune|WD=Q471780|lt=Fujiwara no Yoritsune|1=KujЕҚ Yoritsune}}, the fourth {{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|WD=Q131767|lt=Seii Taishogun|1=Shogun}}, who gave the same character to Tokiyori. This suggests Tsunesuke underwent his coming-of-age ceremony (genpuku) between 1245 and 1246. There are varying theories regarding Tsunesuke's birth and death years. One theory suggests he was born in 1229 and died in 1292, while another suggests 1226 to 1289.[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B0%91%E5%BC%90%E7%B5%8C%E8%B3%87-1082512 гӮігғҲгғҗгғігӮҜ_е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ] However, as genpuku typically occurred in oneвҖҷs teens, the 1229 birth year is widely considered accurate. Tsunesuke shared responsibilities with his father in governing northern Kyushu and played a crucial role during the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|en|Mongol invasions of Japan|WD=Q208156|lt=Mongol invasions of Japan|1=Mongol invasions of Japan}}. He oversaw the fortification of defenses, such as the {{ill|GenkЕҚ BЕҚrui|en|GenkЕҚ BЕҚrui|WD=Q2154769|lt=Ishizumi walls|1=GenkЕҚ BЕҚrui}}, and coordinated efforts against the Yuan forces. His earliest documented activity appears in a record from 1269.жңҚйғЁгҖҒ2014th year гҖҒP.416гҖӮе…ёжӢ гҒҜгҖҺжӯҰйӣ„зҘһзӨҫж–ҮжӣёгҖҸпјҲгҖҺйҺҢеҖүйҒәж–ҮгҖҸз¬¬14е·»гғ»10503еҸ·ж–ҮжӣёпјүгҖӮ In 1274, during the {{ill|Battle of Bun'ei|en|Battle of Bun'ei|WD=Q2237386|lt=Battle of Bun'ei|1=Battle of Bun'ei}}, Tsunesuke's brother {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Kagesue|1=ShЕҚni Kagesuke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10263912}} led the Japanese forces. Tsunesuke's role during this campaign is unclear. By 1275, he inherited the familyвҖҷs headship from his father. In 1281, during the {{ill|Battle of KЕҚan|en|Battle of KЕҚan|WD=Q2237622|lt=Battle of KЕҚan|1=Battle of KЕҚan}}, Tsunesuke fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki|ja|еЈұеІҗеі¶гҒ®жҲҰгҒ„|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Iki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, where he expelled the Yuan forces but lost his son, {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Toshizane|1=ShЕҚni Suketoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464745}}.
[[File:Mooko-SamuraiShips.jpg|300px|right|thumb|'''ShЕҚni Tsunesuke''' and other samurai ships during the Mongol invasion.
{{small|гҖҺ{{ill|MЕҚko ShЕ«rai Ekotoba|en|MЕҚko ShЕ«rai Ekotoba|WD=Q3087790|lt=MЕҚko ShЕ«rai Ekotoba|1=MЕҚko ShЕ«rai Ekotoba}}гҖҸеҫҢе·»гғ»зөө15гғ»з¬¬19зҙҷ}} Despite this tragedy, he continued to serve as a {{ill|Chinzei BugyЕҚ|en|Chinzei BugyЕҚ|WD=Q440978|lt=Chinzei BugyЕҚ|1=Chinzei BugyЕҚ}} with {{ill|ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|ja|еӨ§еҸӢй јжі°|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|1=ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433274}}. In 1284, following the death of the eighth {{ill|Shikken|en|Shikken|WD=Q1045904|lt=Shikken|1=Shikken}}, {{ill|HЕҚjЕҚ Tokimune|en|HЕҚjЕҚ Tokimune|WD=Q430964|lt=HЕҚjЕҚ Tokimune|1=HЕҚjЕҚ Tokimune}}, Tsunesuke took Buddhist vows and adopted the Dharma name JЕҚe. During the {{ill|Shimozuki Disturbance|ja|йңңжңҲйЁ’еӢ•|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimozuki Disturbance|1=Shimotsuki incident|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10565914}} of 1285, he supported {{ill|Taira no Yoritsuna|ja|е№ій јз¶ұ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Yoritsuna|1=Taira no Yoritsuna|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483787}} and defeated his brother {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Kagesue|1=ShЕҚni Kagesuke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10263912}}, aligning with the ruling {{ill|Tokuso family|ja|еҫ—е®—е®¶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokuso family|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to еҫ—е®—|1=TokusЕҚ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1759237}}. This led to a weakening of the ShЕҚni clanвҖҷs influence, as they lost control of several provinces. In 1286, Tsunesuke was appointed to the {{ill|Chinzei Dangi-sho|ja|йҺ®иҘҝи«Үиӯ°жүҖ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinzei Dangi-sho|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to йҺ®иҘҝжҺўйЎҢ|1=Chinzei tandai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8191592}} alongside {{ill|ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|ja|еӨ§еҸӢй јжі°|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|1=ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433274}}, {{ill|Utsunomiya Michifusa|ja|е®ҮйғҪе®®йҖҡжҲҝ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Michifusa|1=Q119380431|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119380431}}, and {{ill|Shibuya Shigekuni|ja|жёӢи°·йҮҚйғ·|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Shigekuni|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. He passed away in 1292. His eldest son {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Toshizane|1=ShЕҚni Suketoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464745}} having died in battle, Tsunesuke was succeeded by {{ill|ShЕҚni Moritsune|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣзөҢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Moritsune|1=ShЕҚni Moritsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q93783030}}. His younger sons {{ill|ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷӮзөҢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|ShЕҚni Morishige|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣж°Ҹ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Morishige|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} later founded the {{ill|Shiga clan|ja|еҝ—иіҖж°Ҹ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiga clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Hirai clan|en|Hirai clan|WD=Q29526001|lt=Hirai clan|1=Hirai clan}}, respectively. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *[[Motohisa Yasuda]], ed. гҖҺйҺҢеҖүгғ»е®Өз”әдәәеҗҚдәӢе…ё гӮігғігғ‘гӮҜгғҲзүҲгҖҸпјҲ[[Shin Jinbutsu Oraisha]], 1990пјү *[[Hattori Hideo]] гҖҺи’ҷеҸӨиҘІжқҘгҖҸпјҲ[[Yamakawa Publishing]], 2014пјү *[[Kuroita Katsumi]], ed. гҖҺж–°иЁӮеў—иЈңеӣҪеҸІеӨ§зі»гғ»е°ҠеҚ‘еҲҶи„ү з¬¬2зҜҮгҖҸпјҲ[[Yoshikawa KЕҚbunkan]]пјү {{ShЕҚni clan heads}} {{translated page|ja|е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ|version=101501413|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ]] [[ko:%EC%87%BC%EB%8B%88 %EC%93%B0%EB%84%A4%EC%8A%A4%EC%BC%80]] {{Commons category|ShЕҚni Tsunesuke}} [[ko:мҮјлӢҲ м“°л„ӨмҠӨмјҖ]] {{wikidata link|Q11464740}} [[Category:1229 births]] [[Category:1292 deaths]] [[Category:Gokenin of the Kamakura shogunate]] [[Category:People of the Mongol invasions of Japan]] [[Category:ShЕҚni clan]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

year 6жңҲгҖҒ26-34й ҒгҖӮ | spouse = | children = {{ill|ShЕҚni Moritsune|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣзөҢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Moritsune|1=ShЕҚni Moritsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q93783030}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Toshizane|1=ShЕҚni Suketoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464745}}, {{ill|ShЕҚni are debated. }} '''ShЕҚni Tsunesuke''' (е°‘ејҗ зөҢиіҮ) was a {{ill|samurai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/samurai|qq=}} and retainer of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura shogunate|qq=}} during the mid-{{ill|Kamakura period|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura period|qq=}}. He was the son of {{ill|ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮиғҪ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Sukeyoshi|1=ShЕҚni Sukenaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464746}} and the third head of the {{ill|ShЕҚni clan|en|ShЕҚni clan|WD=Q2975368|lt=ShЕҚni clan|1=ShЕҚni clan}}. == Overview == The character "зөҢ" in Tsunesuke's name was likely granted as a favor by the fourth {{ill|Shikken|en|Shikken|WD=Q1045904|lt=Shikken|1=Shikken}}, {{ill|HЕҚjЕҚ Tsunetoki|en|HЕҚjЕҚ Tsunetoki|WD=Q2008076|lt=HЕҚjЕҚ Tokiyori|1=HЕҚjЕҚ Tsunetoki}}, or by {{ill|KujЕҚ Yoritsune|en|KujЕҚ Yoritsune|WD=Q471780|lt=Fujiwara no Yoritsune|1=KujЕҚ Yoritsune}}, the fourth {{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|WD=Q131767|lt=Seii Taishogun|1=Shogun}}, who gave the same character to Tokiyori. This suggests Tsunesuke underwent his coming-of-age ceremony (genpuku) between 1245 and 1246. There are varying theories regarding Tsunesuke's birth and death years. One theory suggests he was born in 1229 and died in 1292, while another suggests 1226 to 1289.[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B0%91%E5%BC%90%E7%B5%8C%E8%B3%87-1082512 гӮігғҲгғҗгғігӮҜ_е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ] However, as genpuku typically occurred in oneвҖҷs teens, the 1229 birth year is widely considered accurate. Tsunesuke shared responsibilities with his father in governing northern Kyushu and played a crucial role during the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|en|Mongol invasions of Japan|WD=Q208156|lt=Mongol invasions of Japan|1=Mongol invasions of Japan}}. He oversaw the fortification of defenses, such as the {{ill|GenkЕҚ BЕҚrui|en|GenkЕҚ BЕҚrui|WD=Q2154769|lt=Ishizumi walls|1=GenkЕҚ BЕҚrui}}, and coordinated efforts against the Yuan forces. His earliest documented activity appears in a record from 1269.жңҚйғЁгҖҒ2014th year гҖҒP.416гҖӮе…ёжӢ гҒҜгҖҺжӯҰйӣ„зҘһзӨҫж–ҮжӣёгҖҸпјҲгҖҺйҺҢеҖүйҒәж–ҮгҖҸз¬¬14е·»гғ»10503еҸ·ж–ҮжӣёпјүгҖӮ In 1274, during the {{ill|Battle of Bun'ei|en|Battle of Bun'ei|WD=Q2237386|lt=Battle of Bun'ei|1=Battle of Bun'ei}}, Tsunesuke's brother {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Kagesue|1=ShЕҚni Kagesuke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10263912}} led the Japanese forces. Tsunesuke's role during this campaign is unclear. By 1275, he inherited the familyвҖҷs headship from his father. In 1281, during the {{ill|Battle of KЕҚan|en|Battle of KЕҚan|WD=Q2237622|lt=Battle of KЕҚan|1=Battle of KЕҚan}}, Tsunesuke fought valiantly in the {{ill|Battle of Iki|ja|еЈұеІҗеі¶гҒ®жҲҰгҒ„|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Iki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, where he expelled the Yuan forces but lost his son, {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Toshizane|1=ShЕҚni Suketoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464745}}.
[[File:Mooko-SamuraiShips.jpg|300px|right|thumb|'''ShЕҚni Tsunesuke''' and other samurai ships during the Mongol invasion.
{{small|гҖҺ{{ill|MЕҚko ShЕ«rai Ekotoba|en|MЕҚko ShЕ«rai Ekotoba|WD=Q3087790|lt=MЕҚko ShЕ«rai Ekotoba|1=MЕҚko ShЕ«rai Ekotoba}}гҖҸеҫҢе·»гғ»зөө15гғ»з¬¬19зҙҷ}} Despite this tragedy, he continued to serve as a {{ill|Chinzei BugyЕҚ|en|Chinzei BugyЕҚ|WD=Q440978|lt=Chinzei BugyЕҚ|1=Chinzei BugyЕҚ}} with {{ill|ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|ja|еӨ§еҸӢй јжі°|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|1=ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433274}}. In 1284, following the death of the eighth {{ill|Shikken|en|Shikken|WD=Q1045904|lt=Shikken|1=Shikken}}, {{ill|HЕҚjЕҚ Tokimune|en|HЕҚjЕҚ Tokimune|WD=Q430964|lt=HЕҚjЕҚ Tokimune|1=HЕҚjЕҚ Tokimune}}, Tsunesuke took Buddhist vows and adopted the Dharma name JЕҚe. During the {{ill|Shimozuki Disturbance|ja|йңңжңҲйЁ’еӢ•|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimozuki Disturbance|1=Shimotsuki incident|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10565914}} of 1285, he supported {{ill|Taira no Yoritsuna|ja|е№ій јз¶ұ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Yoritsuna|1=Taira no Yoritsuna|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483787}} and defeated his brother {{ill|ShЕҚni Kagesue|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷҜиіҮ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Kagesue|1=ShЕҚni Kagesuke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10263912}}, aligning with the ruling {{ill|Tokuso family|ja|еҫ—е®—е®¶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokuso family|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to еҫ—е®—|1=TokusЕҚ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1759237}}. This led to a weakening of the ShЕҚni clanвҖҷs influence, as they lost control of several provinces. In 1286, Tsunesuke was appointed to the {{ill|Chinzei Dangi-sho|ja|йҺ®иҘҝи«Үиӯ°жүҖ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinzei Dangi-sho|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to йҺ®иҘҝжҺўйЎҢ|1=Chinzei tandai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8191592}} alongside {{ill|ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|ja|еӨ§еҸӢй јжі°|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|1=ЕҢtomo Yoriyasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433274}}, {{ill|Utsunomiya Michifusa|ja|е®ҮйғҪе®®йҖҡжҲҝ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Michifusa|1=Q119380431|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119380431}}, and {{ill|Shibuya Shigekuni|ja|жёӢи°·йҮҚйғ·|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Shigekuni|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. He passed away in 1292. His eldest son {{ill|ShЕҚni Toshizane|ja|е°‘ејҗиіҮжҷӮ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Toshizane|1=ShЕҚni Suketoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464745}} having died in battle, Tsunesuke was succeeded by {{ill|ShЕҚni Moritsune|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣзөҢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Moritsune|1=ShЕҚni Moritsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q93783030}}. His younger sons {{ill|ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja|е°‘ејҗжҷӮзөҢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Tokitsune|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|ShЕҚni Morishige|ja|е°‘ејҗзӣӣж°Ҹ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ShЕҚni Morishige|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} later founded the {{ill|Shiga clan|ja|еҝ—иіҖж°Ҹ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiga clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Hirai clan|en|Hirai clan|WD=Q29526001|lt=Hirai clan|1=Hirai clan}}, respectively. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == *[[Motohisa Yasuda]], ed. гҖҺйҺҢеҖүгғ»е®Өз”әдәәеҗҚдәӢе…ё гӮігғігғ‘гӮҜгғҲзүҲгҖҸпјҲ[[Shin Jinbutsu Oraisha]], 1990пјү *[[Hattori Hideo]] гҖҺи’ҷеҸӨиҘІжқҘгҖҸпјҲ[[Yamakawa Publishing]], 2014пјү *[[Kuroita Katsumi]], ed. гҖҺж–°иЁӮеў—иЈңеӣҪеҸІеӨ§зі»гғ»е°ҠеҚ‘еҲҶи„ү з¬¬2зҜҮгҖҸпјҲ[[Yoshikawa KЕҚbunkan]]пјү {{ShЕҚni clan heads}} {{translated page|ja|е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ|version=101501413|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:е°‘ејҗзөҢиіҮ]] [[ko:%EC%87%BC%EB%8B%88 %EC%93%B0%EB%84%A4%EC%8A%A4%EC%BC%80]] {{Commons category|ShЕҚni Tsunesuke}} [[ko:мҮјлӢҲ м“°л„ӨмҠӨмјҖ]] {{wikidata link|Q11464740}} [[Category:1229 births]] [[Category:1292 deaths]] [[Category:Gokenin of the Kamakura shogunate]] [[Category:People of the Mongol invasions of Japan]] [[Category:ShЕҚni clan]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
